To help make Wellspring the best it can be, the Staff team requires the support of the community in running events. Support can come in many varieties of varying commitment - from joining our full-time Staff Team, to helping at game while being a player. 


Please apply for any role by opening a ticket in our Discord or emailing wellspringlarp@gmail.com.


Staff Team:
Our staff team is full-time, meaning they do not play PCs and spend the event fully engaged in the event timeline and encounters/scenes. Internally Staff is divided into two types - the first is Plot Staff who write stories and design encounters and mods. The Second is Production Staff - who manage all of the logistic and material concerns of the game. All staff members full-time-NPC during the games themselves - they do not have PCs they play part time. This is a great opportunity to shape the game and be a core member in the development and execution of the story of Wellspring. The game is always recruiting staff members and this opportunity will include mentorship from our experienced staff team. Anyone who takes on this mantle will have the endless love, support, and admiration of the existing staff team too!


Currently, the staff team is looking to add two Plot staff (extra consideration for someone with puzzle/non-combat writing experience), and two Production staff. 


Player Helper Roles:
There are a number of roles that support the game, but do not require a player to be an NPC or Staff. Players can apply to these roles when we have openings. Who is given these roles will be decided by whoever is the best fit within a batch of applicants. In general we want to give people the opportunity to help if they can - but some roles are best handled by people with specific skills. In some cases we may have multiple people filling the same role. These roles award benefits for every event the player attends in this capacity, and each benefit can vary for each role. 


Player Representative - 50 Service Points per Event
The player representative is responsible for helping to mediate disputes between players when the players cannot come to an agreement, or to mediate conflict between players and staff if players are not comfortable bringing their concerns to staff directly. Additionally, the player representative is responsible for bringing player concerns to the staff team during events. If a player is uncomfortable with bringing issues up directly with the staff team between events, the Player Representative will be responsible for bringing concerns to the attention of staff between events as well.  


Player Emotional Safety Helper - 50 Service Points per Event
The Player Emotional Safety Helper is responsible for helping players who may be overwhelmed and may need additional support. They should be the first one to offer help in any way a player needs, and respond to aid players who are in need of support. For example, this could mean bringing an overwhelmed player to Serenity, asking if they need anything, and helping them decompress. This position is not responsible for bringing concerns to staff - just to support players when needed, but may require referring players to the Player Rep once the player is emotionally ready. 


Weapons and Armor Marshal - 50 Service Points per Event
The Weapons and Armor Marshal is responsible for checking player weapons before an event and ensuring that they meet safety and length requirements. They are additionally responsible for rating player armor and finalizing and checking off on armor level ratings. 


Logistics and Check-In - 50 Service Points per Event
Logistics and Check-In staff check players in to the event, ensure they understand and sign up for community shifts, give them required check-in items, and collect and record game fee. 


Material Logistics Helper - 50 Service Points per Event
The Material Logistics helper is responsible for handing out and exchanging gathering materials and physreps when requested by players. This role will involve regularly checking the "Player Inbox" nearby the tavern, filling the requests by accessing the stored physreps/tags and recording them in a staff ledger, and returning them to the "Player Outbox". Time commitment will vary based on total number of players signed up for this role, but any time spent in excess of 2 hours will award bonus Service Points at the "Extra Service Shifts" rate.


Meal Plan Coordinator - 150 Service Points per Event
The Meal Plan Coordinator is responsible for putting together a meal plan, approving it with the staff team, purchasing meal-plan materials, and preparing the meal plan during the event. The actual cost of materials will be covered by Wellspring. To ensure they have enough help, Meal Plan Helper is also a helper role.


Meal Plan Helper - 50 Service Points per Event
A Meal Plan Helper helps the Meal Plan Coordinator prepare and serve the meal plan during the event.


Event Medic - 50 Service Points per Event
The event medic will act as a first aid responder in the event of a physical injury during the game. They should have some kind of significant medical training - please specify when applying. 


Designated Driver - 25 Service Points per Event
This player is responsible for driving players off-site in the event that a player needs to leave for emergencies. This player should have a car of their own, and be comfortable driving it at night. Additional credits will be applied in the event that this job takes a significant amount of effort and time from the player. 


Production Assistant - 25 Service Points per between-event period. Additional SP awarded for work completed
Production Assistants will help with between-game production tasks. These tasks will be tailored to the skills of the individual production assistant. This could involve helping make props, build or repair weapons, make packets, designing music, etc! Applicants to the Production Assistant role should tell the staff team what production-related skills they have when applying. In all cases, production tasks that contain game spoilers will not be assigned to Production Assistants. Material costs will be covered by Wellspring, and time spent will award additional SP. 


Player Rules Marshal - 25 Service Points per Event 
A Player Rules Marshal helps other players understand rules and rules interactions in the event questions arise during an event. A player looking to apply to this position needs to be proficient in the entire Wellspring rules system, and be able to quickly interpret the written rulebook when they do not know the answer to a question.


Event Photographer - 50 Service Points per Event
The event photographer will be responsible for taking photos of larger set-piece moments of the event. Before an event, the photographer will be told when/where they would like to have photos taken, and may be asked to take photos at the start of large cornerstone mods. They will not be asked to take photos full time during the event, just during special moments to aid in marketing Wellspring. Staff may also request that the event photographer take pre-game portraits of consenting players. Reminder: Per our Code of Conduct, no photos may be posted publicly or used for marketing without permission of the portrayed participants.  


Special Projects - Variable Service Points per project
Staff may occasionally ask for assistance with specific projects that do not fall under the above categories. These will be described on an individual basis when applications are being requested, and SP awards will be set per-project.
________________


Base Classes (All)
Base Classes - Table of Contents

Classes - Introduction


Each time your character gains a level, that level will be put into a Class. Each Class below is divided into the following sections:
Starting Skills
If your first level is in this Class, you receive all of the listed Starting Skills for free. The listed numbers in parentheses after the name of each skill represents the number of BP (Build Points) the skill would normally cost - see “Free BP” section below.
Multiclass Skills
If you take the first level in any additional classes other than your starting class, you will instead receive these listed skills for free (or receive free BP if you already have them - see below).
Redundant Skills and Discounts - Free BP
Any time a character would gain a new skill or perk that they already have, they instead gain free BP equal to the cost of the skill or perk. In addition, any time a character would gain a discount on a skill or perk that they already have, they gain free BP equal to that discount (unless the source states otherwise).
Class Progression Table
This section lists the number of Powers/Spells the class receives at each level. Note that this level is specifically the number of levels in that class, not total character level.
Innate Powers
These Powers are received automatically for free at the listed Class level.
Class Skills
These are Skills that can be purchased at any time for the listed number of BP, as long as the character meets the listed requirements/prerequisites.
Power Tiers
Powers are divided into Tiers of strength. For martial classes, the tiers are Utility, Basic, Advanced, and Veteran. For spellcasting classes, the tiers are Cantrip, Novice, Adept, and Greater. Martial classes, when they gain levels, may choose any Powers from the list of the appropriate Tier, based on the number of slots given in the Progression Table. Spellcasting classes add one known Spell of any Tier each time they level, and gain additional Spell-slots for specific Tiers (and additional Cantrips) based on the Progression table.
Power Ranks
If a Skill or Power has a number listed in parentheses after its name, it can be taken multiple times, up to that number total.
Artisan: Base Class
Crafting & Support Martial Class


Description
It is difficult to simply define an Artisan. Certainly many are actual makers of art, such as painters and sculptors, while others are Artisans in a more “craftsperson” sense, building mechanical devices, exploring the dark corners of alchemy, or mastering the creation of magical objects. These examples only begin to encompass the kinds of arts available though. Chefs, chocolatiers, draftspersons, and masters of engineering all fall under the wide umbrella that are Artisans.


Mad scientists, dedicated scholars, and weird mystics, these characters are unified by their love of what they do and their ability to create. Some create knowledge, others exquisite artwork. Those who become Champion Artisans are true masters of their art and generally the best crafters in the world.
Starting Skills
               * A Good Defense - Basic Martial Weapons (1), Short Weapons (3)
               * Protective Equipment - Basic Armor (2)
               * Productive Equipment - Choose one Apprentice Crafting: [Alchemy, (Ritual Magic AND Ritual Lore), Enchanting, or Tinkering] (3)
               * The Land Provides - Choose a Gathering Skill: Forage I, Prospect I, or Scavenge I (3)
               * A Path Unfolds - Choose one of the below:
               * Apprentice & Journeyman Profession: [Your Choice] (3)
               * Apprentice Crafting: [Alchemy, (Ritual Magic AND Ritual Lore), Enchanting, or Tinkering] (3)
               * Note: This must be different from the choice made in Productive Equipment.
               * Basic Medicine (2) and one of [Bits & Pieces (1), Hearth (1), or Soothing Touch (1)]
Multiclass Skills
               * Materials, Everywhere: Basic Martial Weapons (1), Choose a Gathering Skill: [Forage I, Prospect I, Scavenge] (3)


Note: If the character selects an option and already has the maximum amount of any of the selected skills, the costs for those skills will be granted as free BP instead.

Becoming a Specialist
Many of the Basic and Advanced Powers an Artisan can choose as they gain proficiency in the ways of being an Artisan have one of three tags, indicated by a bracketed word. These tags are [Artificer], [Crafter] and [Mystic]. Whenever the Artisan has more Powers from one of these specializations than any other, they are a Specialist in that type of art. The type of Specialist an Artisan is may change from event to event as they gain and lose Artisan powers. This comes with bonuses defined below.


A character with more Artificer Powers is an “Artificer”. A character with more “Crafter” Powers is a Crafter. A character with more “Mystic” Powers is a Mystic. Throughout this class, Artisans will be referred to by these terms. A character who does not have more Powers chosen with one tag over others may choose their Specialization from the tied frontrunners. So if a Power says “If the Artisan is a Crafter, they may add an Accent”, this would only be available if the Artisan had more Crafter-tagged Powers than any other tag.


Specializations


Artificer
The combination of human and machine is the Artificer’s specialty. Artificers are more likely to be Tinkers than other crafting specialties and make extensive use of Forgesources. The Artificer is a master of repairing armor and shields in combat, going toe-to-toe with their enemies and coming out alive, and taking up space on the battlefield. If you want to play an Artisan that can participate in combat, this is the specialization for you! 
Crafter
When it comes to the productive makers, there is no equal to the Crafter. They are skilled craftsmen in their field and excel at creating items with more profound and useful benefits than their non-Crafter counterparts. Their toolkit includes many ways to alter the items that they have made, or enhance them to give situational mastery over niche encounters. 
Mystic
The Mystic is a ritualist who specializes in mixing mundane crafting with magical or divine energies. While any mage or cleric can pick up a scroll and cast it with an objective understanding of what the magic contained may do, it is the Mystic who goes beyond, to enhance themself and their allies farther than that which is written on the crumbling parchments of the ritual scroll. 
Artisan Innate Powers
Living Iron [Innate]
Requirement: Artisan Level 2
Incantation: “By the power of my craft I repair what is broken” or Focus Quick 30 while miming repairing armor
Call: “Mend X”
Target: Armor                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Immediate


The Artisan gains a Living Iron pool equal to 3 x Artisan’s Class Level. This pool resets every Long Rest. The Artisan may subtract from their Living Iron pool when the power is used to Mend armor (Physical, Summoned, or Natural) up to the Artisan’s class-level. 


Example: A 4th level Artisan has 12 total points of Mending per Long Rests, and can Mend up to 4 points per use of the Power.


Artificers gain additional points to their Living Iron pool based on the amounts below. Artisans that are Artificers gain bonuses based on how many tagged [Artificer] powers they have from all their classes.


               * 5+         [Artificer] Artisan Powers - Living Iron pool is 4 x Artisan’s Class levels
               * 7+         [Artificer] Artisan Powers - Living Iron pool is 5 x Artisan's Class levels
               * 10+         [Artificer] Artisan Powers - Living Iron pool is 6 x Artisan's Class levels
               * 15+         [Artificer] Artisan Powers - Living Iron pool is 7 x Artisan's Class levels
Brilliant Thinker [Innate]
Requirement: Artisan Level 3
The Artisan learns an additional Basic Power from the Artisan Class Powers.
The Learned One [Innate]
Requirement: Artisan Level 4
Skills and Options: The Artisan gains one of the following skills for free based on their Specialization when they attain level 4 Artisan (this one time skill bonus does not change if their Specialization later changes). The Artisan must have the prerequisites for this skill and if they do not then they may instead take the Apprentice or Basic level of the skill. 


               * Artificer- Basic Shields (4), Basic Traps (5), Medium Armor (4)
               * Crafter- Journeyman Alchemy (4), Journeyman Enchanting (4), Journeyman Tinkering (4)
               * Mystic- Advanced Medicine (4), [Journeyman Ritual Magic (2) AND Ritual Lore (2)]
Pragmatist [Innate]
Requirement: Artisan Level 5


The Artisan learns an additional Utility Power from the Artisan Class Powers.
Grand Ritualist [Innate]
Requirement: Artisan Level 7
Mystics gain the ability to modify the Dark Territory of any ritual they are a Participant or Ritualist of, increasing or decreasing it by up to 1 if they are a Participant or up to 2 if they are the Ritualist. Additionally, after completing a ritual successfully, the Mystic gains 5 points of Summoned Armor that last for a Short Rest.


Artisans that are of the Specialization Mystic may Grant each Participant of a ritual where they are the Primary Ritualist a number of Counters to Damage that do not interrupt the ritual when used. The Call for this Counter is “Counter, Grand Ritualist.” The Artisan may do this once per Long Rest. These Counters last until the next Short Rest, the end of the Ritual or until used whichever is first. The ritualist must explain these counters expire after the ritual has been completed.


               * 5+         [Mystic] Artisan Powers: Short Grant 1 Counter to Damage
               * 8+         [Mystic] Artisan powers: Short Grant 2 Counter to Damage
               * 11+         [Mystic] Artisan powers: Short Grant 3 Counter to Damage
               * 15+         [Mystic] Artisan Powers: Short Grant 4 Counter to Damage
Technical Expertise [Innate]
Requirement: Artisan Level 8
The Artisan learns an additional Advanced Power from the Artisan Class Powers.
Expert Craft [Innate]
Requirement: Artisan Level 9
Skills and Options: The Artisan gains a rank of the Fortunate Finder Character Option.
If the Artisan meets the following criteria:
               * Artisan is the Crafter Specialization
               * At least 7 [Crafter] tagged Powers (Basic, Advanced, Veteran, Utility)
               * Possesses the prerequisite Journeyman Skill


Then the Artisan also gains one of the following for free:
               * Greater Alchemy (5)
               * Greater Enchanting (5)
               * Greater Tinkering (5)
Opus [Innate]
Requirement: Artisan Level 10


The Artisan gains one of: Aetheric Infusion if they are an Artificer, Artisan’s Reforge if they are a Crafter, or Eureka! If they are a Mystic. The Artisan may only have one Opus Power. If the Artisan’s Specialization changes, a different Opus Power will automatically replace their current one.
Opus: Aetheric Infusion [Artificer]
Requirement: Artificer Specialization
Incantation: None
Call: “Short Grant Resistance to Shatter”, “Heal to Full”, “Instruction: Until you complete a Short Rest, your Armor is considered Natural Armor.”
Target: Individual item                                Duration: Event
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Immediate
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Event Duration


The Artificer can do any of the following at-will.
               * Expend a Mithril Ore to Touch another and Call “Short Grant Resistance to Shatter.”
               * Expend a Golden Blossom to charge 5 packets with “Heal to Full”. Each packet must be thrown separately.
               * Expend a Raw Scale to Touch another and give the explanation: “Instruction: Until you complete a Short Rest, your Armor is considered Natural Armor.”


Expended Resources should be placed in an Ashbin as soon as possible. 
Opus: Artisan’s Reforge [Crafter]
Requirement: Crafter Specialization
Incantation: None
Call: None, “Rebuild [Armor, Shield, Weapon]”
Target: One Schematic, One Item                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Immediate


The Artisan may reduce the Basic Resource cost of listed Basic Resources in crafting formulae by 1, to a minimum of 1 of each listed Basic Resource.


The Artisan can Rebuild equipment at a Tinker’s Workshop at no Resource cost. In addition, at-will, the Artisan can expend 10 points of Living Iron to Rebuild a weapon, suit of armor, or shield after the Living Iron Pool Incantation is complete. 


Finally, the Artisan’s Living Iron pool is doubled.
Opus: Eureka! [Mystic]
Requirement: Mystic Specialization
Incantation: None
Call: “Grant Long Rest by Mind”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Event
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Grant


Once per Event, the Artisan may Touch a willing or helpless target and call “Grant Long Rest by Mind.”
Artisan Class Skills


Studied Process [Class] - 2 BP (3)
Requirement: Artisan Level 5
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: None                                        Duration: Passive
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None


The Artisan has found shortcuts and efficiencies in Plans after many hours spent honing and perfecting their craft.


Choose one Craft: Alchemy, Enchanting, or Tinkering. The Artisan may reduce all mentions of “turns of the hourglass” by half, and single turns of the hourglass are reduced to a Quick Count of 30. All other repeated actions are reduced by half (rounded up). This power may be purchased once for each Craft.


Note: This Power stacks with other time reduction skills for crafting, however, the reductions from this Power are calculated first.


Utilitarian [Class] - 4 BP
Requirement: Artisan Level 6


The Artisan learns an additional Utility Power from the Artisan Class Powers.
Batch Process [Class] - 4 BP (4)
Requirement: Artisan Level 7
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: None                                        Duration: Passive
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None


Masters of their trade, Artisans craft some Plans so often that the patterns have become rote muscle memory, making it trivial to build several copies in parallel. 


Choose one Craft: Alchemy, Ritual Magic, Enchanting, or Tinkering. The Artisan may call “Batch Process” at the beginning of their crafting attempt to double the number of outputs from the crafting plan. This requires that the Artisan expend double the appropriate resources for the plan they are crafting. The Forgesource plan cannot be affected with this skill.


Note: Crafting processes requiring a Dark Territory card pull must be pulled twice from the same hand. Resolve the first card before the second is pulled. 
Quality Control [Class] - 2 BP (5)
Requirement: Artisan Level 8
Incantation: None
Call: “[Name or Description] Quality Control”, Explanation
Target: Other Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Verbal                                        Refresh: Event


The Artisan may oversee their own or an ally’s crafting recipe once per event. The crafting resources are refunded if the process fails so long as the failure condition does not specify otherwise.
It’s Alive! [Class] - 7 BP
Requirement: Artisan Level 10
Incantation: None
Call: “[Name or Description] Revive by Lightning”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Verbal                                        Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: Lightning                                Effect: Revive


Once per Long Rest, an Artificer may sacrifice an Ingot to call “Revive by Lightning” to one individual.
Artisan Utility Powers
A Spoonful of Sugar [Utility] [Crafter]
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: One Alchemical Object                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Long Rest


The Artisan gains the Apprentice Alchemy skill (3).

Once per Long Rest, the Artisan can remove the “Mana Sickness” detriment from a single Alchemical item they produce during the crafting process.
Adept Ritualist [Utility] [Mystic]
Skills and Options: The Artisan gains the Apprentice Ritual Magic skill.


The Adept Ritualist gains the following benefits at various Artisan Levels:


               * Level 1 - The Artisan may count as two Participants in Rituals, taking up the available slots and giving benefits.
               * Level 3 - If the Artisan is acting as a Ritualist, reduce one Basic Resource (Bloom, Hide, Ingots, or Wealth) required for a Ritual to a minimum of 1, once per Long Rest. Place a stand in item if the Resource has a part in the Ritual
               * Level 7 - As above, the Artisan may count as two Ritualists. They may not perform functions of multiple Ritualists.
Apt Assistant [Utility] [Crafter]
Incantation: None
Call: “[Name or Description] Instruction: Halve all repeated task repetitions and turns of the timer”
Target: Other Individual                                Duration: Special
Delivery: Verbal                                        Refresh: Immediate
Accent: None                                        Effect: Special


The Artisan can streamline another crafter’s process by helping at a Crafting Station of any kind. RP handing tools, helping follow the Recipe, etc. Halve all repeated task repetitions and the number of turns on the timer (round up).


Additionally, in specific cases, this power can reduce the time to accomplish Counted tasks.


Note: This Power stacks with other time reduction skills for crafting, however, no repetitions / turns can be reduced below 1 each by this Power.
Custom Creation [Utility] [Crafter]
Incantation: Focus Quick 100
Call: None
Target: Individual item                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Immediate
Accent: None                                        Effect: Special
Skills and Options: The Artisan gains the Apprentice Enchanting (3) skill.


This Power has two uses which may be used separately or simultaneously. The Artisan spends a few seconds focusing on the item to alter its structure, allowing them to add or alter Attunement requirements.


Item Requirements:


               * Crafted by the Artisan
               * Has a card
               * Requires Attunement


Power Use #1 - Add Attunement


After focusing, the Artisan may add any kind of Attunement requirement. Examples include something as specific as “Can only be Attuned to Teo Trastvara” and as generic as “Can only be Attuned to a worshiper of The Steed”. Attunement is not lost if the required trait changes (such as converting to a different Devotion).


Power Use #2 - Add Limitation


After focusing, the Artisan may add a specific limitation to an item’s activation and use. This limitation might be “Can only be activated by Humans” or “To activate the item, the character must yell ‘I love soup!’” Limitations requiring abusive or non-consensual behavior immediately fail.


For either use of this Power, the item’s card must be updated with the information. 
Forged From Steel [Utility] [Artificer]
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive, Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None, Immediate


The Artisan gains 1 additional Mend Point per Artisan class-level to their Living Iron pool. 


Additionally, the Artisan may Sacrifice a Forgesource to gain 10 Mend Points to their Living Iron pool at-will. The Forgesource should be placed in an Ashbin as soon as is convenient.
Forgesource Specialist [Utility] [Artificer] [Crafter]
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive, Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None, Immediate


The Artisan gains the skill Apprentice Tinkering (3).


Forgesource Specialists may make Forgesources as an Apprentice Tinker, and may craft two simultaneously.


Finally, the Artisan may replace any Uncommon resource (Rare Minerals, Harvest, Night Prizes) in a Tinkering Schematic with a Forgesource at-will.
Good Timing [Utility] [Crafter]
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None


The Artisan gains Prospect I, refunding as necessary. Additionally, you may take Prospect III before reaching 10th level.


The Artisan may skip Prospecting during the first Long Rest period of an Event and instead Prospect twice per Mine/Rare Mineral Spring in the next Long Rest period.
Hunter Gatherer [Utility] [Crafter]
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None


The Artisan gains Forage I, refunding as necessary. Additionally, you may take Forage III before reaching 10th level.


The Artisan is used to walking the land looking for Bloom and Night Prizes. Hunter Gatherers may take 1 additional Night Prize from a "mushroom patch" for every rank of Foraging they possess. This does not increase the total number of Night Prizes they may collect per Event overall, just from any particular patch.
Mechanical Augmentation [Utility] [Artificer]
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Long Rest


The Artisan can mechanically augment themselves, becoming a powered construct. They must have a Forgesource that is not being used for other things with them the entire time the Power is activated. While Augmented the Artisan has the Construct Qualifier (Not Type).


In addition, when using a Power that targets themselves, Mechanically Augmented Artisans may use a Spike in lieu of a Forgesource once per Power. The Artisan is inherently slowed as their body is infused with mechanical features. The Artisan may spend 4 points of Living Iron to Counter Poison, Agony, and Fear Accents by calling “Counter, Augmentation”. 
Otherworldly Sight [Utility] [Mystic]
Incantation: Focus Quick 100
Call: “[Name or Description] Subtle Discern by Mind: What is your Lineage?” OR “[Name or Description] Subtle Discern by Mind: Are you a/an [Exalted, Undead, Voidborn]?”
Target: Other Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Verbal                                        Refresh: Immediate
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Discern


The Artisan can examine an individual and try to discern their nature. Note that in mass melees and high pressure/speed situations, Discerning is hard and erroneous/no answer may occur.
Perspicacity [Utility] [Artificer] [Mystic]
Incantation: Focus Quick 100
Call: “[Name or Description] Subtle Discern by Mind: What is one of your Vulnerabilities?”
Target: Other Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Verbal                                        Refresh: Immediate
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Discern


The Artisan can Focus on a particular foe to determine its weaknesses (must be a vulnerability Condition). Note that in mass melees and high pressure/speed situations, Discerning is hard and erroneous/no answer may occur.
Protected Casting [Utility] [Mystic]
Incantation: None
Call: Explanation
Target: One Circle                                Duration: Special
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Long Rest


The Artisan can protect a ritual from disturbance with a small rite of consecration. First, the Artisan lays down a white rope or cord that is no longer than 100 feet long that has been laid down to make a continuous shape (usually a circle). Then the Artisan walks the perimeter of the rope, chanting and speaking words of mystic significance. During this time, the Artisan should also explain to the others who will take part in the Ritual how the Weapon and Packet resistance works (see below). This should take at least 30 seconds. 


Anyone not there for the explanation, will not get the benefit. At the completion of this invocation, the caster should touch the completed circle, and declare the casting complete, at which point the lain rope cannot be purposefully moved by anyone other than the caster, and anyone who attempts to do so should be informed by use of “Instruction: You may not move the rope.” or similar. This does not prevent people from crossing the threshold of the cord, rope, or the like. 


Should the rope be moved purposefully by the caster, the Power ends. This should be communicated to others trying to touch or manipulate it. The circle can be Dispelled by Dispel Magic and similar magic. The circle’s power ends as soon as a ritual completes inside it, or when moved by the caster, whichever comes first.


While inside the cord and engaged in a Ritual casting, all Ritualists and Participants actively involved in a Ritual have resistance to Weapons and Packets from sources not involved with the ritual and taking damage from Weapons and Packets will not disrupt the Ritual.
Smithy’s Blessing [Utility] [Artificer]
Incantation: None
Call: “Mend 1”
Target: Worn armor                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Immediate


Tight spots and dangerous situations taught the Artisan to make resources stretch where others could not. The Artisan may Mend 1 to their own Armor when administering a Health Draught to another. The Artisan may also Mend 1 to their own Armor whenever they expend a Forgesource for any reason.
Shield Bearer [Utility] [Artificer]
Incantation: None
Call: “Counter, Shield Bearer”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Immediate

The Artisan gains the Basic Shield Skill (4).


The Artisan may Sacrifice 2 Life Points to Counter any Disarm Effect against the shield. 
Spirit Guide [Utility] [Mystic]
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None


The Artisan gains the Supernatural Perk: Medium (5).
Tanner [Utility] [Crafter]
Incantation: None
Call: “Counter, Tanner”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Immediate


The Artisan gains Scavenge I, refunding as necessary. Additionally, you may take Scavenge III before reaching 10th level.


The Artisan knows their way around a knife and hide. Tanners may instantly Scavenge a creature not already being Scavenged by another. The Artisan may call "Counter, Tanner" to any damage received while waiting for their token.


Note: This Power ends once they receive their token, or if they take any actions other than waiting to receive their loot. The Counter does not prevent any damage taken from the Scavenging itself. The Counter does not stop Effects.
Triage [Utility] [Mystic]
Incantation: None
Call: “Discern: [Question]”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Immediate
Accent: None                                        Effect: Discern


The Artisan gains the Basic Medicine and Diagnose Skills.


The Artisan has seen their fair share of combat and served as a healer. Quickly diagnosing what is wrong and how to best handle the situation has become second nature. 


The Artisan has more questions they may ask when using Diagnose, honing their perception to uncover personality secrets based on physical observations. The wording need not be exact but the question must stay true to the spirit:


               * Are you currently in a heightened state of emotion/stress?
               * Are you under the effects of mind-altering substances?
               * Have you been in Spirit Form in the last 12 hours?
               * Have you ever had your memory altered?
               * Are you possessed or controlled?
               * What is your profession/class?
Artisan Basic Powers
Acid Soak [Basic] [Artificer]
Incantation: Focus Quick 100
Call: “Wounding 8 by Acid” 
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Packet                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: Acid                                        Effect: Wounding


Carefully, the Artisan processes a number of reagents in sinister ways, adding their lethal humours to soak a Packet full of materia in a solution that will react over a few seconds, making for a powerfully acidic projectile. The Artisan must work on the packet for a Focus Quick 100, sacrificing three Night Prizes, and may then throw it at any time. If the Packet leaves their hand or touches any object other than their hand, it will discharge. The Night Prizes should be deposited in an Ashbin when convenient.
Aegis of the Unknown / Sundering of the Unknown [Basic] [Mystic]
Incantation: None
Call: “Short Grant Minus One Dark Territory” or “Short Grant Plus One Dark Territory”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Dark Territory


The Artisan has been around the block a few hundred times, and knows how to focus their energies in a way to limit or expand the potential for chaotic outcomes in their work. The artisan marks the target with a glyph personal to them, and the glyph either represents the contraction or expansion of possibility, depending on if they are granting less or more Dark Territory.


Once the target is appropriately marked, the target’s Dark Territory modifier is modified as per the Artisan’s choice by one until their next Short Rest. The target may only be the recipient of this boon once per Event from any source.


Enhancement: Sacrifice 3 Rare Minerals: The change in Dark Territory will be 2.
Analysis [Basic] [Crafter]
Incantation: Focus Quick 100
Call: “Subtle Discern by Mind: What are the properties of this item?”
Target: Individual item                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Discern


The Artisan can inspect any item that has an information card for a Quick Count of 100 and then read the information card. This will not make the character immune to any Effects or consequences from being in range of the item. If there is a Marshal available, the Marshal may be asked to give the properties of any item, including ones that do not have information cards, but the information given is up to the attending Marshal. 


Note: This power will reveal only the information available on the card, but not obfuscated effects.
Antidote [Basic] [Crafter]
Incantation: None
Call: “Cure [drained/slept/tainted]”, “Cure All Conditions by Poison”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None, Poison                                Effect: Cure


After a minute role-playing finding, preparing, and applying various ingredients to an individual, the Artisan can Cure the drained, slept, or tainted Condition.


Enhancement: Expend a Bloom: The Artisan can Cure all Conditions (except for Obedient). Place the Bloom in the Ashbin when possible.
Armor Upkeep Specialist [Basic] [Artificer] [Crafter]
Incantation: Focus Quick 100
Call: “Mend Armor to Full”
Target: Tinkering Craft: Field Patch Kit                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Mend


The Artisan can alter a Field Patch Kit they are crafting, instead creating a "Specialist Field Patch Kit" with a 1 year expiration. The Specialist Field Patch Kit can mend armor to full once per Long Rest. This use does not consume the Kit if the Artisan who created it does the repair.


Maintaining the Specialist Field Patch Kit requires dedicated attention, so the Artisan can only manage one Kit at a time. Furthermore, it will revert to a normal Field Patch Kit if this Power is lost or removed.


The item’s card must be updated with these special features.
Borrow Knowledge [Basic] [Mystic]
Incantation: None
Call: “Subtle Discern by Mind: What Lore Skills do you have?”
Target: Willing Individual                                Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Discern


Out of combat, the Artisan sits down with a willing creature and puts their hands near the other’s temples (remember not to touch unless consent has already been explicitly established). They should walk the target through a meditation, or explain how they are sifting through the other’s Skills and talents. When they are done with the role-play, the Artisan Discerns all the Lore Core Skills the target has, and gains all those Skills until they complete a Short Rest.
Blood Scroll [Basic] [Crafter]
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Long Rest


Normally when an Enchanter creates a scroll using the Scribe Novice Scroll formulae they must use Simple Ink to write the words of the scroll. The Artisan has gained the ability to ignore the alchemical part of this process and scribe the magics onto the hide using their own blood. Using Blood Scroll, the Artisan can ignore the need for Simple Ink for one scroll when they use Scribe Novice Scroll by Sacrificing 1 Life Point of blood. 
Combat Analysis [Basic] [Artificer]
Incantation: Focus Quick 30
Call: “Counter, Dodge”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Short Rest (or combat ends)
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Counter


Once per Short Rest the Artisan must watch an enemy or group of enemies carefully for a Quick Count of 30. Doing so allows the Artisan to Counter one Weapon attack by a member of that group. The Call is “Counter, Dodge”. If the Counter is not used before the Artisan goes out-of-combat or the Artisan completes a Short Rest, it is lost.
Reconstitute Summon [Basic] [Mystic]
Incantation: None
Call: “Mend Summoned Armor to Full and Grant Three Barrier by Mind”
Target: Other Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Mend, Grant Barrier


The Artisan can Touch another and call “Mend Summoned Armor to Full and Grant Three Barrier by Mind.”
Create Infusion [Basic] [Crafter]
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Essence                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Long Rest


Normally when an Enchanter pulls from the Reality Tear the Enchanter may either Soulhold what is pulled or place it in an Essence Infusion and leave the Reality Tear with it. The Artisan has gained the ability to ignore the alchemical part of this process and through sheer force of will bind the Essence into a form that is identical to an Essence Infusion. 


Using this Power, the Artisan can create an Essence Infusion without any other resources or alchemy when they use Delve Reality (any level) and may create an Essence Infusion card for one resource they pulled from the Tear without previously having an Essence Infusion to store it.
Critique Construction [Basic] [Artificer]
Incantation: Focus Quick 100
Call: “Imbue Weapon with Shatter Shield”
Target: Other Individual                                Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Imbue


The Artisan focuses, examining the shields around them, especially those of the enemy. The Artisan then explains the weakness to another allowing them to exploit that weakness (and must RP a short piece of advice to the target of this Power). The recipient of the explanation may use a single Shatter against a shield.
Custom Brew [Basic] [Crafter] (3)
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Alchemy Craft                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Long Rest


The Artisan can alter the creation of any alchemical item they are crafting that has an associated item card to require 1 less Bloom or 1 less Night Prize (minimum 1).
Dig Deep [Basic] [Mystic]
Incantation: Focus Quick 30, short inspiring speech
Call: “Short Imbue with Refresh All Spikes”
Target: Other Individuals                        Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Imbue
The Artisan can give a short speech (an Incantation) to up to six other individuals. They all gain the ability to refresh all their Spikes once before they complete a Short Rest.
Forcefield [Basic] [Artificer]

Call: "Grant Protect vs Packets by Lightning"
Target: Individual                                Duration: Until Used
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: Lightning                                Effect: Grant Protect


The Artisan may focus while holding a Forgesource to Grant a Protect vs Packets.


Enhancement: Expend the Forgesource: Target becomes up to four individuals and the Artisan.
Intimidating Spines [Basic] [Artificer]
Incantation: None
Call: “[Name or Description]: Short Repel by Fear”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Verbal                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: Fear                                        Effect: Repel


The Artisan surrounds themselves with harmful blades, spikes, and whirling implements of doom. For the duration, the affected reels back in fear and refuses to approach them. 
Jury Rig [Basic] [Artificer]
Incantation: Focus Quick 30
Call: “Mend 5”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Mend


By Focusing for a Quick 30 while fixing and readjusting a character’s armor, the Artisan can Mend Armor for 5 points.


Enhancement: Expend two ingots or a Patch Kit: The Power refreshes immediately. Put the expended resources in the earliest convenient Ashbin.
Kick [Basic] [Crafter]
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Object: [Alchemy Craft]                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Immediate
Accent: None                                        Effect: Heal, Grant Barrier, Intoxicate


An Artisan can take an existing potion (any quaffed liquid created with alchemy or tagged quaffed liquid found in the world) and add a little kick to it. After spending a minute or so adding to, mixing, and enhancing the potion, they may choose one of three benefits that will take place when the potion is imbibed:


               * Heal 1
               * Grant 1 Barrier
               * Intoxicate


No Potion can be Kicked more than once. The Kick effect should be added to the information card for the item if it has one. If there is no information card, either one must be made, or only the Artisan can get the Kick effects.
Mesmerize [Basic] [Mystic]
Incantation: None
Call: “[Name or Description] Short Charm by Mind”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Verbal                                        Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Charm


The Mystic has learned the ways of hypnotism and beguilement. Out-of-combat, after a minute of conversation the Mystic may Call “[Name or Description] Short Charm by Mind” to the target of their words. This Power has no function for Artificer or Crafter Artisans.
Overcharge [Basic] [Artificer]
Incantation: Focus Quick 100
Call: “Short Grant Plus One Bonus Spike Damage”, “Short Grant Plus Two Bonus Spike Damage”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Long Rest


The Artisan may focus while holding a Forgesource to Grant themselves a +1 bonus Spike Damage until their next Short Rest.


Enhancement: Sacrifice the Forgesource: Instead the Artisan Grants themselves +2 bonus Spike Damage for the same duration.
Share [Basic] [Mystic]
Incantation: Special
Call: “[Name or Description] Discern by Mind: How do you feel right now?”, “[Name or Description] Cure [Condition or Accent]”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Verbal                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Discern


This Power can only be used out of combat with another Individual and, ideally, should be done in private. The Artisan connects themself to the emotions of the other sitting in silence with them for at least a minute. After the minute, the Artisan should describe their own emotional state in honest, descriptive terms. They should not convey facts other than about how they feel (i.e. “I feel hurt and betrayed” rather than “Because Triss ended up taking Tymera on his quest instead of me, I feel hurt and betrayed”). 


When they feel they have fully described their feelings, they should Call “Discern: How do you feel right now?” The target should then describe their emotional state. After this sharing, until the end of the Event, the Artisan can, once, as a Verbal, Cure that character of dominated, charmed, obedient, or any Condition caused by the Fear accent. The Artisan can only have the power to Cure any one individual once at any one time (i.e. Share cannot be used multiple times on the same individual to stack the Curing Effect.)
Spiritual Anchor [Basic] [Mystic]
Incantation: None
Call: “[Name or Description] Short Grant resistance to Insubstantial.”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Verbal                                        Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant


The Artisan can force an individual to remain corporeal. The Artisan can call out “[Name or Description] Short Grant resistance to Insubstantial.” Reminder: Inherent conditions, such as ones passively granted from having a Type, cannot be removed or prevented.
Trap Recovery [Basic] [Artificer]
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: One Expended Trap                        Duration: Instantaneous        
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Long Rest


The Artisan can spend a Quick 100 gathering the pieces of a trap that they put down themself which has since gone off. This will allow a Tinker to remake the trap without any additional expenditure of resources. Alternatively, the Artisan can turn in the pieces to Staff to convert them into all the resources that were required to make the trap, minus one (i.e. the Artisan must choose one resource used to make the trap and sacrifice it). At 10 Artisan class-levels they can salvage any trap into its component resources in the above listed manner. 
Treat [Basic] [Artificer] [Mystic]
Incantation: Focus Slow 10 per target
Call: “Heal 1 by Poison”
Target: Up to six Individuals                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Short Rest


This Power can only be used out of combat. By expending three Bloom, the Artisan can provide a powerful curative brew for up to six other Individuals, moving from one to the other giving each a sip which will heal small wounds and injuries. No one target can be given more than one sip. The Artisan must Focus for at least 10 seconds over each character to be Treated. All targets are Healed for 1 Life Points. The Bloom should be deposited in an Ashbin when possible.
Warming Elixir / Cooling Draught [Basic] [Crafter]
Incantation: Prepare a Drink
Call: “Grant Protect vs Ice by Flame” or “Grant Protect vs Flame by Ice” 
Target: Individual                                Duration: Until Used
Delivery: Explanation                                 Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: Flame, Ice                                Effect: Grant Protect


The Artisan brews a potion expending 1 Bloom to receive the boon. When brewing, the Artisan chooses the effect and must choose the same effect for all who receive the benefit. The brew is only potent for a short time and must be consumed immediately after the Artisan finishes brewing it. After drinking the brew, up to 4 participants are granted “Grant Protect vs Ice by Flame” or “Grant Protect vs Flame by Ice.” The Bloom should be deposited in an Ashbin when possible.
Weapon Upkeep Specialist [Basic] [Crafter]
Incantation: Focus Quick 100
Call: None
Target: Tinkering Craft: Sharpening Stone        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Long Rest


The Artisan can alter the creation of a Sharpening Stone they are crafting. If this process is used, the resulting creation is called a "Perfect Sharpening Stone" with an expiration of 1 year. This one-use item provides a +1 bonus to Spike Damage until the user completes a Short Rest. On Thrown and Projectile weapons, the bonus is only on those specific weapons, and the weapons must be distinguishable in some way from the others. The item-cards must be updated with these special features.
Weird Wanderings [Basic] [Mystic]
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None


When this power is chosen the Artisan can choose one Basic-Tier Power from any other non-Artisan Base Class. This choice is permanent, and the Power will have the same Incantation, requirements, etc. as it did in the original class. The Power cannot have a Refresh of “Spell”.
Artisan Advanced Powers


Advanced Treatment [Advanced] [Mystic] [Crafter]
Incantation: Focus Slow 10 per individual
Call: “Short Grant +1 Maximum Life Points by Poison.”
Target: Up to Six Individuals                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: Poison                                        Effect: Grant Maximum Life


This Power can only be used out of combat. By expending one Bloom and one Rare Mineral, the Artisan can provide a powerful curative brew for up to six Individuals, moving from one to the other giving each a sip which will invigorate and fortify their bodies. No one target can be given more than one sip. The Artisan must Focus for at least 10 seconds over each character to be Treated. As each one is treated, the Artisan may call “Short Grant +1 Maximum Life Points by Poison.” The Resources should be deposited in an Ashbin when possible.
Detonate [Advanced] [Artificer]
Incantation: Focus Quick 100
Call: “Imbue Weapon with Explosive Impact”, Explanation
Target: Individual                                Duration: Until Used
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Power


The Artisan can prepare a weapon to ignite and explode, by preparing strange fuses, oils, and other ingredients. After the Artisan spends a Quick Count of 100 in deep Focus, role-playing this preparation, the character is ready to detonate their weapon. At any time the target chooses, a melee weapon can be ignited to use the Explosive Impact Power below. This should be explained to the target while the preparations are being made. 


Note: The Role-Playing of this Power may include props or equipment given to the target, or infusing the character’s limb with bizarre oils; the exact details are up to the Artisan. 


Explosive Impact
Call: “Wounding 8 by Flame”, “Shatter Weapon Final to Self”
Target: Individual, Self                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Weapon, Verbal                        Refresh: None
Accent: Flame                                        Effect: Wounding, Shatter


A melee weapon can be used to deliver a Wounding Effect for 8 points of Flame damage. This will, unfortunately, also cause the weapon to Shatter. This should be explained to the target while the weapon is being prepared.
	

Efficiencies of Scale [Advanced] [Crafter]
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None


The Artisan can perform any of the “collection” Plans (Ingot Sheets, Bloom Powder, Night Prize Essence, etc.) regardless of their Crafting Skills. They can perform any of those away from a Crafting Station (but out-of-combat) by spending at least a minute on each one and instead of the normal process substitute their own roleplay choices of crafting the item.
Empower Friends [Advanced] [Mystic]
Incantation: RP: See Description
Call: “Refresh One Novice Spell Slot”, “Refresh One Adept Spell Slot”
Target: Up to Two Other Individuals                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Refresh


The Artisan may use their knowledge of the mystical forces to refresh the spell slots of up to two other individuals. The Artisan restores 1 Novice and 1 Adept spell slot in total. These slots may be Arcane or Divine and may be from Base or Advanced Classes. The Artisan should roleplay an incantation of meaning to their character that lasts approximately 30 seconds or longer to use this Power. This Power may not be refreshed by any means outside of receiving a Long Rest.
Exploding Arrows [Advanced] [Artificer]
Incantation: Focus Quick 100
Call: “Imbue Weapon with Shatter Shield by Flame”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Event
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Immediate
Accent: Flame                                        Effect: Imbue


Out-of-combat, the Artisan can treat a single projectile with a powerful explosive. This mixture is concocted by Sacrificing two Rare Mineral and two Harvest and treating an arrow. This preparation takes Focus of a Quick 100 Count.


Note: Reminder that any one individual can only have one Imbued Power at any one time.
Grounded [Advanced] [Artificer]
Incantation: None
Call: “Counter, Grounded”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Counter


The Artisan has so many small trinkets, wires, and other conductive objects, that Lightning based attacks have a significant chance of simply routing into the ground. Once per Short Rest, the Artisan may call “Counter, Grounded” against any effect with the Lightning Accent.
Hiatus [Advanced] [Mystic]
Incantation: None
Call: “Hiatus, Grant Insubstantial to Self”, “Cure Insubstantial to Self.”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Focus Slow 300
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Insubstantial


While in a Ritual, the Artisan may call “Grant Insubstantial to Self” and Focus for a Slow 300. Once the Count is complete, the Artisan must call “Cure Insubstantial to Self.”


While Insubstantial, the Artisan can pause a ritual in progress in which they are the Primary Ritualist. If their Focus is broken, the Slow Count is completed, or at the Artisan’s discretion, whichever comes first, the Ritual resumes as normal, and the Artisan must call “Cure Insubstantial to Self.” Any actions taken during the Focus period should be ignored by the attending Ritual Marshal for the purposes of determining failure or Dark Territory.


Note: This means that while the Artisan themselves are incapable of interacting with things, others may do so on their behalf.
Infuse [Advanced] [Crafter] [Artificer]
Incantation: Focus Quick 100
Call: None
Target: Weapon                                        Duration: Event
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Short Rest


The Artisan unravels an Essence Infusion - Energy to alter and empower a weapon, giving it properties beyond its normal. This process must be done out-of-combat and should take Focus for a Quick Count of 100. The weapon should be given a tag that shows it having the following properties: It has a +1 bonus to Spike damage and can be used with any of the four elemental Accents (Acid, Flame, Ice, or Lightning) as desired. The tag should be given an expiration that matches the day after the Event finishes. The Essence Infusion should be deposited in an Ashbin when possible.
Perfect Practice [Advanced] [Crafter] [Mystic]
Incantation: None
Call: Special
Target: Self                                        Duration: Special        
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Immediate


The Artisan gains additional benefits each time they use a Craft of Apprentice level or higher (ie: Supervise Armor Mending would not trigger this Power). 


               * Each time the Artisan completes a Tinkering Plan, they gain 3 to their Living Iron pool. This has no effect if the Artisan already has more points in their Living Iron Pool than their reset number.
               * Each time the Artisan completes an Enchanting Ritual, they may gain a Protect vs Packets.
               * Each time the Artisan completes an Alchemy Craft, they gain the ability to reduce a Quick 100 Count to a Quick 30 Count once. The Artisan can only have one instance of this active at a time.
               * Each time the Artisan Mends at least 5 Armor at a time, they may also “Grant 2 Barrier” to the person whose armor was Mended.
Reinforce Shield [Advanced] [Artificer]
Incantation: None
Call: “Grant Protect vs. Shatter Shield”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Immediate
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Protect


Whenever the Artisan uses the Rebuild Effect on a Shield, they can also Grant “Protection vs. Shatter Shield” to the shield’s wielder. If no one is wielding the shield, the Artisan may either choose not to Grant the Protect, or can take it themself.
Soulhold Survival [Advanced] [Crafter]
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Short Rest


When the Artisan draws an Unravelling Essence that would normally kill them, they can choose to soulhold it. If they do so, as long as the Unravelling Essence is soulheld, the Artisan will remain alive, but the moment it ceases being soulheld, the Artisan will take the Death Final effect. 
Spark of Doom [Advanced] [Artificer]
Incantation: Focus Quick 100 
Call: “Wounding 2 by Lightning” 
Target: Individual                                 Duration: Short Rest 
Delivery: Packet                                         Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: Lightning                                Effect: Wounding


Carefully, the Artisan breaks down a Forgesource, altering it to expend its charges directly into Materia. The Artisan must work on the Forgesource for a Focus Quick 100 to complete the alterations. Until the Artisan completes a Short Rest, they can touch a Packet to the Forgesource to charge it with Lightning Power. Charging takes a Focus Quick Count of 30, and each Packet does "Wounding 2 by Lightning". This damage cannot exceed a maximum of 3 from any other source. Only one Packet can be charged at a time. The same Forgesource may charge multiple packets in this way, but after the power expires the Artisan should deposit the Forgesource in the Ashbin when possible. Note: This Forgesource cannot be substituted using the Mechanical Augmentation power.
Spiritual Defense [Advanced] [Mystic]
Incantation: None
Call: “Counter, Spiritual Defense”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Short Rest


The Artisan’s very spirit itself has been strengthened through their dabbling in Mystical forces. When targeted with a Power or attack that has the “to Living”, “to Substantial”, or “to [Artisan’s Devotion]” qualifiers the Mystic may call “Counter, Spiritual Defense” and negate the attack. If the Mystic believes they know the name of the creature that attacked the Mystic, the Mystic may also gain five Barrier when they use this Power. The Mystic being correct about the target’s name is inconsequential for this Power, only that the Mystic has a good faith belief that the name is correct.
Strange Token [Advanced] [Mystic]
Incantation: None
Requirement: Mystic Specialization
Call: “Grant Power: Curious Balm”, Explanation
Target: Object                                        Duration: Event
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Power


Through prayers to a Divine Being, energy manipulation, or binding of minor spirits, the Mystic energizes up to six objects with power that may be gifted to others. When bestowed to another, the Mystic explains the token’s healing properties, and how they are used. The bound powers are non-transferrable, and cannot be regifted. Such power comes with a price, and the life given will take something in return, causing the recipient to be drained until they complete a Short Rest. The recipients are granted the Curious Balm Power below:


Curious Balm
Incantation: Quick 100
Call: “Heal 1”, “Short Drain”
Target: Self                                Duration: Until Used (Long Rest), Instantaneous, Short Rest
Delivery: None                        Refresh: None
Accent: None                                 Effect: Heal, Drain


By using the small token that they were given by the Artisan, the character gains the ability to Heal herself for 1 point before the next time they complete a Long Rest. This healing takes a Quick Count of 100, but can be done while the character is dying. After the healing is complete, the character is drained until they complete a Short Rest.
	

Studied Focus [Advanced] [Artificer] [Crafter] [Mystic]
Instead of taking an Advanced Power, the Artisan can choose two Basic-tier Powers from the Artisan Base Class with the same Specialty Tag (ie: Artificer, Crafter, Mystic).
Uninterrupted Creativity [Advanced] [Crafter]
Incantation: None
Call: “[Name of Description] Short Obey by Mind: Do not interrupt my crafting.”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Verbal                                        Refresh: Immediate
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Obey


While touching a Crafting Station of any kind (Tinker’s Workshop, Enchanting Forge, or Hermetic Lab) and actively crafting, the Artisan can make others leave them alone to work. They can use this Power an unlimited number of times (but only once per individual), and doing so will not interrupt any Crafting they are doing. The Artisan may only target the same target once per application of this power.
Zenith of Knowledge [Advanced] [Mystic]
Incantation: None
Call: “Short Obey by Mind, Tell me one of your most important secrets that I do not know.”
Target: Individual or Marshal                Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Verbal                                        Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Obey


After completing a Ritual where the Artisan Mystic is the Primary Ritualist, the Artisan may ask one of the following questions of the Marshal guiding the ritual, which the Marshal should attempt to answer honestly:


               * “What is the effect of the next card in the deck (either higher or lower)?”
               * “What downsides to this ritual being cast may I not have considered that you can think of?”
               * “Provide me a potentially helpful cryptic phrase, vision, or short story.”

Or instead, the Ritualist may target one of the ritual’s Participants with a verbal Obey Effect:

               * Short Obey by Mind, “Tell me one of your most important secrets that I do not know.”
               * Long Obey by Mind, “Tell no one of this Ritual or what took place here.”


Cleric: Base Class
Divine Spell-Casting Class
Description
Clerics are the backbone of any standing force lucky enough to have one. They give strong support with their enchantment magic and keep the team alive with their healing skill. They have some small offensive ability as well. These characters come in many forms, from the followers of the Dancer, who distribute death, to those who follow the path of pacifism and light. Regardless, the Clerics are a powerful addition to any force.


The Cleric comes from a tradition of Divine Being-worshiping magic-users, whose granted abilities could heal the hurt, harm the living, and even raise the dead. Every Cleric should choose a Devotion that they give fealty and worship to, and from whom they are granted favors and power.


The Cleric is a Divine spell-caster. The Cleric should choose a special symbol that is their caster-sigil and mark their spellbook and packets with it.
Starting Skills
               * The Path of the Initiate: Basic Faith (4), Worship (1), Basic Martial Weapons (1)
               * One of the Cloth: Basic Armor (2)
               * The Means of Service - Choose one of the following:
               * Short Weapons (3)
               * Extended Capacity - Novice (3)
               * Basic Medicine (2), Diagnose (1)
               * Divine Study - Choose one of the following:
               * Extended Capacity- Novice x2 (6)
               * Additional Cantrip (6)
               * Bookcaster (1) x 3, Peacecaster (3)
               * Basic Shields (4), Advanced Shields (2)
Multiclass Skills
               * A Vow to the Divine: Basic Faith (4), Worship (1)


Note: If the character selects an option and already has the maximum amount of any of the selected skills, the costs for those skills will be granted as free BP instead.
