Note: Life Points and Maximum Spikes can be found in the Level Progression Table in the Wellspring Core Rules.
Spells Known
The Cleric has a number of Spells that they can choose from. These Spells should be recorded in the Cleric’s Spellbook. Each time the character gains a Cleric level, they can remove one Spell from the list and replace it with another. There are no limits on what tier Spell replaces the removed Spell (meaning a Novice Spell could be removed and replaced with a Greater Spell). 
Base Incantation
“I call my faith: divine and right, I wield and shape creation's light,
With fervent plea and ardent zeal, I summon strength for woe and weal,
From realms beyond, my power's source, With prayer and faith I give it force,
As vessel for the will divine, I claim the power that is mine…”
Cleric Innate Powers
Refreshing Prayer [Innate]
Requirement: Cleric Level 1
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Long Rest


The Cleric can pray to their Divine Being or Devotion to rebuild focus and refresh their Divine Spell-slots. The Cleric can spend a Short Rest studying their spellbook, meditating, or speaking to their higher powers.
 
The Cleric can perform this special study and refresh all of their expended Divine spell-slots. If the Cleric has used any other Innate Power from another class to refresh their Divine spell slots this long rest they may not use Refreshing Prayer.


Additional benefits come if this prayer is done in an area of sacred faith (such as consecrated ground), especially an area sacred to the Cleric’s Devotion. Doing so, the Cleric gains three additional Divine Novice-tier Spell-Slots. These Spell-Slots last until the Cleric completes a Long Rest. Additional benefits may be possible (see the Skills, Perks, and Flaws document for more details). 


Healing Touch [Innate]
Requirement: Cleric Level 2
Incantation: "I call my faith, divine and right, to knit your wounds with healing light."
Call: “Heal [X] by Divine”
Target: Individual (Other)                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Immediate
Accent: Divine                                        Effect: Heal


The cleric gains a pool of healing that can be used between Long Rests called the Healing Touch Pool. This Healing Touch pool is equal to three times the Cleric’s class-level.


Each time this power is used, the Cleric can heal another Individual up to the Cleric’s class-level, subtracting the healing from the Healing Touch Pool. Healing Touch will not work on the caster themself.


For Example: a 4th level Cleric would have 12 total points of Healing to give out between Long Rests and could use this Power to Heal up to 4 points at a time. There is no limit to the number of points that can be in this pool; however, the pool resets to three times the Cleric’s class-level at the end of a Long Rest.


Note: While this Power is an Innate Power, for purposes of Advanced Peacecaster, it counts as a Cantrip. Bonus healing provided by Advanced Peacecaster is not subtracted from the Cleric’s Healing Touch pool, however a minimum of 1 point must always be subtracted.


Ritual Affinity [Innate]
Requirement: Cleric Level 3
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None


The Cleric can use their understanding of Divine magic to help navigate the complexities of Ritual magic. They receive the Apprentice Ritual Magic skill at no cost. Once they achieve 7th level Cleric, they can purchase Journeyman Ritual Magic skill for one fewer Build Point. Once they achieve 12th level Cleric, they can purchase Greater Ritual Magic skill for one fewer Build Point. If they have already purchased either of these skills, they get one Build Point back for each. 
Shield of Faith [Innate]
Requirement: Cleric Level 5, Basic Shields
Incantation: None
Call: “Counter, Shield of Faith”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                         Effect: Counter


The Cleric's faith protects them from those that seek to do them harm, forming a magical aegis around a shield the Cleric wields in battle.


Once per Short Rest, the Cleric may call “Counter, Shield of Faith” when their shield is hit by an attack with Weapon or Packet delivery, Preventing the attack. Attacks that do not strike the Cleric's shield may not be countered in this way.
Holy Armor [Innate]
Requirement: Cleric Level 9
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Maximum Summoned Armor


The Cleric's Divine Being lends their power to enhance their defenses, increasing the Maximum Summoned and Physical Armor of the Cleric by 1.


Shepherd the Faithful [Innate]
Requirement: Cleric Level 10
Incantation: None
Call: “Refresh [Power] by Divine”, “Refresh all Novice and Adept Spell Slots to Self by Divine”
Target: Individual (Other), Self                        Duration: Instantaneous        
Delivery: Verbal                                        Refresh: Long Rest, Event
Accent: Divine                                        Effect: Refresh


Once per Long Rest, after having an extended conversation with another member of their Devotion, the Cleric may Refresh one Power of the target with a Refresh of Short or Long Rest (Note: this cannot be used on Powers that themselves have the Refresh effect). Any individual may be the recipient of Shepherd the Faithful only once per Event.

Additionally, once per Event, the Cleric may call upon the power of their Divine Being to refresh all of their Novice and Adept Divine spell slots. Alternatively, they may entreat a creature of the Exalted type for a boon. 


Simple prayer generally is not enough to ask for such a boon through this power, and the entity must be present with the Cleric: often the Beckon Ritual is used for this purpose.


What is granted is up to the entity’s discretion and may vary greatly, as are the consequences of being found unworthy or the presumption of the request. Asking powerful entities for boons is not without risk, especially entities known to be fickle or exacting. (Note: asking a Player Character with the Exalted type for a boon may not have any particular mechanical benefit.)
Cleric Class Skills


Path of Panic [Class] - 1 BP
Requirement: Cleric Level 2
Incantation: None
Call: “Heal 1 to Dying”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Immediate
Accent: None                                        Effect: Refresh


The Cleric can expend 3 points from their Healing Touch Pool to immediately call “Heal 1 to Dying”.  Note, this effect has no incant whatsoever.
Mantle of Faith [Class] - 3 BP
Requirement: Cleric Level 4
Incantation: None
Call: “Grant Protect vs [Devotion Accent]”
Target: Self, Individual                                Duration: Until Used
Delivery: Touch                                         Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Protect


When taking a Short Rest, the Cleric may spend a quick moment reciting prayers or a creed specific to their Devotion. In doing so, they may grant protection against those who would use the nature of their Divine Being’s power against them.

When completing a Short Rest, they may “Grant Protect vs [Devotion Accent]” to an ally and themselves.


Cantrip Scholar [Class] - 4 BP
Requirement: Cleric Level 6


The Cleric learns an additional Cantrip from the Cleric Class Powers or Cantrips.


Expand Focus [Class] - 3 BP
Requirement: Cleric Level 6
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Immediate
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Accent, Grant Qualifier


The Cleric may expend a Spike to alter the “to Undead” Qualifier and Accent on a Spell they cast to “to Exalted by Divine.” The Spell must come from the Cleric Base Class Spell List.


Expansive Worship [Class] - 1 BP
Requirement: Cleric Level 6
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: None                                        Duration: Passive
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None
Accent: None                                        Effect: Special


The Cleric may take abilities within a third divine domain associated with their Devotion. 


Greater Healing Touch [Class] - 4 BP
Requirement: Cleric Level 9
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None
Accent: None                                        Effect: Special


The depth of the Cleric’s devotion to their cause deepens, and with it, new boons are born from the mastery of novice skills. The Cleric now gains one additional point to their healing pool per cleric level for the Healing Touch Archetype Power.


Divine Substitution [Class] - 5 BP
Requirement: Cleric Level 10
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: None                                        Duration: Passive
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None
Accent: None                                        Effect: Special


The Cleric has gained enough mastery over the interpretation of their doctrine that they can begin offering less… common interpretations of their Divine Domain. They may choose a single domain not within their Devotion’s standard domains, and take it as if their Devotion had access to this domain. The Cleric may not take a divine domain that is in direct opposition to their Devotion’s standard domains. See the Divine Domains document for more information. 


Opposed Domains:


Chaos
	Order
	Destruction
	Creation
	Manipulation
	Expression
	Death
	Life
	Light
	Shadow
	Peace
	War
	



Cleric Cantrips
Cleric Cantrip Incantation


“I call my faith divine and right…”


Blade Blessing [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: "Grant Protect vs Uncalled Damage"
Target: Individual                                Duration: Until Used
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Immediate
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Protect


This Protect can be used to stop any hit that has no Call. This is always going to be an attack with no Effects and no Accent that would do 1 point of damage.


Cancel [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Immediate
Accent: None                                        Effect: Refresh


This  cantrip takes the power out of a Spell-Packet (or multiple Spell-Packets) that has already been charged and returns that power to the caster. The Spell-Packet becomes inert, and the caster refreshes the Spell-Slot that was used to charge the Spell-Packet. If the Spell charges more than one Spell-Packet, all the Spell-Packets must still be charged in order to cancel the Spell.
Curing Prayer [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Cure [Condition] By Divine”
Target: Individual (Other)                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Immediate
Accent: Divine                                        Effect: Cure


This spell can only be cast out of combat. The Cleric may Cure any Condition other than tainted, obedient, resistance, and immunity.


Note: During particular marshaled encounters, this spell may not be available, as the entire encounter may be considered “In-combat.”
Detect Devotion [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “[Name or Description], Subtle Discern by Divine: Are you a follower of [Devotion]?” 
Target: Individual (Other)                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Verbal                                        Refresh: Immediate
Accent: Divine                                        Effect: Discern


This divination spell will let the Cleric know if a particular creature is a follower of a specific Devotion or Divine Being, depending on how the spell is cast. The spell may not always result in information, due to the frenetic nature of combat. This spell may be cast upon a living, unconscious, spirit, or dead creature.


Devotion’s Strike [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “2 [Devotion Accent]”, "Imbue Weapon with 2 [Devotion Accent]"
Target: Individual                                 Duration: Until Used
Delivery: Weapon, Touch                        Refresh: Immediate
Accent: [Devotion Accent], None                        Effect: Damage, Imbue


This Cantrip can be cast in two different ways.  Either the caster may charge a single Weapon delivered attack for “2 [Devotion Accent]”, or can Imbue another target with the power to do a single Weapon attack that is Accented with the Cleric’s Devotion Accent. The Imbue call may not be altered.
Divine Bolt [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “1 [Devotion Accent]”
Target:        Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Immediate
Accent: [Devotion Accent]                        Effect: Damage


This minor cantrip charges a Spell-Packet with a bolt of energy tied to their Devotion’s domains, which will do 1 point of damage to those that it hits. 


Revivify [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Heal 1 to Dying”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Focus Quick 100
Accent: None                                        Effect: Heal


The Cleric can Heal 1 Life Point to a Dying target. The Call may not be altered.
Sense Supernatural [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “[Name or Description], Subtle Discern by Divine: Are you a/an [Exalted/Undead/Voidborn]?” 
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Verbal                                        Refresh: Immediate
Accent: Divine                                        Effect: Discern


This divination cantrip will let the Cleric know if a particular creature is Undead or Exalted. Only one of the options can be detected by any single casting of the spell. The spell may not always result in information, due to the frenetic nature of combat. Each question (Undead/Exalted) may only be asked once of any given target.


Shield of Devotion [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Grant Protect vs [Devotion Accent]”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Until Used
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Focus Quick 100
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Protect


The Cleric throws up a shield of divine energy to protect themself. The caster must choose their Devotion’s Accent at the time of casting. 
Cleric Novice Spells
Cleric Novice Incantation


“I call my faith: divine and right, I wield and shape creation's light…”




Armor of Faith [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Long Grant 2 Summoned Armor by Divine” or “Long Grant +1 Physical Armor by Divine”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Long Rest
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: Divine                                        Effect: Grant Summoned Armor, Grant Physical Maximum Armor


The caster grants themself 2 Summoned Armor Points. This is considered a new “suit” of armor and is not stackable with other sources of Summoned Armor. If the Cleric is wearing Physical Armor, they may instead grant themself +1 Armor, increasing their Maximum Armor Points.


Augury [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Instruction: Tell me if it is a good idea or bad idea to [suggested immediate action] by answering ‘boon’, ‘bane’ or ‘unknown’.”
Target: Individual (Marshal)                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Obey


The spell lets the Cleric determine whether an action they are about to take will be, on the whole, harmful or beneficial, to them and their goals. They might, for example, ask if it is boon or bane to walk down the apparently empty hallway in front of them. Or they might ask if it is boon or bane to open the chest they are holding. 


If there is no good answer, the answer is unknown, or there are bad assumptions in the question (“Boon or bane to trust the emissary of The Steed?” when there is not an emissary of The Steed present) will all give “Unknown” as an answer. If the answer is Unknown, the Spell-Slot used to cast the spell is Refreshed.


Benediction [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Heal 2 by Divine” or “Mend 2 by Divine”
Target: Self and up to 6 Individuals                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: Divine                                        Effect: Heal, Mend


This spell can be cast on a group of people who are within Touch range of the caster and can only be cast out of combat. The Cleric can either Heal each target for 2 Life Points, or Mend the armor of each target for 2 points. The same option must be chosen for all targets.


Burn [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Quick Disarm [Left/Right/Both] Arm(s) by Flame.”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Quick 100
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Flame                                        Effect: Disarm


The Cleric calls down holy flame to cause another’s equipment to burn. Any hand that is holding an item will cause that equipment to be disarmed. The caster should only call out arms that they can tell are holding items. If the caster cannot tell whether the hand is empty or not, that arm shouldn’t be called.


Enhancement: Expend a Spike: An additional Spell-Packet is charged.


Cure [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Cure [Condition] By Divine”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Divine                                        Effect: Cure


This spell charges three Spell-Packets with the ability to Cure any Condition, however, each may cure a different condition: they need not be the same condition. Casting this spell will add two points to the Cleric’s Healing Touch pool if they have one, or three if they have Peacecaster. 


Disable Undead [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Disable [Right/Left] Arm to Undead by Divine”
Target: Individual (Undead)                         Duration: Permanent
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Divine                                        Effect: Disable


With the power of their faith, the Cleric charges two Spell-Packets that can destroy the arms of an undead foe.


Enhancement: Expend a Spike: Three Spell-Packets are charged, and the Cleric may target an undead’s legs, in addition to the arms.


Faith’s Lash [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Short Grant Divine to Weapon Attacks”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Accent


Calling on their Devotion, this spell allows the caster to add or substitute the Divine Accent on Weapon attacks.


Fortress of Faith [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Long Imprison to Self by Force”, “Cure Imprisoned”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Long Rest
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: Force                                        Effect: Imprison, Cure


After the incantation is successfully completed, the caster gains the power to cross their hands in front of their chest once and call “Long Imprison to Self by Force.” to lock themself into a prison of power, ideally protecting themselves from harm. At any time after imprisoning themself, the Cleric can call “Cure Imprisoned” to drop the Fortress. The ability to put up the Fortress will only last until the completion of the next Long Rest once the spell is cast.


Heal [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Heal 3 by Divine”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Divine                                        Effect: Heal


The caster heals the target of the spell for 3 Life Points. This spell also adds 2 points to the Cleric’s Healing Touch pool or 3 if they have Peacecaster.


Enhancement: Adept Spell-Slot: Heals for 10.
Enhancement: Greater Spell-Slot: Heals for 20.


Heretics’ Brand [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “3 Divine” or “Wounding 3 [to Devotion] by Divine”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Divine                                        Effect: Damage, Wounding


Tapping into the depths of their Devotion,  the caster pulls forth a spark of power to charge two Spell-Packets to harm others, especially followers of other Devotions. The caster may add the Qualifier “to [Devotion]” and make the Call “Wounding 3 [to Devotion]”.


Intercession [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Grant 4 Healing Touch Pool to Cleric By Divine”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: Divine                                        Effect: Grant Healing Touch Pool


With this Spell, the caster can Grant 4 points to a Cleric’s Healing Touch Pool if they have one. Peacecaster increases all these numbers by one.


Enhancement: Adept Spell-Slot: It instead adds 9 points.
Enhancement: Greater Spell-Slot: It instead adds 15 points 
Blade Guard [Novice]

Target: Individual, Self                                Duration: Until Used
Delivery: Touch, None                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Divine                                        Effect: Grant Protect, Grant Barrier


This Spell grants a single Protection from Weapons. The Cleric also gains 3 points of Barrier themselves on casting this spell.


Prayer of Rest [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Grant Power: Holy Rest”, Explanation 
Target: Individual (Other)                        Duration: Until Used
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: [Devotion Accent]                        Effect: Grant Power


This spell can only be cast out of combat on a willing target and the Cleric cannot cast it on themself, nor can they cast it on someone they believe already has the Holy Rest Power. Once the Incantation and explanation are complete, the recipient is granted the Holy Rest Power below: 


Holy Rest
Call: “Heal 1 by [Devotion Accent]”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                 Refresh: None
Accent: [Devotion Accent]                        Effect: Heal


After the character has been Dying for at least a Slow Count of 120, they can Heal themself for 1 Life Point even if unconscious or Dying. 


Note: This Power can be used at any time after the user has been Dying for at least 120 seconds. The character will have the Power until it is used.
	



Rekindle [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Revive to 5th level or Lower by Divine” or “Revive to [Devotion] by Divine”
Target: Individual (Dead)                        Duration: Instantaneous        
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: Divine                                        Effect: Revive


This spell will return the target to life with 1 Life Point if they are 5th level or lower. If the spell fails because the target is not fifth level or lower, the spell immediately Refreshes.


Alternatively, the Spell can be used to bring a character back to life, with 1 Life Point, as long as they share the same Devotion as the Cleric.


Note: The Cleric can only cast the version of this spell that returns an individual that shares their Devotion once per Long Rest.

Spirit Speak [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Short Root to Spirit by Mind”, “[Name or Description] Short Obey to Spirit by Mind: Converse with me for at least one minute. You are not required to answer questions.”, “[Name or Description] Cure Root to Spirit”
Target: Individual Spirit                                Duration: Short, Instantaneous
Delivery: Spell-Packet, Verbal                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Root, Obey


The Cleric can require a Spirit to stop and talk with them. The Spirit is not required to answer questions. When the Cleric is done with their conversation, they must call “[Name or Description] Cure Root to Spirit” to end the spell and release the Spirit. Until the Spirit is released, the Cleric cannot cast additional spells.
Cleric Adept Spells
Cleric Adept Incantation


“I call my faith: divine and right, I wield and shape creation's light,
With fervent plea and ardent zeal, I summon strength for woe and weal…”






Bless Shield [Adept]

Target: Individual                                Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Resistance


Bless Shield gives one shield Wielded by the target of the Spell resistance to Wounding Effects. The wielder of the shield should call “Resist” when the shield is hit with such an Effect, and should take one point of damage. This resistance lasts only until the wielder completes a Short Rest.


Blessing [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Short Grant +1 Maximum Spike by Divine”, “Grant 3 Barrier by Divine”
Target: Self and up to six Individuals                Duration: Short Rest, Instantaneous 
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: Divine                                        Effect: Grant Maximum Spikes, Grant Barrier


This spell can be cast on the caster and up to six people who are within Touch range of the caster and can only be cast out of combat. This spell can be cast one of two ways: offensive or defensive. An offensive Bless will grant each beneficiary an additional Maximum Spike. A defensive Bless will grant each target three points of Barrier. 


Cleanse [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Cure all Conditions By Divine”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: Divine                                        Effect: Cure


The Cleric ends all Conditions on the character, including those that might be beneficial to the target. Casting this spell will add three points to the Cleric’s Healing Touch pool if they have one. If the caster has the Peacecaster skill, instead the caster will add four points into their Healing Touch pool if they have one.


Disrupt [Adept]

Target: Undead Individuals                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Voice                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: Divine                                        Effect: Damage


A powerful pulse of energy comes from the Cleric, washing over all nearby Undead. 


Enhancement: Greater Spell-Slot: The Called damage is 5 points. 


Divine Armor [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Long Grant 4 Summoned Armor by Divine”, “Grant Protect vs Melee” “Long Grant +2 Maximum Physical Armor by Divine”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Long Rest, Until Used
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: Divine                                        Effect: Grant Summoned Armor, Grant Maximum Physical Armor, Grant Protect


The caster grants themself 4 Summoned Armor Points. This is considered a new “suit” or armor and is not stackable with other sources of Summoned Armor. 


If instead of granting Summoned Armor the Cleric is wearing Physical Armor, this Spell instead grants the Cleric +2 Maximum Armor.

Either way, this spell also grants a single Protect vs Melee Weapons to the Cleric. The recipient should call out “Protect” to Counter a melee weapon attack.


Flame of Faith [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Wounding 10 to Undead by Divine”
Target: Undead        Individual                        Duration: Instantaneous        
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Divine                                        Effect: Wounding


There are few spells as good for destroying the undead as Flame of Faith: by summoning righteous fire, the Cleric causes 10 points of damage to Undead. 


Enhancement: Spend 3 Healing Touch & a Spike: Charge an additional Spell-Packet.


Gift of Life [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Revive by Divine”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous        
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: Divine                                        Effect: Revive


This powerful spell washes the target in life-energy. If the target is a corpse, it will bring it back to the living with 1 Life Point. 


This spell can only be used to bring one corpse back from the dead once per Long Rest. However, If a Power refreshes a Long Rest ability, it can target the “once per Long Rest” limitation of this spell, allowing one more individual to be brought back to life by this spell. 


If Gift of Life is used on an Undead, it will cause the creature to immediately discorporate and take Spirit Form. 


Judgment [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Quick Imprison by Mind”, “[Name or Description] Heal 2 to Imprisoned by Divine”, “[Name or Description] 2 to Imprisoned by Divine”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Quick 100
Delivery: Spell-Packet, Verbal                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: Mind, Divine                                Effect: Imprison, Heal, Damage


Wrapping their target in iron bands of willpower, the Cleric captures a creature and locks it away. While the creature is Imprisoned, the Cleric may charge harming or healing powers to use on that creature. After focusing for a Quick 30, the Cleric may either do “[Name or Description] 2 to Imprisoned by Divine” as a verbal, or may do “[Name or Description] Heal 2 to Imprisoned by Divine” as a verbal. This may be done as often as desired until the target ceases to be Imprisoned by this spell. 


Note: If the caster has the Peacecaster skill, instead the Call becomes “[Name or Description] Heal 3 to Imprisoned by Divine”, and if the caster has the Warcaster skill, instead the Call becomes “[Name or Description] 3 to Imprisoned by Divine.” This is an exception to normal Peacecasting / Warcasting rules. 
Minor Circle [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Grant Immunity”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Short Rest, Special
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Immunity
        
This spell must be cast while inside a white cord, rope, or the like that is no longer than 10 feet long that has been laid down to make a continuous shape (usually a circle). At the completion of the Incantation, the lain rope cannot be purposefully moved by anyone other than the caster, and anyone who attempts to do so should be informed by use of “Instruction: You may not move the rope.” or similar. This does not prevent people from crossing the threshold of the cord, rope, or the like. 


Should the rope be moved purposefully by the caster, the spell ends. This should be communicated to others trying to touch or manipulate it. As long as the caster has both feet completely inside the circle, they have immunity to all Effects and Damage.


If the caster uses a Power on anyone who has both feet entirely outside the Circle, causes damage to another creature, or if the caster steps out of the Circle, the immunity granted by the Circle is removed. In addition, they must pick up the circle and end the Spell. The Circle can be Dispelled by Dispel Magic and similar magic.


Note: The caster may only have one Circle active at any given time, regardless of origin. 


Punish [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “8 Divine” or “Wounding 8 by Divine to [Devotion]”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Divine                                        Effect: Wounding


Tapping into the depths of their Devotion, the caster punishes those of other Devotions. The caster may add the Qualifier “to [Devotion]” and make the Call “Wounding 8 [to Devotion]”, instead, but the Cleric may not include their own Devotion as the Qualifier.


Enhancement: Greater Spell-Slot: The damage becomes “12 Divine” and “Wounding 12 to [Devotion] by Divine” instead.


Retributive Protection [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Counter, Retributive Protection”, “[Name or Description] Piercing 2 by Divine”
Target: Self, Individual                                Duration: Until Used, Instantaneous
Delivery: None, Verbal                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Divine                                        Effect: Counter, Piercing


This spell allows the Cleric to protect themself from damage, giving two “Retributive Protection” Counters, which Prevent two damaging attacks of any kind. In order to prime the retributive part of this spell, the Cleric must point at a creature and say “I call my faith to warn you against harming me.” Only the last creature that they warned will be a valid target for retribution. 


When the Cleric uses a Counter defense from this spell to stop damage from the last warned creature, they may call out a Verbal Effect against that attacker: “[Name or Description], Piercing 2 by Divine.” This can be done once for each Counter defense that is used. This spell ends early if the Cleric causes harm to another with Damage or Effects other than the Damage from this spell.


Safe Return [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Grant Insubstantial” “Cure Insubstantial”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Special
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Insubstantial, Cure


After completing the incantation, the caster will become Insubstantial. They can then walk directly to a nearby area (within 10 minutes walk) which they have previously been to, that is consecrated or holy to their Devotion.


When they arrive and enter they lose their insubstantial Condition and should call “Cure Insubstantial.” 


If they are unable to enter the area (if it is proofed against insubstantial creatures, for example) and have no other viable options, they must immediately call the “Cure Insubstantial” and end the spell, leaving them outside the area. 


Salvation [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “By My Voice, Heal 1 to Dying by Divine”
Target: Individuals                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Voice                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: Divine                                        Effect: Heal


This spell will save all those who might be dying, bringing them to 1 Life Point. 


Speak With The Dead [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “[Name or Description] Discern to Corpse by Divine: [Yes or No Question]”
Target: Individual (Dead)                                Duration: Special        
Delivery: Verbal                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: Divine                                        Effect: Discern


Out of combat, after completing the incantation while within Touch range of a corpse, the Cleric can cause the corpse to temporarily animate and truthfully answer questions posed by the Cleric. The Cleric may ask up to three questions. 


The questions posed must be yes or no questions and the call for each question is “Discern to Corpse by Divine: [Yes/No question]”. Once a corpse has been asked all of the allotted questions, the Cleric may not cast this spell on that same corpse again.


The spell will end immediately if the Cleric leaves Touch range of the corpse, speaks for any reason other than to ask more Speak With Dead questions of the corpse, or enters combat.


Enhancement: Greater Spell-Slot: The Cleric may ask up to five questions.. 


Spell-Warding [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Grant Protect vs Packets by Divine”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Until Used
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Divine                                        Effect: Grant Protect


When cast the spell allows the Cleric to charge two Spell-Packets with the ability to Protect individuals from attacks from Packets.


        Druid: Base Class
Divine Spell-Casting Class


Description
The Druid is a formidable warrior, summoning magic found deep within the wilds of nature; weaving spells to enhance their weapons, or unleashing claws as the need arises. In addition, Druids are able to communicate with the animals and plants that inhabit the area. Through these interactions, they might track their quarry by relying on the many eyes and ears of the forests, or learn of imminent attacks upon the lands they call home.


Druids place nature at the center of their beliefs, drawing their spells and abilities from a mystical bond with the earth itself. Most druids worship the umbrella Devotion of nature itself (see Druidism), although it has been known that some druids worship other Divine Beings, and use their connection with the natural world as a method of prayer in and of itself.


Fiercely protective of the lands they have chosen to protect, few can withstand the fury of an enraged Druid: fearsome claws, vines that bind and tear, and elemental blasts of fire and ice make the Druid a force to be reckoned with.
Starting Skills
               * Natural Survival: Basic Martial Weapons (1), Profession - Apprentice (1), and either Forage I (3) or Scavenge I (3)
               * The Song of the Land: Basic Faith (4)
               * Budding Wisdom - Choose one of the following:
               * Extended Capacity - Novice (3), Lore: Nature (2)
               * Short Weapons (3), Two-Weapon Style (2)
               * Peacecaster (3), Basic Medicine (2)
Multiclass Skills
               * The Voice of the Forest: Basic Faith (4)


Note: If the character selects an option and already has the maximum amount of any of the selected skills, the costs for those skills will be granted as free BP instead.
