Note: Life Points and Maximum Spikes can be found in the Level Progression Table in the Wellspring Core Rules.
Spells Known
The Druid has a number of Spells that they can choose from.  Each time the character gains a Druid level, they can remove one Spell from the list and replace it with another. There are no limits on what tier Spell replaces the removed Spell (meaning a Novice Spell could be removed and replaced with a Greater Spell). 
Forms
The Druid has access to a number of Form Spells that allow them to change into various special combat-forms. These are marked with the [Form] tag. The Druid may only ever be in one Form at a time. In addition to their listed duration, Forms will go down if the character is killed, struck with a Dispel Magic, or the Druid chooses for the Form to drop. These forms do not need to be physically represented, but they may be represented by the player by adding costuming elements representing the form they have taken.


There are three Forms that the Base Druid can take. These are: Hulking Bear, which specializes in resisting damage and staying alive; Striking Serpent, which wants to be in melee, causing damage and weapon-channeled Effects; and Hunting Panther, which specializes in evasion and devastating “from behind” attacks much like a Rogue.

No Form

Some Spells have a line that states “Enhancement: No Form: [Effect]. These Enhancements are only active when the Druid has not been under the effects of a Form Spell this event. NOTE: These Enhancements can only be applied if the character’s Druid level is their highest (or tied for highest) class level.
Attacking From Behind
A number of Druid Powers reference attacking “from behind.” In order to count as an attack from behind, the Druid must have one foot on the back side of a line created by the target’s shoulders and must strike with a hand that is also behind that line. These rules include thrown weapon attacks, and the check for these requirements occurs when the call is made, which should be as the strike lands. “From behind” Powers will always be usable against targets that are helpless or willing. An attack that must come “from behind” which doesn’t for some reason, becomes a normal attack (for example: the opponent turns, the attack hits the wrong target, etc.). In this case it is up to the Druid to let the target know that they should take the normal uncalled damage of one.
Base Incantation
“I foster life that blooms in Spring, from budding earth and insect sting. 
I bask in Summer's endless days, as stalking beast and fleeing prey. 
I burn like every fallen leaf, from Autumn fires’ ashen grief. 
I channel chill and dying light, from howling storm and Winter’s bite…”
Druid Innate Powers


Commune with Nature [Innate]
Requirement: Druid Level 1
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Refresh


The Druid can spend a Short Rest in meditation, feeling the strength of nature all around them, and drawing from its power and majesty.
 
The Druid can perform this special meditation and refresh all of their expended Druid spell-slots. If the Druid has used any other Innate Power from another class to refresh their Divine spell slots this long rest they may not use Commune with Nature.


Additional benefits come if this meditation is done in an area of special natural power, sometimes called a Natural Locus. Doing so, the Druid gains three additional Divine Novice-tier Spell-Slots. These Spell-Slots last until the Druid completes a Long Rest. Additional benefits may be possible (see the Skills, Perks, and Flaws document for more details). 
Nature’s Understanding [Innate]
Requirement: Druid Level 2
Incantation: Standard
Call: “[Name or Description] Discern [to Animal/Beast] [Question] by Mind”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Special
Delivery: Verbal                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Discern


This power allows the character to determine if a targeted creature is a beast of nature, and its intent. The power lasts until the Druid moves their right foot or takes a Short Rest, whichever comes first. Until it ends, the Druid can ask any of the following questions as often as desired of any number of targets that they can see, but may only ask each target each question once.


               * Do you have the Animal Type?
               * What kind of Animal are you?
               * Why are you here?
               * What are you feeling?
               * Do you mean me harm?
               * What is one of your Vulnerabilities?
Message of the Flowers [Innate]
Requirement: Druid Level 3
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Bloom or Night Prize Patch                 Duration: Event
Delivery: None                                          Refresh: Immediate 


The Druid may leave a note locked with a Black Key marker and "Has Foraging Skill" on it at any Bloom or Night Prize Patch.
Earth’s Vitality [Innate]
Requirement: Druid Level 5
Incantation: None
Call: “Long Grant +1 Maximum Life Point”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Long Rest
Delivery: None                                          Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Maximum Life Points


The Druid gains Long Grant +1 Maximum Life Points after spending at least 5 minutes communing with Nature outside. They may do this while taking a nature walk, meditating in their Grove, or other such activities.
Natural Cycle [Innate]
Requirement: Druid Level 9
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: None                                         Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                          Refresh: Event


Once per Event, after the Druid believes they have killed a creature (including and most often through a successful Deathblow) they remove the restriction on their ability to Revive.  This restriction to return someone to life in a particular Long Rest is common among spells that have the Revive effect. This power will only lift the restriction once, and one it has does so, will not do so again.


If the Druid knows the target was not killed this power fails to refresh their ability to Revive another, however, the power is not wasted.


Note: This power does not refresh the Spell-Slot used to Revive, it just removes the restriction the spell or power might have against doing so.
Grove [Innate]
Requirement: Druid Level 10
Incantation: RP: Sanctification Rite of Druid’s Creation
Call: None
Target: One Location                                Duration: Event
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Imbue


The Druid can create a special area that is their grove. To do this, the Druid should do a small sanctification rite of their choosing, role-playing creating a connection with the grove for at least a minute. The grove will last until the beginning of the next Event (even if the Druid is not in attendance). The area must either be marked off with a border of green material (rope, engineering tape, etc.) not to exceed 100 ft long, or be an obvious area (such as an actual clearing). 


The area may not have any permanent structures in it but may overlap with Druid groves that already exist, as long as the other Grove’s owner consents. The area should have some natural significance or beauty; just putting it on the lawn in front of the Tavern may cause Staff to claim the Grove is defunct. The Druid must provide an easily visible item card for the grove, indicating that it is a special place, its effects, the name of the Druid who created it, and any others presently attuned to it, which must be posted somewhere easily visible in the center of the grove itself.


The grove has a number of properties:


               * The plants and vegetation there grow particularly well, as if perfectly watered with plenty of sunlight.
               * Plants and small animals within the grove are cheered, and generally feel safe and happy.
               * Plants and small animals within the grove are unusually hardy and difficult to be blighted, diseased, or harmed by outside magic.
               * The area is considered a Natural Locus, and should be conspicuously noted as such on the card.
               * Once ever, when the Druid discorporates, they may Resurrect with 1 Life Point at their Grove instead of moving to the Spirit River.
               * While within the Grove, the Druid may deliver the Cure effect at-will via Touch. If this is used to cure the Taint condition, this ability is suppressed until the druid takes a Short Rest.
Druid Class Skills


Red in Tooth and Claw [Class] - 2 BP
Requirement: Druid Level 1
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None


Early in every Druid’s life comes the time in which they must defend themselves, whether from the natural denizens of forest, or the “civilized” of the cities. Many learn that always having a means of defense at a moment’s notice can mean the difference between life and death. The Druid may manifest up to two Short Claws as Natural weapons and can use them proficiently. These claws should be no longer than 36” long. 
Favored Form [Class] - 4 BP
Requirement: Druid Level 2
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Long Rest


The character chooses one of the three Forms (Hulking Bear, Hunting Panther, Striking Serpent) and can cast one Form Spell that grants that Form without expending a Spell-Slot.
Find the Path [Class] - 1 BP
Requirement: Druid Level 2
Incantation: “By dawning growth that blooms in Spring I ask the forest for a path forward.”
Call: “Short Grant Tracking to Self”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Tracking


Upon using this power, the character may ask the trees what they have seen, and follow specific paths. They can see and follow green flags marked with a white circle (called a “Halo”) until they have finished a Short Rest. This Power must be cast while within Touch range of a tree of any size, but its benefits may be used anywhere.


If the Druid later purchases the Tracking Skill, they may refund this skill.
Hunter [Class] - 1 BP
Requirement: Druid Level 2
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None


The Druid gains the Projectile Weapon Skill, but does not gain proficiency with crossbows. Anything that lists Projectile Weapon Skill as a prerequisite works normally.
Wyvern’s Tooth and Claw [Class] - 8 BP
Requirement: Druid Level 3, Red in Tooth and Claw
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None


The Druid can manifest up to two Natural Long Weapons or one Natural Great Weapon proficiently.


Cantrip Scholar [Class] - 4 BP
Requirement: Druid Level 6


The Druid learns an additional Cantrip from the Druid Class Powers or Cantrips.
Flexible Form [Class] - 6 BP
Requirement: Druid Level 6, Favored Form
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Long Rest


Once per Long Rest, the Druid can cast one Form Spell from their Favored Form without expending a Spell-Slot and without the “out-of-combat” restriction. This is often used to pop out of the Form, cast other Spells, and then dive right back in.
Druid Cantrips
Druid Cantrip Incantation


 “I foster life that blooms in Spring…”
Antitoxin [Cantrip] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Grant Protect vs Poison”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Until Used
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Focus Quick 100
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Protect


The Druid throws calls on nature to protect themselves from poisons. This gives the caster one Protect from Poison.
Cancel [Cantrip] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Immediate
Accent: None                                        Effect: Refresh


This minor cantrip takes the power out of a Spell-Packet (or multiple Spell-Packets) that has already been charged and returns that power to the caster. The Spell-Packet becomes inert, and the caster refreshes the spell-slot that was used to charge the Spell-Packet. If the spell charges more than one Spell-Packet, all the Spell-Packets must still be charged in order to cancel the spell.


Care for the Fallen [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Heal 1 to Dying”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Focus Quick 100
Accent: None                                        Effect: Heal


A simple trick taught by Druids of the past to prevent the transition to death, the caster can Call “Heal 1 to Dying.” This Call cannot be altered.
Enchanted Thorn [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “1”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Immediate
Accent: None                                        Effect: Damage


The Druid is able to create a wooden thorn that can be thrown as a Thrown Weapon. The caster can use any Powers or other abilities (like Spikes) on the thorn as if it were a mundane Thrown Weapon. When the Spell-Packet strikes, however, it will act as a Spell-Packet, not a Weapon and will be defended against appropriately.
Festering Lash [Cantrip] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Disarm [Weapon] by Disease”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Weapon                                Refresh: Focus Quick 100
Accent: Disease                                        Effect: Disarm


By calling the toxic wrath of nature the Druid can lash out, disarming foes that seek to harm them. Unlike other Effects, if no duration is called, the duration of Disarm is Instantaneous, and the item may be picked up immediately upon hitting the ground. 
Hide [Cantrip] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Grant 1 Barrier”
Target:        Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Immediate
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Barrier


As long as the Druid has no points of Barrier, they can grant themself one point of Barrier.


Enhancement: No Form: Grants 2 Barrier instead of one.
Life Tap [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Heal [X] to Living”
Target: Individual (Other)                         Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Immediate
Accent: None                                        Effect: Heal


The Druid can heal damage to an Individual by drawing life from nearby flora, represented by their Life Tap pool. This Cantrip works normally if the Druid is outside. If the character is inside, they lose double points from their Life Tap pool for the same effect (to heal for 1 they would spend 2 Life Tap Points) unless they are touching a Plant creature (Including a character under the effects of Arboreal Transformation).


The Life Tap pool is a number representing how much the Druid can heal for that Long Rest. Every time this Cantrip is used, the Druid can heal another individual up the Druid's Class-Level, subtracting the healing done from the Life Tap Pool.


The Life Tap Pool resets to twice the Druid's Class-Level at the end of a Long Rest.
Nature’s Rebuke [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “1 [Poison or Disease]”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Immediate
Accent: Poison, Disease                                Effect: Damage


The Druid is able to release a small bit of contagion or poison to harm their foes.


Enhancement: No Form: Charges 2 additional Spell-Packets.
Nettles and Barbs [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “2 Poison”, “Wounding 2 by Poison”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Weapon                                Refresh: Immediate
Accent: Poison                                        Effect: Damage


The Druid can make one attack with a Claw weapon that does “2 Poison”. If they expend a Spike while doing so, the call is altered to “Wounding 2 by Poison.”
Natural Remedies [Cantrip] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Cure [Poison, Disease, or Taint]”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                         Refresh: Immediate
Accent: None                                        Effect: Cure


From the deep lore of the woods, Druids have found ways to cure any number of poisons, toxins, and venoms with ease. Out of combat, the Druid may incant, and then call “Cure [Poison, Disease, or Taint].”


Enhancement: No Form: Can be cast in combat with a Quick 30 refresh.
Druid Novice Form Spells
Druid Novice Incantation


“I foster life that blooms in Spring, from budding earth and insect sting…”
Lesser Form of the Hulking Bear [Novice] [Form]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Grant Lesser Form of the Hulking Bear to Self”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Power


This Spell can only be cast while out of combat. The Druid is inhabited by the essence of the Bear Spirit. Powerful and capable of taking enormous punishment, the Bear can ride any front line. While in the Lesser Form of the Hulking Bear, the character cannot cast Spells of any kind or use physical or summoned armor, but can activate the Powers of items and can cast the Hide Cantrip, but no others. The Druid has the following benefits:


               * +1 Maximum Life Points.
               * 4 points of Natural Armor.
               * May manifest and Wield up to two Natural Short weapons proficiently.

The Bear has the following additional abilities:


                  * Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to Counter a damaging Weapon attack. The Call is “Counter, Bearhide!”.
                  * Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to do a Weapon attack that does “Quick Provoke [No Accent/by Force]”
                  * Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to Heal themself for 3 Life Points. The Call is “Heal 3”.
Lesser Form of the Striking Serpent [Novice] [Form]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Grant Lesser Form of the Striking Serpent to Self”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Power


This Spell can only be cast while out of combat. The Druid is inhabited by the essence of the venomous snake, giving them speed and poisonous power. The serpent is a predator, striking hard and cruel. While in the Lesser Form of the Striking Serpent, the character cannot cast Spells of any kind, but can activate the Powers of items and can cast the Nettles and Barbs Cantrip, but no others. The Druid has the following benefits:


                  * +1 Base Spike Damage.
                  * May manifest and Wield up to two Natural Short weapons proficiently.
                  * May add the “Poison” Accent to any Claw Weapon attack that does not have an Accent.

The Serpent has the following additional abilities:


                     * Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to make a Weapon attack that does “Wounding 3 by Poison”
                     * Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to make a Verbal attack that does “[Name or Description] Quick Slow by Fear”
                     * Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to make a Weapon attack that does “Quick Disable [Left/Right Arm] by Poison”
Lesser Form of the Hunting Panther [Novice] [Form]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Grant Lesser Form of the Hunting Panther to Self”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Power


This Spell can only be cast while out of combat. The Druid is inhabited by the essence of the panther, the perfect predator. The panther is darkness and shadow, slipping in for the kill. While in the Lesser Form of the Hunting Panther, the character cannot cast Spells of any kind, but can activate the Powers of items and can cast the Enchanted Thorn Cantrip, but no others. The Druid has the following benefits:


                     * +1 Base Spike Damage.
                     * May manifest and Wield up to two Natural Short weapons proficiently.
                     * May Counter the Damage and Effects caused by a fall by Sacrificing 2 Life Points and Calling “Counter, Catfall”.

The Panther has the following additional abilities:


                        * Expend a Spell-Slot to make a Weapon attack from behind that does 5 per Tier expended.
                        * Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to make a Weapon attack that does “Short Slow by Agony”
                        * Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to make a Weapon attack that does “Quick Root”
Druid Novice Spells


Arboreal Transformation [Novice] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: "Grant Transformation", “Cure Transformation”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Special, Long Rest
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Power


While this spell is active, and the Druid has their feet planted, the Druid gains the Plant Type and may count for any tree size requirements for spells for the caster and other character’s requirements. 


Enhancement: No Form: The Druid may move as if Slowed without ending this spell in combat, or move normally outside of combat.
Balm [Novice] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Heal 3” or “Cure [Condition]”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Heal, Cure


The Druid heals the target of the spell for 3 Life Points or Cures the target of one Condition.


Enhancement: No Form: The Druid may heal and Cure the target.
Barkskin [Novice] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Long Grant 2 Natural Armor”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Long Rest
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Natural Armor


Pulling the protection of the forest around them, the Druid hardens their skin with bark, giving them two points of Natural Armor.
Blessing of the Oak [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Grant Protect vs Poison and Disease”
Target: Individual                                 Duration: Until Used
Delivery: Touch                                         Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Protect


The Druid may bestow the blessings of the mighty oak to bolster their allies' fortitude granting a Protect against Poison and Disease. 


Additionally, the caster gains 3 points to their Life Tap pool if the Druid has not been under the effects of a Form spell this event.


Enhancement: Spike: The Druid may expend a Spike to add one additional Target.
Bounty of the Land [Novice] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Counter, Land’s Bounty”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Long Rest
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Counter


The Druid knows how to take from the land without ruining its natural beauty or despoiling its resources permanently. In return for this treatment, the land is kinder to the Druid. Until the Druid's next Long Rest, the next time the Druid would suffer negative consequences of Gathering, the character may Counter those consequences for themself.


The character can Counter one negative effect of Scavenging a creature, Prospecting, or Foraging as long as it happens before they complete a Long Rest. The appropriate Call for preventing this negative effect is “Counter, Land's Bounty.”


Enhancement: No Form: In addition to the above, once during the duration of the Spell, the Druid may put back a card and re-draw from the deck when Prospecting.
Corrosive Bolt [Novice] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Wounding 3 by Acid”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Acid                                        Effect: Wounding


Casting this spell, the Druid infuses materia with the destructive power of Acid. 


Enhancement: No Form: The Druid gains 1 additional charge.
Ensnare [Novice] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Short Root by Ice”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Ice                                        Effect: Root


When this spell is cast, the Druid calls the icy tendrils of the earth to reach up and grab a hold of their enemy, charging two Spell-Packets with “Short Root by Ice.”


Enhancement: Adept Spell-Slot: The Delivery becomes Verbal and the Call is “[Name or Description] Short Root by Ice”.
Entangling Vines [Novice] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “[Name or Description] Quick Disable [Left/Right Leg] by Poison”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Quick 100
Delivery: Verbal                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: Poison                                        Effect: Disable


Venomous thorns reach up from the underbrush, or through the cracks in the stones, striking at the legs and feet of the Druid’s enemies to disable their foe’s leg.
Eyes of the Crow [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: Grant Eyes of the Crow
Target: Self                                        Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Power


With this Spell up, Instead of searching a target, the Druid can quickly size up a helpless or willing creature and determine where to search. Please note that, like searching, this should not include any actual touching without express pre-negotiated consent. 


The caster can search instantaneously, by calling “Instruction: Give me your loot”, rather than searching for the Quick 100. Although this can be done any number of times while the Spell is active, the caster must wait until they either have the Loot, or are told there is none, before it can be used again.
Frostbite [Novice] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Short Slow by Ice”
Target: Individual(s)                                Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Ice                                        Effect: Slow


The Druid is able to call forth the freezing cold of winter to hinder their enemy. After casting they may throw three Spell-Packets for “Short Slow by Ice.”
Nature’s Embrace [Novice] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Long Obfuscate vs Expose”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Long Rest
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Obfuscate


The Druid is able to cloak themselves from those that wish to find them. By calling for nature’s aid this spell will protect them from the Expose effect. While touching a tree, laying in grass, or cloaked in darkness (such as night time or deep shadow), the Druid is Obfuscated vs Expose. 
Purifying Flame [Novice] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Cure [Condition] and 1 by Flame”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: Flame                                        Effect: Cure, Damage


When casting this spell, the Druid infuses their touch with the burning purity of the sun. They may call “Cure [Condition] and 1 by Flame”. The Druid may do this up to three times to the same Individual consecutively, each one Curing an additional Condition, but causing additional damage.


Enhancement: No Form: The 3 charges no longer have to target the same individual.
Quell Beast [Novice] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “[Name or Description] Short Charm to Animal by Mind”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Verbal                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Charm


The Druid is at home with the creatures of the forest, and is able to soothe even the most dangerous of natural beasts. They may make a verbal call to a creature known to be an Animal and call “[Name or Description] Short Charm to Animal by Mind.”


Enhancement: Expend a Spike: The Call can be changed to “[Name or Description] Short Sleep to Animal by Mind.”
Sap of Life [Novice] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “[Name or Description] 1 Final”, “Grant [X] Life Tap Pool”
Target: Individuals (up to eight and the caster)        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Damage, Grant Life Tap Pool


This Spell can be cast on up to eight other individuals (and the Druid), but must be cast out-of-combat. The Druid must explain that each person in the casting will lose one Life Point if they consent. Once the Incantation is complete, the Druid asks each target (other than themself) in turn whether they are willing to give up life for this Spell. This can be “Are you willing to sacrifice your blood for the good of the wilds?” or “May I have a sip of you?” or any similar question. 


As each one says they consent, the Druid will say “[Name or Description] 1 Final”. For each Life Point given up, the Druid adds 1 point to their Life Tap Pool. If they Sacrifice a Life Point themself, they add 2 more. This can add up to 10 points back into the Druid’s Life Tap Pool.
Shape Metal [Novice] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Short Imbue Touch with [Mend 4]”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Imbue


The Druid can give another the power to shape metal, molding armor over itself to repair rents and tears. The Druid must sacrifice an Ingot during the casting of the Spell, infusing it into the target. The target will be able to do a Mend 4 once by Touch. 
Submerge [Novice] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Short Silence by Ice”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Ice                                        Effect: Silence


This spell charges a Spell-Packet to curse the victim with lungs full of freezing water causing “Short Silence by Ice.”
Druid Adept Form Spells
Druid Adept Incantation


“I foster life that blooms in Spring, from budding earth and insect sting.
By Summer’s heat in brightest day, from stalking beast and fleeing prey…”


Form of the Hulking Bear [Adept] [Form]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Grant Form of the Hulking Bear to Self”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Power


This Spell can only be cast while out of combat. The Druid is inhabited by the essence of the Bear Spirit. Powerful and capable of taking enormous punishment, the Bear can ride any front line. While in the Form of the Hulking Bear, the character cannot cast Spells of any kind or use physical or summoned armor, but can activate the Powers of items and can cast the Hide Cantrip, but no others. The Druid has the following benefits:


                        * +3 Life Points
                        * 5 points of Natural Armor
                        * May manifest and Wield up to two Natural Short or Long weapons proficiently.
                        * May add the “Force” Accent to any Claw Weapon attack that does not have an Accent.

The Bear has the following additional abilities:


                           * Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to Counter a damaging Weapon attack. The Call is “Counter: Bearhide!”.
                           * Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to Heal themself for 3 Life Points. The Call is “Heal 3”.
                           * Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to do a Weapon attack that does “Quick Provoke [No Accent/by Force]”
                           * Expend an Adept Spell-Slot to Grant themself +1 Bonus Spike Damage until they leave the Form.
                           * Expend an Adept Spell-Slot to Cure themself of one Condition. The Call for this is "Cure [Condition]" and the Bear can do this while unable to speak, but not while unable to use Powers. 
Form of the Striking Serpent [Adept] [Form]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Grant Form of the Striking Serpent to Self”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Power


This Spell can only be cast while out of combat. The Druid is inhabited by the essence of the venomous snake, giving them speed and poisonous power. The serpent is a predator, striking hard and cruel. While in the Form of the Striking Serpent, the character cannot cast Spells of any kind, but can activate the Powers of items and can cast the Nettles and Barbs Cantrip, but no others. The Druid has the following benefits:


                           * +1 Base Spike Damage.
                           * +2 Base Maximum Spikes.
                           * May manifest and Wield up to two Natural Short weapons proficiently.
                           * May add the Serpent Accents to any Claw Weapon attack that does not have an Accent.
                           * Serpent Accents: [Poison, Fear]

The Serpent has the following additional abilities:


                              * Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to make a Weapon attack that does “Wounding 4 by [Serpent Accent].”
                              * Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to make a Verbal attack that does “Quick Slow by [Serpent Accent].”
                              * Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to make a Weapon attack that does “Quick Disable [Arm] by [Serpent Accent].”
                              * Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to Refresh one Spike by Calling “Refresh Spike.”
                              * Expend an Adept Spell-Slot to do a Weapon attack that does “Taint by [Serpent Accent].”
                              * Expend an Adept Spell-Slot to do a Weapon attack that does “Quick Drain by [Serpent Accent].”
Form of the Hunting Panther [Adept] [Form]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Grant Form of the Hunting Panther to Self”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Power


This Spell can only be cast while out of combat. The Druid is inhabited by the essence of the panther, the perfect predator. The panther is darkness and shadow, slipping in for the kill. While in the Form of the Hunting Panther, the character cannot cast Spells of any kind, but can activate the Powers of items and can cast the Enchanted Thorn Cantrip, but no others. The Druid has the following benefits:


                              * +2 Base Spike Damage.
                              * May manifest and Wield up to two Natural Short weapons proficiently.
                              * May Counter the Damage and Effects caused by a fall by sacrificing 1 Life Point and Calling “Counter, Catfall.”
                              * Gain the benefits of the Adept Druid Spell Eyes of the Crow.
                              * May do a Weapon attack at-will that does “Death to Helpless.”
                              * May add the Panther Accents to any Claw Weapon attack Accent or substitute another existing accent on a Claw Weapon attack.
                              * Panther Accents: [Disease, Agony]


The Panther has the following additional abilities:


                              * Expend a Spell-Slot to make a Weapon attack from behind that does 5 per Tier expended.
                              * Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to make a Weapon attack that does “Quick Slow by [Panther Accent]”
                              * Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to make a Weapon attack that does “Quick Root”
                              * Expend an Adept Spell-Slot to do a Weapon attack that does “Silence by [Panther Accent]”
                              * Expend an Adept Spell-Slot to Counter a Weapon or Packet attack by Calling “Counter, Dodge”
Druid Adept Spells


Curse the Despoiler [Adept] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Long Obey by Fear: Do not [Prospect/Forage/Scavenge] without my permission.”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Long Rest
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Fear                                        Effect: Grant Obey


Woe unto the enemy of the Druid who takes from the land in a way unsustainable. The Druid may charge a Spell-Packet with a powerful curse that prevents the individual from gathering from the land and using either their Prospecting, Foraging, or Scavenging skill until the next Long Rest. During the Call, the Druid may prevent the other from using any or all of the Gathering Skills.
Dart Frog’s Skin [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Counter, Poison Skin” and “[Name or Description] 4 Poison”
Target: Self, Individual                                Duration: Short Rest, Instantaneous
Delivery: None, Verbal                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Counter


The Druid gains three Counters against melee attacks. Each time the Counter is used, the Druid may call a Verbal “4 Poison” against the attacker. The skin is delicate, however, and will slough away and prematurely end the Spell if the caster throws Materia or makes a Weapon attack.
Feral Strength [Adept] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Grant Feral Strength”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Power


The Druid is able to call upon the feral nature of the wild beasts of the wood. Upon casting this spell the Druid will be able to pick up and carry an individual in each hand while running even while holding Natural Weapons (See pickup and carry rules). 


In addition, while the Spell is active, the Druid is considered to have proficiency with Great Weapons and all Shields.
Health of the World [Adept] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Grant [5/10] Life Tap”
Target: Individual, Self                                Duration: Instantaneous, Short Rest
Delivery: Touch, None                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Life Tap


The Druid may cast this spell to add 5 points to their Life Tap pool or the Life Tap pool of another Druid.

Enhancement: No Form: The Druid may choose to add 10 points to their Life Tap pool or the Life Tap pool of another Druid.
Putrid Blade [Adept] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Imbue Weapon with [Short Taint by Disease]”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Imbue


The Druid can summon the powers of rot and decay, infusing them into an individual, tainting them, stopping healing from being effective and possibly killing the victim of the attack.
Phoenix Reborn [Adept] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Revive by Flame”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous 
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: Flame                                        Effect: Revive


Upon casting this spell the Druid must Sacrifice 10 Life Points (or however many they currently have if it is less). 


This powerful spell washes the target in flame and magical life. If the target is a corpse, it will bring it back to the living with 1 Life Point. 


This spell can only be used to bring one corpse back from the dead once per Long Rest. If a power refreshes a Long Rest ability, it can target the “one per Long Rest” limitation of this spell, allowing one more individual to be brought back to life by this spell. 


If used on an Undead, it will cause the creature to immediately discorporate and take Spirit Form.


Enhancement: No Form: The Druid may deliver “Heal to Full by Flame” to the target affected by Revive
Hide of the Rhino [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Long Grant 3 Natural Armor”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Long Rest
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Natural Armor


The Druid is able to tap into some of the deeper energies of the forest to become more bestial in nature. This spell will give the Druid three points of Natural Armor.
Lifethrive [Adept] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “[Name or Description] Heal 8”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Verbal                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Heal


The Druid can cause life all around to infuse an individual, healing them even at a distance. If the Druid is standing in their Grove when the Spell is cast, they are also Granted 4 points into their Life Tap pool. 
Nature’s Blessing [Adept] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Short Grant +1 Maximum Life Points”
Target: Individual (Other)                        Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Maximum Life Points


This spell augments an ally, increasing the life essence within, increasing their Maximum Life Points by one.


If the Druid has not been under the effects of a Form spell this event, they also add 4 points to their Life Tap pool.
Spitting Venom [Adept] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: Short Weakness by Poison”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Poison                                        Effect: Weakness


This Spell infuses two Spell-Packets with highly potent poison: anyone struck will become weakened. 
Spirit Strike [Adept] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Short Imbue Weapon with [6 to Insubstantial]”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Short Rest, Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch, Weapon                        Refresh: Spell, None
Accent: None                                        Effect: Imbue, Damage


The Druid can use this spell to give the power to strike through the veil and harm the incorporeal. The imbued target can inflict one attack that does 6 damage to Insubstantial creatures.


Enhancement: Greater Spell-Slot: The spell can, instead, do “Short Imbue Weapon with [Cure Insubstantial to Insubstantial]”.
Thorns of Ruin [Adept] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Grant Thorns of Ruin”, “Wounding 4”
Target: Self, Individual                                Duration: Short Rest, Instantaneous
Delivery: None, Spell-Packet                        Refresh: Spell, Immediate
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Power, Wounding


Vines with deadly thorns wrap around and extend from the Druid’s hands, ready to infuse materia with deadly power. While this spell is active, the Druid can expend a Spike and Sacrifice a Life Point to call “Thorn of Ruin!” to charge a Spell-Packet with “Wounding 4.”
Sprout Wings [Adept] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Grant Wings”, “Grant Flying”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Short Rest, Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Spell, Quick 100
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Flying


The character sprouts wings and gains the ability to take flight for a short amount of time. The call to take flight is “Grant Flying,” at which point the character gains the flying condition. After taking flight, the character must begin moving at a minimum of a walking pace, taking up to 50 steps, at which point they must land and end the flying condition. If the character ever stops moving, voluntarily or involuntarily, while in flight, they also must end the flying condition. After landing, the character may not take flight again using this ability until they perform a Focus Quick 100. The character may not take any other actions, besides making Defense Calls, while under the flying condition.
Weasel’s Agility [Adept] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Short Grant Weasel’s Agility”, “Counter, Weasel’s Agility”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Counter


The Druid is able to tap into some of the deeper energies of the forest to become more bestial in nature. This Spell will give the Druid the ability to resist the snares and traps that would slow or pin them down. The Druid may spend a Spike to Counter any Root or Slow Effects.






Fighter: Base Class
Martial Class


Description
There are those that choose the path of battle; some are heroes, others are thugs, but all who choose this path live and die by the sword. The Fighter is one such. They are an expert with weapons and in battle, and their skills are specifically tailored to fighting. Of all the paths available to an adventurer, Fighter is the broadest. Whether one is a street-rat who has earned their way by murder and robbery or a valiant warrior serving the causes of justice and light, one can easily fit into the role of the Fighter. For skill in battle and deadly effectiveness, the Fighter is ultimate.


Throughout history there have been hundreds of thousands of fighters. They are the soldiers that stand on the front lines and in the War Councils of every war since the dawn of mortalkind. Fighters in and of themselves come in all different shapes and sizes, and Fighters can be found in almost any kind of garb. Most feel most comfortable in armor and with a weapon or three by their side, but some prefer agility and speed to heavy armor and protection. Practical clothing, loose and easy to fight in, is always advisable.
Starting Skills
                              * The Most Basic Training: Basic Martial Weapons (1), Basic Shields (4)
                              * The Second Skin: Basic Armor (2), Light Armor (3)
                              * The Path of Combat - Choose one of the following: 
                              * Projectile Weapons (3), Lore: Historical (2)
                              * Short Weapons (3), Advanced Shields (2) or Two Weapon Style (2)
                              * Great Weapons (5)
Multiclass Skills
                              * Combat for the Uninitiated: Basic Martial Weapons (1), Short Weapons (3)


Note: If the character selects an option and already has the maximum amount of any of the selected skills, the costs for those skills will be granted as free BP instead.
