Fighter Innate Powers
Deadly Remnants [Innate]
Requirement: Fighter Level 1
Incantation: None
Call: “Altered, Deadly Remnant”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive        
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None
Accent: None                                        Effect: Altered


The Fighter can use shattered weapons to block and do 1 point of damage. Using a shattered weapon will never do more than 1 point, regardless of other modifiers and enhancements. When a Shatter is taken to a wielded weapon that the Fighter will continue to use with this Power, they should call “Altered, Deadly Remnant,” to convey that the Shatter was taken, and altered. In addition, when picking up a shield from the ground that the character does not know the status of, they can treat the shield as if it were intact unless they are informed otherwise.
Parry Blow [Innate]
Requirement: Fighter Level 3
Incantation: None
Call: “Counter, Parry”
Target: One Weapon Attack                        Duration: Instantaneous        
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Counter


While wielding a weapon or shield, a character may use Parry Blow to Counter any weapon delivered attack, by calling “Counter, Parry.” 
Fighting Instructor [Innate]
Requirement: Fighter Level 4
Incantation: RP: Slow 300 Instructing a character in how to use the weapon.
Call: “Long Grant [Skill]”
Target: Other Individual                                Duration: Long Rest 
Delivery: Touch                                         Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Skill


After spending at least 5 uninterrupted minutes instructing another character in how to fight using specific weapons or styles, the Instructor may grant the target a temporary Skill. Regardless of the Instructor's own skills, they may grant the following Skills to the target until their next Long Rest: Basic Martial Weapons, Short Weapons, Great Weapons, Projectile Weapons, Thrown Weapons, Basic Shields, Advanced Shields, or Two Weapon Style. If the Instructor has proficiency with Long Weapons or Great Shields, they may teach one of those instead. 
Mountain of Steel [Innate]
Requirement: Fighter Level 8
Incantation: None
Call: “Refresh Parry Blow”
Target: Self                                         Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                         Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Refresh


The Fighter may Sacrifice 5 Points of Physical Armor (not Barrier, Summoned Armor, or Natural armors) to Refresh the Parry Blow Power instantly. The character should call “Refresh, Parry Blow” when they use this power.
This ability may be used a number of times equal to half the character’s Fighter class-levels per Short Rest.


Warrior Spirit [Innate]
Requirement: Fighter Level 10
Incantation: None
Call: Special
Target: Self                                        Duration: Long Rest
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Damage, Counter


At the beginning of an Event, and after finishing a Long Rest, the Fighter may choose one of the following bonuses that last until the next Long Rest:


                              * While wielding a Melee Weapon with one hand, you may add the Piercing effect to any Spike attack once per Short Rest and increase the Base Spike Damage of that attack by 3.
                              * While Wielding a Great Weapon in both hands, you may add +1 to your Melee Base Damage.
                              * While Wielding a Projectile Weapon, you may add the Wounding Effect to your normal shots that utilize the Aiming mechanic, these shots also gain +1 Base Damage.
                              * While Wielding a Shield, you gain a Counter vs Shatter after completing a Short Rest. The Call is “Counter, Warrior Spirit.” Additionally, the Fighter gains +1 Maximum Health, physical Armor Point, and Natural Armor Point.
Fighter Skills
Reinforce [Class] - 4 BP 
Requirement: Fighter Level 3
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Refresh


Whenever the Fighter's physical armor is repaired by any type of crafted Patch Kit they may Refresh all powers with the [Reinforce] tag. The Fighter can benefit from this ability a number of times equal to their Fighter Class-Levels per Long Rest.


Accuracy [Class] - 3 BP
Requirement: Fighter Level 4
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Base Damage


Whenever they are using Thrown or Projectile Weapons, the Fighter’s Base Damage increases by 1.


Field Maintenance [Class] - 3 BP 
Requirement: Fighter Level 5
Incantation: RP Focus Quick 100
Call: “Mend [X] to Self”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Mend


Knowing how to properly maintain one’s equipment can mean the difference between life and death. The Fighter gains a pool of Maintenance Points equal to 10 plus their Fighter Class-Level that refreshes upon the completion of a Long Rest. They may spend a Focus Quick 100 roleplaying patching their own armor and use any amount of Points to apply them as Mending to their worn armor. When the character uses the Reinforce Skill, they gain two Maintenance Points.


Healthy [Class] - 3 BP
Requirement: Fighter Level 6
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None
Accent: None                                        Effect: None


The Fighter’s health is paramount, and there are those who take it as a matter of personal pride. The Fighter’s Base Maximum Life Points is increased by one. This is a Base change, and does not interact with Stacking rules.


Utilitarian [Class] - 4 BP
Requirement: Fighter Level 6


The Fighter learns an additional Utility Power from the Fighter Class Powers.


Flesh of the Titan [Class] - 4 BP
Requirement: Fighter Level 9
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive        
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Healing


Whenever the Fighter is Healed by another, they are Healed an additional Life Point. Thus, two points of Healing would heal the Fighter three Life Points. 
Fighter Utility Powers
Armored Shell [Utility]
Prerequisites: Light Armor
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive        
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None


Whenever the Fighter is physically representing and using at least 4 Armor Points of physical armor, they are Granted +1 Armor, increasing their Base Maximum Armor Points. 


Armored Shell II [Utility] 
Requirement: Fighter Level 10
Prerequisites: Armored Shell Utility Power
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None


Once the Fighter reaches 10th level, they gain +1 to their Armor Point total as long as they are wearing at least one point of Physical Armor.  
Bowyer [Utility]
Incantation: Focus Quick 100
Call: “Rebuild [Projectile Weapon] by Force”, “Grant +1 Spike Damage”
Target: Projectile Weapon                         Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Immediate
Accent: Force                                        Effect: Rebuild, Grant Spike Damage


The Fighter can spend a Focus Quick 100 Rebuilding a projectile weapon (such as a bow or crossbow) that has been Shattered.


In addition, out of combat, the Fighter may prepare ahead of time tending to their projectile weapons and ammunition, spending a Focus Quick 100 to give themselves a +1 Bonus Spike Damage on their first Spike used with their projectile weapon.


Execute [Utility]
Incantation: None
Call: “Death to Helpless”
Target: Helpless Individual                        Duration: Instantaneous        
Delivery: Weapon                                Refresh: Immediate
Accent: None                                        Effect: Death


The Fighter gains the Hard to Kill (1) and Carnal Creature (1) Perks.


Instead of doing a Death Blow, the character can instead use Execute to instantly kill a Helpless creature.
Fortitude [Utility]
Incantation: None
Call: “Counter, Fortitude” or “Cure disabled”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous        
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Counter, Cure


Fortitude can be used to Counter any Disable or Disarm Effect. Alternatively, the Fighter can Cure the disabled Condition on themself.


Knight’s Strength [Utility]
Incantation: None
Call: “Knight’s Strength”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Special        
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Spike Damage


The Fighter can gain a short burst of strength. This will last long enough to complete one carry, pull one person out of a trap or murk, open one portcullis, etc. If the Fighter picks up an ally while under this effect, they may move at any speed while carrying a body and only needs one hand to start the carry. Alternatively, the Fighter may use this power to gain +1 Bonus Spike Damage on their next spike attack with a weapon.
Lessons from Scars [Utility]
Incantation: None
Call: “[Name or Description] Subtle Discern by Mind: What is your Maximum Life Points?”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous        
Delivery: Verbal                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Discern


The Fighter gains Lore: Historical (2), Lore: Noble (2) Perks.


As a veteran of many campaigns, the fighter can gauge a rough level of toughness in those around them by sight. Choose one target that you can see, and look them over, then make the appropriate call to determine the target’s Maximum Life Points.
Linked Armor [Utility]
Prerequisites: Light Armor
Incantation: RP: Patching Armor
Call: “Grant 1 Barrier”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive, Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Barrier


The Fighter gains Medium Armor Skill.


As long as the character is physically representing and using at least 5 points of physical armor, the character may Grant 1 Barrier to themself at the end of each Short Rest after roleplaying patching their own armor.


Reload [Utility]
Incantation: None
Call: “Grant Insubstantial”, “Cure Insubstantial”
Target: Self                                         Duration: Special
Delivery: None                                         Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Insubstantial, Cure


The Fighter can only use this ability after moving off to a location removed from combat. They then go insubstantial and can return to the battle and collect projectile ammunition or thrown weapons. Once they have collected a maximum of six, they must return to their original location and Cure their insubstantial Condition by calling "Cure Insubstantial". While insubstantial the fighter can do nothing besides collect projectile ammunition or thrown weapons and return.
Renowned Soldier [Utility]
Incantation: None
Call: “Counter, Soldier”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive, Instantaneous        
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Counter


Having seen the worst that war has to offer, there’s little that can truly shake your will. 


The Fighter gains Minor Fame (1) and the Apprentice Profession (1) as bonus Skills. The character may choose a Profession suitable to describe their particular niche, but should reflect the actualities of being from a military background, examples include: Soldier, Mercenary, Gladiator, and Bounty Hunter. 


In addition, the fighter may Counter one Effect with the Fear Accent once per Short Rest.
Scales of the Serpent [Utility] [Reinforce]
Prerequisites: Medium Armor
Incantation: None
Call: “Counter, Scales of the Serpent”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous        
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Counter


The Fighter gains the Heavy Armor Skill.


The flexibility of Heavy armor is often overlooked, but veterans used to wearing these sorts of armor know that each scale is part of a whole, greater than its pieces. As long as the character is physically representing and using at least 7 points of physical armor, and has at least one point of armor remaining, they may, once per Short Rest, choose to Counter one Wounding attack.


Shoulder [Utility]
Incantation: None
Call: "Shatter [Target]”
Target: Door, Wall, or other obstacle                 Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: RP                                        Refresh: Focus Quick 100
Accent: None                                        Effect: Shatter


By taking a running start and putting their shoulder into a door, wall, or other immobile object, the Fighter can cause a Shatter Effect against it by force of pure strength. Care should be taken by the player not to damage any actual property. This power has no effect against held objects. If a door is shattered successfully in this way, it should be propped open.


Wall of Steel [Utility] [Reinforce]
Prerequisites: Heavy Armor
Incantation: None
Call: “Counter, Wall of Steel”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous        
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Counter


The Fighter gains the Ironclad Armor (2) Skill.


After years of living in the heaviest of armors, the Fighter has learned to depend on their armor’s durability, especially in the thickest of battles. Whenever the Fighter is physically representing at least 10 points of armor on both the Upper Torso and Lower Torso locations, once per Short Rest, if the Fighter is using Physical Armor and has at least one Armor Point remaining, they may “Counter” one Piercing attack that they receive.
Fighter Basic Powers


Battlemind [Basic] (2)
Incantation: Focus Slow 60
Call: “Short Grant +2 Bonus Maximum Spikes by Mind”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Grant Maximum Spikes


By spending a Focus Slow Count of 60 concentrating and meditating, the Fighter can put themselves into a mental state ready for combat. Until they take a Short Rest, their Maximum (and current) Spikes increases by two. 
Disarming Strike [Basic] (5)
Incantation: None
Call: “Quick Disarm [Item] by Force”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Quick 100
Delivery: Weapon                                Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: Force                                        Effect: Disarm


While striking an opponent or their equipment with a weapon, the fighter may expend a Spike to call out “Quick Disarm [Item] by Force”. 


Disengage [Basic] (3)
Incantation: None
Call: “Quick Repel by Mind” or “[Name or Description] Quick Repel by Mind”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Quick 100        
Delivery: Weapon or Verbal                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Repel


The Fighter can either make a weapon attack and cause the target to stay away from them for a short time or if they are wielding a projectile weapon in hand when the Power is used, can use this Power with a Verbal delivery instead, indicating the target’s name or description to cause them to be repelled.
Flaming Arrow [Basic] (5)
Incantation: None
Call: “Wounding 5 by Flame”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Weapon                                Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: Flame                                        Effect: Wounding


While wielding a projectile weapon, the fighter may set their ammunition aflame. For up to two shots with a projectile weapon, the archer may strike a foe and then call “Wounding 5 by Flame”. These shots need not be consecutive, but they expire when the archer takes a Short Rest.

If the Fighter has at least 11 levels of Fighter they may instead fire 3 shots instead of 2.


Grit [Basic] [Reinforce] [3]
Incantation: None
Call: “Counter, Grit”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous, Quick 100        
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Counter, Drained


The Fighter, through pure dedication, toughness, or sometimes concentrated hatred, can Counter one damaging attack delivered by Packet. Using this ability will leave the Fighter drained for a Quick 100 as they shake off the devastating damage, however, the Fighter may also expend a Spike and Sacrifice one Life Point to Counter the Drain Effect if they choose.


Harry [Basic] [Reinforce]
Incantation: None
Call: "Quick Provoke by Mind"
Target: Individual                                Duration: Permanent
Delivery: Melee Weapon                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Provoke


The Fighter is adept at keeping the attention of their foes, by word, deed, or well placed blow. The Fighter can deliver “Quick Provoke by Mind” with a single melee weapon attack. If the Fighter expends a Spike this power refreshes immediately.


Heroic Save [Basic] (2)
Incantation: None
Call: “Grant Insubstantial”, “Heroic Save”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Description, Slow 60
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Insubstantial


Seeing one of their comrades has fallen in battle, the Fighter heroically dashes in to save them, gaining a temporary surge of heroic inspiration allowing them to shrug off blows and deflect attacks.


The fighter may call "Grant Insubstantial to Self” and walk towards a downed ally. Once at the ally, they must call "Cure Insubstantial to Self", they then gain resistance to Damage and Effects (the appropriate call is "Resist, Heroic Save") and must pick up the ally. They may move at any speed while carrying the ally and only need one hand to start the carry.


These effects last while carrying the ally, ending when they are dropped, conscious, or dissipate. While carrying the ally, the fighter can't take any other actions but moving to safety and putting the ally down. In no case will any effect from this power persist beyond a Slow 60. 


Patchwork Defense [Basic]
Requirement: Parry Blow
Incantation: None
Call: “Refresh Parry Blow”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Refresh


Once per Long Rest, after having their physical armor repaired with a Patch Kit, the Fighter may refresh Parry Blow.


Raging Blows [Basic] (2)
Incantation: ROAR “That Shield Can’t Stop Me!”
Call: “Shatter Shield”
Target: Shield                                        Duration: Instantaneous        
Delivery: Weapon                                Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Shatter


If wielding a great weapon with two hands, the character may Call “Shatter Shield” after doing the Incantation (remember while doing Incantations a foot must be planted).


Enhancement: You may expend a spike to ignore the Incantation.


Resolve [Basic] [Reinforce] (2)
Incantation: None
Call: “Altered, Resolve”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous        
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Altered


After being affected by an Effect or Damage that would take the character to 0 Life Points or kill the character, the character may call “Altered, Resolve” and instead be reduced to 1 Life Point.


Shake It Off I [Basic] [Reinforce]
Incantation: None
Call: “Reduced”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Reduced Duration


The Fighter can reduce one Effect with a duration of “Quick 100” or “Short” to a Quick Count of 30. When the duration ends, the character must let out a bellow, yell, or cry before taking any other actions. This can reduce the duration of Effects caused by the Fighter’s own Powers, such as Grit.


Shot Placement [Basic] (2)
Incantation: None
Call: “[4/8]”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous        
Delivery: Weapon                                Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Damage


The Fighter can make one melee attack that will do 4 points of damage. If the Character performs the attack with a Great Weapon, the damage is 8 instead.


Smite [Basic] (2)
Incantation: None
Call: “Wounding [X]”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous        
Delivery: Weapon                                Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: Weapon                                Effect: Wounding


The Fighter can make one melee attack that will do Wounding damage equal to their Base Damage plus two. If the Character is wielding a Great Weapon and no other weapons or shields, the damage is equal to their Base Damage plus three.


Snap Shot [Basic] (3)
Incantation: None
Call: “[Name or Description] Quick Root”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Quick 100
Delivery: Verbal                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Root


While wielding a projectile weapon, the Fighter may fire a shot so quick that many people don't see them even draw the string, causing them to pin a foe to the ground with a call of “[Name or description], Quick Root.”


Stifle [Basic]
Incantation: None
Call: “Short Silence”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Projectile Weapon                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Silence


While wielding a projectile weapon, the Fighter may make a projectile attack and call “Short Silence” against an opponent, as the projectile neatly pierces the opponent’s neck. The strike should not actually target the player’s neck.


Twin Shot [Basic]
Incantation: None                                
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: None


The character can fire two projectiles in succession that are identical. If the archer lands two consecutive shots, they may immediately use the Call from the first shot on the second.


Fighter Advanced Powers


Archer’s Eye [Advanced]
Incantation: None
Call: “Grant Wounding”, “Wounding [X]”
Target: Self,  Individual                                Duration: Short Rest, Instantaneous
Delivery: None, Weapon                                Refresh: Long Rest, Immediate
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Wounding, Wounding


Once activated, while wielding a projectile weapon, the Fighter may choose to aim for a Quick Count of 30, rather than a Quick count of 10, and add the Wounding Effect to their next shot. The Fighter may use this power at-will until they take a Short Rest or leave combat. This Effect cannot alter the calls of other Powers.
Challenging Shout [Advanced]
Incantation: None
Call: "[Name or Description] Quick Provoke by Mind"
Target: Individual                                Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Verbal                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Provoke


The Fighter might not always be able to reach ranged combatants with their blades, but their words can sometimes assault a heart even from far away. After issuing a challenge, insult, or raging bellow, the Fighter can verbally deliver a “Quick Provoke by Mind” to an opponent. If the Provoke is Prevented, the Fighter gains a +1 Bonus to their Spike Damage until they complete a Short Rest.


Expert Parry [Advanced] [Reinforce]
Requirement: Parry Blow
Incantation: None
Call: “Refresh Parry Blow”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Refresh


Once per Long Rest, the Fighter may refresh Parry Blow.
Hammer Down [Advanced]
Incantation: None
Call: "Shatter [Item] by Force"
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Melee Weapon                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: Force                                        Effect: Shatter


Many fighters find that a good solid shield is the key to a sturdy defense. However, it can become a liability once destroyed, leaving the warrior both unbalanced and scrambling. The Fighter may call “Shatter [Item] by Force” once per Short Rest. If the Fighter is wielding a Great Weapon and no other weapons or shields, they may immediately Refresh this power by expending a Spike.


Heartseeker Shot [Advanced] (3)
Incantation: None
Call: “15”or “10 Wounding”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Projectile Weapon                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Damage, Wounding


The Fighter lets loose a deadly shot killing in a single shot powerful enemies. This Power, which only may be used with a projectile weapon deals 15 or may instead deal 10 Wounding.

This call may not be modified, though it may be copied with Twin Shot.
Improved Blows [Advanced]
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous        
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Refresh


When this power is used, it refreshes two Powers that do the Wounding Effect from the Basic Fighter Powers.


Lock Down [Advanced] (2)
Incantation: None
Call: “Quick Root”
Target: Individual                                 Duration: Quick 100
Delivery: Weapon                                 Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Root


The Fighter can make melee attacks that Root those they hit. As long as they keep their right foot planted, as if rooted, they can swing “Quick Root” at-will.


Rally the Troops [Advanced] (2)
Incantation: RP: About 5 seconds of encouraging an ally 
Call: “[Name or Description] Refresh One Spike and Grant 2 Barrier by Mind”
Target: Other Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Verbal                                 Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Refresh, Grant Barrier


Shouting a few words of encouragement, tactics, or sometimes even a simple "look out behind you", the Fighter encourages one of their comrades to greatness. The Fighter must provide instruction and encouragement to an ally for about 5 seconds and should then follow it with the call immediately to provide one individual a Refreshed Spike and 2 Barrier by Mind. The Fighter may not target themselves with this ability.


This instruction and encouragement may be tactical in nature, for example: "Brick, shift left. Okay, now with me. Good. Now we push around the flank. Nice." would be an acceptable type of RP to engage in as long as it is approximately 5 seconds worth.

Rapid Shot [Advanced] (3)
Incantation: None
Call: “Wounding [X]”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Weapon                                Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Wounding


While wielding a projectile weapon, if the Fighter lands (hits the target or their equipment, even if Prevented) two consecutive damaging shots on the same target, they may expend a Spike to make the second shot Wounding [X] where X is the damage called for the first shot.
Refocus [Advanced] (5)

Call: “Refresh 3 Spikes to Self by Mind”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous        
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Refresh


The Fighter refreshes 3 of their Spikes to no higher than their current Maximum Spikes.
Rest [Advanced]
Incantation: None
Call: “Grant Resistance to Death and Deathblows to Self by Mind”, “Heal 1 to Self by Mind”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Special 
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Grant Resistance, Heal


Once per Long Rest, the character may choose to activate this power while Dying. When activated, the character, while dying, gains resistance to Death Blows and Death Effects. In addition, after the character has been dying for a Slow Count of 120, they may heal themselves for 1 Life Point. The character need not be conscious to use this Power.
Riposte [Advanced]

Target: Self                                         Duration: Passive 
Delivery: None                                         Refresh: None
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Refresh


Whenever the Fighter uses a power that uses the call “Counter, Parry”, they may Refresh an expended Spike.


Shake It Off II [Advanced] [Reinforce] (5)
Incantation: None
Call: “Reduce”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous        
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Reduced Duration


The Fighter can reduce one Effect with a duration of “Quick” or “Short” to a Quick 30. When the duration ends, the character must let out a bellow, yell, or cry before taking any other actions. This can reduce the duration of Effects caused by the Fighter’s own Powers, such as Grit. Shake It Off II can be used while drained, imprisoned, or while affected by any Condition that normally stops a character from using Powers. 


Skewer [Advanced] (2)

Stand Ground [Advanced] [Reinforce] (2)

Call: “Counter, Stand Ground”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous        
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Counter


Boots firmly digging into the dirt, the Fighter is adept at staying in the fray and may Counter one Repel, Slow, or Root Effect.


Throat Shot [Advanced]
Incantation: None
Call: “Short Silence”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Weapon                                Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Silence


The fighter strikes the target with skill and precision, disrupting their ability to speak. This Silence lasts until the creature Short Rests. The strike should not actually target the player’s throat.


Thick Skin [Advanced] [Reinforce] (3)

Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Protect


The Fighter may grant themself a Protect vs. Melee Weapons upon the completion of a Short Rest.






Mage: Base Class
Utility & Arcane Spell-Casting Class


Description
The Mage is truly a power to be reckoned with. Their offensive magic is effective and lethal and their defensive magic is some of the most potent available. There are few classes that compare to a high level Mage and Mages always command both fear and respect from their adversaries. They are the disciples of power. Compared to their Sourcerer cousins, Mages command the arcane powers through dedication, study, and control.


The Mage is a strong addition to any team. Mages have a number of enchantments and spells that can help add to a team's strength and effectiveness. The Mage's power trade-off is tightly restricted weaponry and light armor in return for particularly potent defensive magic. They tend to be back-line combatants, as their abilities rarely allow them the capability to stand up to well-armed and armored warrior classes. With the right warrior back-up though, the Mage can become a truly devastating force.


The Mage is an Arcane spell-caster. The Mage should choose a special symbol that is their caster-sigil and mark their spellbook and Materia with it.
Starting Skills 
                              * The Arcane Unveiled: Basic Arcane (4)
                              * Dutiful Scholar: Choose a Lore Skill (2), Library Use (1), Bookcaster (1) x 2
                              * A Magical Specialty - Choose one of the following:
                              * Extended Capacity - Novice (3) x 2
                              * Advanced Recharge (2), Lore: Arcane (2), Bookcaster (1) x 2 
                              * Bookcaster (1) x 6
                              * Additional Cantrip (6)
Multiclass Skills
                              * Dabbling in the Arcane: Basic Arcane (4)


Note: If the character selects an option and already has the maximum amount of any of the selected skills, the costs for those skills will be granted as free BP instead.

Note: Life Points and Maximum Spikes can be found in the Level Progression Table in the Wellspring Core Rules.
Spells Known
The Mage has a number of Spells that they can choose from. These Spells should be recorded in the Mage’s Spellbook. Each time the character gains a Mage level, they can remove one Spell from the list and replace it with another. There are no limits on what tier Spell replaces the removed Spell (meaning a Novice Spell could be removed and replaced with a Greater Spell). 
Mage Base Incantation


“I conjure words of arcane might, from studied tomes in candlelight,
I draw my strength from sky and land. Gather now within my hand.
From strands unseen, my power's Source, By Word and Will I give it force.
My Word as law, my Will supreme, I fuse the strength of mind and dream…”
Mage Innate Powers
Arcane Study [Innate]
Requirement: Mage Level 1
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Long Rest


The Mage can meditate and realign their theoretical constructs to rebuild focus and refresh their Arcane Spell-slots. The Mage can spend a Short Rest studying their spellbook, making notes on spells, or practicing their incants quietly.
 
The Mage can perform this special study and refresh all of their expended Arcane spell-slots. If the Mage has used any other Innate Power from another class to refresh their Arcane spell slots this long rest they may not use Arcane Study.


Additional benefits come if this study is done in an area of powerful arcane magic (such as an Arcane Locus), and performs a personal rite (determined by the Mage). Doing so, the Mage gains three additional Arcane Novice-tier Spell-Slots. These Spell-Slots last until the Mage completes a Long Rest. Additional benefits may be possible (see the Skills, Perks, and Flaws document for more details).
Replicate Enhancement [Innate]
Requirement: Mage Level 2
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: None                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Immediate


When casting a non-damaging touch Mage spell, the number of targets is increased by one. The spell must have a target of Individual, shield, or armor, and the listed Delivery must be Touch (Packet Spells cast as Touch do not get this benefit).


Once they achieve 7th level Mage they may add one additional target per cast. At 12th, they gain a third additional target.
Ritual Affinity [Innate]
Requirement: Mage Level 3
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: None                                        Duration: Passive
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None


The Mage can use their understanding of Arcane magic to help navigate the complexities of Ritual magic. They gain the Apprentice Ritual Magic skill at no cost. Once they achieve 7th level Mage, they can purchase Journeyman Ritual Magic skill for one fewer Build Point. Once they achieve 12th level Mage, they can purchase Greater Ritual Magic skill for one fewer Build Point. If the Mage has already purchased either of these skills, they get one Build Point back for each.
Arcane Barrier [Innate]
Requirement: Mage Level 5
Incantation: “I conjure words of arcane might, from studied tomes in candlelight to grant you the protection of the Arcane."
Call: “Short Grant [1/2/3] Barrier by Force”
Target: Individual                                 Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Touch                                         Refresh: Long Rest, Special
Accent: Force                                         Effect: Grant Barrier


The Mage can call upon the powers of the Arcane to grant an individual one Barrier by Touch. This Power refreshes whenever the Mage casts a Spell (not Cantrip) from their list of Mage spells or after a Long Rest. Once they achieve 10th level Mage this Power instead grants two Barrier. At 15th level Mage it instead grants three Barrier.
Cantrip Mastery [Innate]
Requirement: Mage Level 8
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: None                                        Duration: Passive
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None
Accent: Force                                         Effect: Grant Plus Damage


All damaging Cantrips cast by the Mage do one additional point of damage.
Practiced Sight [Innate]
Requirement: Mage Level 9, Apprentice Ritual Magic
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: None                                        Duration: Passive
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None


When the Mage is a Primary Ritualist in a Ritual, when they pull their Final Card from the Dark Territory Hand, they may also pull one additional card from the Hand. When the second card is revealed, the Marshal will give them a premonition of the outcome of the second card. This premonition is given by the attending Marshal as “Weal (Success), Woe (Failure), or Alteration.“ This does not replace the first drawn card, merely suggests what the outcome of the second card might have been.
Channel the Arcane [Innate]
Requirement: Mage Level 10
Incantation: Focus Quick 100 (See Also description)
Call: “Refresh all Novice and Adept Mage Spell Slots”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Event
Accent: None                                        Effect: Refresh


This Mage power gives them incredible versatility and ability to continue their arcane assistance in battle in exchange for some obvious vulnerability during the channeling process. 


Once per Event, without access to their book or the normal meditation process Mage may engage in an incredibly obvious and loud magical gathering of essence for about 1 minute and then announce loudly “Beginning to Channel Arcane Energies” and begin a Focus for a Quick 100. At the end of this entire process the Mage instantaneously Refreshes all their Arcane Novice and Adept Spell Slots. If interrupted during this Focus Quick count the power is considered expended so the Mage should do this in an area they are certain they will not be interrupted.


Upon attaining the 15th level in Mage this Innate Power also refreshes Arcane Greater spell slots.
Mage Class Skills
Empowered Mage Bolt [Class] - 3 BP
Requirement: Mage Level 2
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None


While before, the nuances of the arcane might have been foreign to the Mage, now, it’s become second nature. The four arcane affinities: Flame, Ice, Lightning, and Acid, are now always close to the Mage’s fingertips. 


When casting the Mage Bolt Cantrip, the Mage may choose to use the Acid, Flame, Ice, or Lightning Accent instead of Force. 
Arcane Charge [Class] - 2 BP
Requirement: Mage Level 4
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None
When the Replicate Enhancement Innate Power is used, the Mage may choose to reduce the number of targets by one and instead place the additional charge granted into a Spell-Packet. This allows the Mage to retain the charge for later use, or deliver the charge at range.


Cantrip Scholar [Class] - 4 BP
Requirement: Mage Level 6


The Mage learns an additional Cantrip from the Mage Class Powers or Cantrips.
Mage Cantrips
Mage Cantrip Incantation


“I conjure words of arcane might…”
Cancel [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: None
Target: Spell-Packet, Self                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                                Refresh: Immediate


This minor cantrip takes the power out of a Spell-Packet (or multiple Spell-Packets) that has already been charged and returns that power to the caster. The Spell-Packet becomes inert, and the caster refreshes the Spell-Slot that was used to charge the Spell-Packet. If the Spell charges more than one Spell-Packet, all the Spell-Packets must still be charged in order to cancel the Spell.
Force Shield [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Grant Protect vs Force or Mind”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Until Used
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Focus Quick 100
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Protect


The Mage throws up a shield of energy to protect themself from Force or Mind.
Fumble [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Disarm [Item] by Force”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Focus Quick 100
Accent: Force                                        Effect: Disarm


A creature stuck by the spell has the named item or item held by the named limb forcibly removed and dropped to the ground. If the specified equipment is a shield (or the limb is wearing a shield) the arm must be straightened so that it is pointing directly at the ground to allow the shield to slide off the arm if possible. Unlike other Effects, if no duration is called, the duration of Disarm is Instantaneous, and the item may be picked up immediately upon hitting the ground. 
Ghost Hand [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Grant Insubstantial to Self”, “Cure Insubstantial” 
Target: Self                                        Duration: Slow Count of 15
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Focus Slow 300
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Insubstantial, Cure


The caster must immediately drop everything they are holding and will go insubstantial. They then have a Slow Count of 15 to perform one action and return to the approximate spot where they completed the incant. At the end of the Slow Count of 15 the caster must immediately return to where they were at the start of the spell, and cure their insubstantial Condition.


The action must be one of the following: pushing a spot with one finger, picking up a small object (no larger than two fists) and placing it/throwing it, pulling on something with one hand, gesturing to another individual with one hand, or tapping a character on the shoulder (if the character has prior explicit consent only). If this movement triggers a trap, the Mage will be considered to have tripped the trap while momentarily physical, and therefore take the effects of the trap per the normal Insubstantial rules. This cantrip can only be cast out of combat.


Note: This spell offers interaction normally unavailable to Insubstantial creatures. 
Identify [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Discern to [Item] by Mind: Identification”
Target: Non-Relic magic item                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Immediate
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Discern


By using this Cantrip on an item while at an Arcane Locus, the Mage may learn all of the magical properties of an item. 


Although it is easy for anyone to determine if an item is magical or special in nature and read the information card to discover obvious properties, this cantrip will allow the player to look at an item’s folded information card (even if they don’t meet the requirements on the outside) to know its exact properties. Only the caster will be allowed to read the properties, although if they choose to, they can open the item card to make the properties discoverable to anyone. If the card says “Divine Relic” on the public portion, this spell has no effect.


Note: Items identified with this spell may still be Obfuscated or otherwise masked, and Identification may not reveal all its properties. Identify may have other specific uses.
Mage Bolt [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “1 Force”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Immediate
Accent: Force                                         Effect: Damage


This minor cantrip charges a Spell-Packet with an explosive burst which will do 1 point of Force damage to those it hits.
See Magic [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: [Name or Description] Discern by Mind: [Question]
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Verbal                                        Refresh: Immediate
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Discern


See Magic gives the Mage the ability to detect magical emanations from enchantments, active spells (including summoned armor), or magical items. Once cast, the Mage can ask any or all of the following questions of as many targets as desired. The Mage can do this until they cast another spell, says anything that is not one of the questions, takes damage, takes an Effect, or completes a Short Rest:


                              * “What non-Inherent Conditions do you have?”
                              * “How many points of summoned armor do you have?”
                              * “What items are you carrying that are magical?”
                              * “What was the last spell you cast?”
                              * “Are you a Construct?”
Stop [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Quick Root by Mind”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Quick 100
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Focus Quick 100
Accent: Mind                                         Effect: Root


A creature struck by the spell has their feet bound to the ground with momentary shackles of pure mental power. 
Mage Novice Spells
Mage Novice Incantation


“I conjure words of arcane might, from studied tomes in candlelight…”
Arcane Barrage [Novice] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Wounding 1 by Force”
Target: Self, Individual                                 Duration: Short Rest or Until Expended, Instantaneous
Delivery: None, Spell-Packet                         Refresh: Spell
Accent: Force                                         Effect: Wounding


After this spell is cast, the caster gains the ability to charge a Spell-Packet with power by simply holding it and calling "Spell-Packet Charged!" Each Spell-Packet charged in this way will do Wounding 1 when it strikes. Only one Spell-Packet can be charged at a time, and Arcane Barrage goes down if the caster completes a Short Rest, casts another Spell, or charges a fifth Spell-Packet using Arcane Barrage. 

Enhancement: Adept Slot: The caster can charge 10 Spell-Packets with Wounding 2.
Enhancement: Greater Slot: The caster can charge 15 Spell-Packets with Wounding 3 and the caster may replace the Accent with the Mind, Acid, Flame, Ice, or Lightning Accent, changing the accent on each attack.
Bolster Shield [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Grant Protect vs. Shatter to Shield by Force” x3
Target: Shield(s)                                        Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Force                                         Effect: Grant Protect


Bolster Shield adds a sheath of magical power around up to three shields, giving it a Protect vs. Shatter.
Carnate [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Cure Insubstantial to Insubstantial by Mind”, “[Name or Description], Piercing 5 to Insubstantial by Mind.”
Target: Insubstantial Individual or Object                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Cure, Piercing


The Mage can use this spell to shove an incorporeal creature or object fully onto their plane, making it vulnerable to many normal attacks. If the Carnate Spell-Packet is Prevented, one more Spell-Packet can be charged with this same spell by calling “Spell-Packet Charged!”. If the second Carnate Spell-Packet is also Prevented the Mage may call “[Name or Description], Piercing 5 to Insubstantial by Mind.”
Cripple [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Short Disable [Limb] by Force”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Force                                         Effect: Disable


The target of this spell feels their limb twisted and crushed by an invisible force. An arm will become useless and will be forced to drop whatever that hand is holding. A disabled leg is unable to hold weight. When the Spell-Packet strikes the target, the caster must choose a limb and make the Call. If the Spell-Packet appears to hit a specific limb, or if it hits a weapon or equipment held by a specific limb, that limb must be called, even if it is already disabled. 


Enhancement: Expend a Spike during Incantation: An additional Spell-Packet is charged.
Flameburst [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “4 Flame”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Flame                                         Effect: Damage


Fireburst charges a Spell-Packet with a dreadful arcane flame. This Spell-Packet will do “4 Flame”.


Enhancement: Expend a Spike during Incantation: An additional Spell-Packet is charged.
Forcebolt [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Wounding 4 by Force”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Force                                         Effect: Wounding


Forcebolt charges a Spell-Packet with a pure magical energy, doing Wounding 4 by Force.


Enhancement: Expend all current points of Summoned Armor during Incantation (minimum 1): An additional two Spell-Packets are charged (Total 3).
Freeze [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Quick Imprison by Ice”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Quick 100
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Ice                                        Effect: Imprisoned


When this spell is cast, it will charge two Spell-Packets at the same time. The Spell-Packets must be thrown separately. When struck by this spell, the target is immediately encased in ice. For the duration, the block of ice will protect them, but will also stop all movement and use of Powers (as normal for imprisoned). 


Enhancement: Expend a Spike during Incantation: An additional Spell-Packet is charged.
Mage Armor [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Long Grant 3 Summoned Armor to Self by Force”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Long Rest
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: Force                                         Effect: Grant Summoned Armor


The caster grants themself 3 Summoned Armor Points. This is considered a new “suit” or armor and is not stackable with other sources of summoned armor. The Mage may instead change the duration of the spell to be Short and grant themselves 4 points of Summoned Armor instead.
Mage Mend [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Mend 5 by Force”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Spell-Packet or Touch                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: Force                                         Effect: Mend


The target of this spell will Mend 5 Armor Points. If the Mage chooses Touch Delivery it may be enhanced by Replicate Enhancement.
Mage Power [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Refresh Basic Short Rest Base Class Martial Power”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Refresh


The Mage reinvigorates a martial target transforming Arcane energy into strength for the target to power through. The target of this spell immediately refreshes one Basic Base Class Martial Power with a recovery period of Short Rest. The same target may only be affected by any particular Mage’s Mage Power spell once per Long Rest. 
Mageskin [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Grant 2 Barrier and Protect vs Weapon by Force”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Until Used
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: Force                                         Effect: Grant Barrier, Grant Protect


Mageskin summons two points of Barrier for the individual and one Protect against Weapons for the individual.
Mask Aura [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Short Grant Counter vs Discerns by Mind”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Counter


The Mage may put a protective bubble around one individual, making the character difficult to Discern. Whenever the recipient is targeted by a Discern by Mind, they must answer “Counter, Masked”. This protection lasts until the recipient completes or otherwise receives the effect of a Short (or Long) Rest, regardless of the number of Discerns they Counter. Mask Aura can only be cast out of combat in a quiet and generally peaceful area and the effects of the Spell must be explained clearly to the recipient.
Revulsion [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Short Repel by Mind”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Repel


The Mage reaches out with their will, putting into others a deep compulsion to stay away. Upon completion of the incantation, two Spell-Packets will be charged with the ability to Repel others.

Enhancement: Adept Slot: Four packets are charged instead of two.
Enhancement: Greater Slot: The Spell charges a single Spell-Packet, but gives the Mage the ability to continue casting the spell as if it were a cantrip until the next Short Rest. Although multiple Spell-Packets are charged at once, they must be thrown one at a time.
Magekey [Novice]

Call: “Dispel Imprisoned by Force”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Force                                        Effect: Dispel


Magekey allows the caster to end the imprisoned Condition on one Individual. This can be to save a friend or to end the protection on a foe. This cannot be used while the Mage themself is imprisoned.
Ruinous Curse [Novice]

Call: “Quick Slow by Mind”, “Quick Weakness by Mind”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Quick 100
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Slow, Weakness


Ruinous Curse charges two Spell-Packets simultaneously. One will Slow its target, the other will Weaken its target, each for a Quick Count of 100.
Shred [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Shatter [Item] by Force”
Target: Hand Held Object                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: Force                                        Effect: Shatter


When this spell is cast, Shred Shatters an item the Mage is holding. If the item is successfully Shattered, and the item was tagged, or otherwise had provable in-game value, like a weapon, shield, or other discarded item on the battlefield, the Mage refreshes one Spike, or gains two Barrier, their choice.
Town Portal [Novice] 
Incantation: Standard
Call: "Town Portal, Insubstantial!", “Grant Insubstantial to Self”, “Instruction: [Explanation]”, “[Name or Description] Cure Insubstantial to Insubstantial”
Target: Self, Individuals                                Duration: Long Rest
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Insubstantial, Cure


This spell must be cast Out Of Combat and the Mage may only have one active Town Portal trigger and sigil at any one time. Upon casting this spell, the Mage inscribes a location, outside, within touch range, with a symbol of their choosing. The Symbol must be at least 6” by 6” and must be visible from at least one angle by a person standing on the ground.


At any time until the Mage finishes a Long Rest, they may trigger this power in order to “Grant Insubstantial to Self” and are compelled to walk directly to their inscribed symbol and touch it. The Mage should call "Town Portal, Insubstantial!" when this occurs.


If the Spell is Enhanced, all individuals must all be within Touch range when the Power is triggered. The caster should make it clear to the targets who are being affected and how. The Mage may use "Instruction" to tell the players that their character is going to be Insubstantial, can only walk, and should Call "Town Portal, Insubstantial." In addition, all affected characters must attempt to remain within melee distance of the caster until they have reached the Sigil, or have been cured of Insubstantial.


When the travelers reach the sigil's original location and the Mage touches the sigil, the caster calls “[Name or Description] Cure Insubstantial to Insubstantial” on all the travelers. If, instead, the Symbol is no longer there or otherwise defaced, the caster immediately dies.


Should the caster die, or the spell ends, all Effects and Conditions related to this spell end.


Note: This means the caster is Dead, but no longer Insubstantial, as all Conditions are removed when dead. Removing the Insubstantial Condition will not remove any other part of this Spell's effects: the Mage (and other Targets) will still need to walk back to the original location and will still die if the sigil has been moved or defaced. 

Enhancement: Adept Slot: Two additional passengers can be taken.
Enhancement: Greater Slot: Four additional passengers can be taken. 
Mage Adept Spells
Mage Adept Incantation


“I conjure words of arcane might, from studied tomes in candlelight,
I draw my strength from sky and land. Gather now within my hand…”
Adept Mage Power [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Refresh Advanced Short Rest Base Class Power or Novice Spell Slot”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: Force                                        Effect: Refresh


The Mage reinvigorates a target, transforming Arcane energy into strength for the target to power through. The target of this spell immediately refreshes one Advanced Tier (or lower) Base Class Martial Power with a recovery period of Short Rest or one Novice Spell slot of any type. The same target may only be affected by any particular Mage’s Adept Mage Power spell once per Long Rest. 
Arcane Bridge [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “It Can Be Seen, An Arcane Bridge is Built!”, “It Can Be Seen, The Arcane Bridge Collapses!”
Target: Special                                        Duration: Special
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: It Can Be Seen


This spell must be cast upon a Spell-Packet that has been marked with a symbol that is special to the caster. The Spell-Packet is then thrown and once it comes to rest, the Mage can briefly touch the ground to create an ethereal, translucent bridge of pure magical power that extends from that spot to the thrown Spell-Packet. 


The bridge is taxing to maintain, and once six Individuals have passed over it, the magical force will dissipate. The bridge will likewise dissipate if the anchoring Spell-Packet is moved in any way. Severe changes in level between the two ends cause the bridge to dissipate immediately, so this spell cannot be used to help climb or descend more than about four stair-steps up or down.


Enhancement: Greater Slot: Ten individuals can cross the bridge before it ends.  
Betrayal [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Berserk by Mind”
Target: Individual(s)                                Duration: Permanent
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Berserk


The Mage charges two Spell-Packets with the energy of madness and pain. Each charge will cause an individual to strike out at those around it, attempting to harm anyone they can reach. A Berserked creature will attack the nearest creature until it is slept or drops to 0 Life Points. They will not use Powers, and will stop attacking a creature once it falls. If the caster expends a Spike an additional Spell-Packet is charged.
Disintegrate Item [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Shatter [Item] by Force”
Target: Individual or Item                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Force                                        Effect: Shatter


When cast, this spell charges two Spell-Packets that cause the items struck to be Shattered. If the Spell-Packet strikes a shield or weapon held by an individual, then that shield or weapon must be targeted. If the Spell-Packet strikes the individual, the caster can choose from a held item, physical armor, or some other visible possession. The target may be an unattended physical item. Magic items are generally immune and their holder may call out “No Effect” in these cases.
Dispel Magic [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: See below
Target: Individual or Object                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Various


This powerful spell can be used to end the duration of many Spells or Powers, including Spells like Elemental Node or protective circles. It can be used in one of the following ways:


                              * Harm Constructs: “Piercing 3 to Construct.” If the caster expends a Spike an additional Spell-Packet is charged.
                              * Dispel any of the following: “Dispel Protects”, “Dispel Summoned Armor”, “Dispel Barrier”, or a specific Condition: “Dispel [Condition].”
                              * Dispel all Conditions caused by a specific Accent: “Dispel [Accent].”
                              * Break any circle represented by a cord, rope, or the like: “Dispel Circle.”


Note: Dispel Magic may have other specific uses.
Dominate Creature [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Short Dominate by Mind”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Dominate


The target of the spell will feel their attitude change and shift, becoming compliant to the caster’s will. The target is Dominated to the caster for a Short Rest, or until the target is reduced to zero Life Points. This spell is exhausting to the caster, and thus the caster may only have a single target dominated with this spell at any one time, and, in addition, this spell may only be cast once per Long Rest.
Elemental Node [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Wounding 3 by [Acid / Flame / Ice / Lightning]”
Target: An Arcane Circle                                Duration: Instantaneous, Short Rest
Delivery: Touch, Spell-Packet                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: Acid / Flame / Ice / Lightning                Effect: Wounding


This spell must be cast while inside a white cord, rope, or the like that is no longer than 10 feet long that has been laid down to make a continuous shape (usually a circle). At the completion of the Incantation, the lain rope cannot be purposefully moved by anyone other than the caster, and anyone who attempts to do so should be informed by use of “Instruction: You may not move the rope.” or similar. This does not prevent people from crossing the threshold of the cord, rope, or the like. 


Should the rope be moved purposefully by the caster, the spell ends. This should be communicated to others trying to touch or manipulate it.


As long as the caster is completely inside the circle (regardless of whether they have remained in the circle), they can charge a Spell-Packet with elemental energy by calling “Spell-Packet Charged” Only one Spell-Packet may be charged at a time with this spell. 


The Spell-Packet will cause “Wounding 3 by [Acid/Flame/Ice/Lightning] Damage. The Mage may charge a number of Spell-Packets with this spell equal to their Mage class-level. The Mage can continue to do this until they complete a Short Rest or until the Circle is moved, broken, or Dispelled. Incidental/accidental movement of the circle will not disturb the spell (such as a foot moving the cord accidentally).


The Circle can be Dispelled by Dispel Magic and similar magic.


Note: The caster may only have one Circle active at any given time, regardless of origin. 
Elemental Survival [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: Explanation
Target: Individuals                                Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Resistance


Elemental Survival must be cast focusing on one of the Elemental Cornerstones (Fire, Air, Water, Earth). The caster designates 3 targets to gain traits based on the plane chosen. The character cannot be affected by this spell more than once at a time, even if a different plane is chosen.


                              * Air: The character is unaffected by high winds, including natural tornadoes and the like. The character is unaffected by natural lightning strikes and can see normally through clouds and the dust caused by weather. The character has resistance to Lightning and resistance to falling damage a number of times equal to their Maximum Life Points.
                              * Fire: The character takes no damage from normal fire or heat, and can breathe and see normally in smoky environments. The character has resistance to Flame a number of times equal to their Maximum Life Points.
                              * Earth: The character can pass through some rock and stone (the player can touch stone for a Focus Quick 10 then meld into or out of it, becoming Insubstantial and inherently Slowed while in this state). The character has resistance to Acid a number of times equal to their Maximum Life Points.
                              * Water: The character can walk normally on icy surfaces and can breathe normally underwater. The character will suffer no ill effects from being in deep water and can see normally underwater, even in almost total darkness. The character has resistance to Ice a number of times equal to their Maximum Life Points.


Enhancement: Greater Slot: The Spell’s Duration changes to be Long Rest.

Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Power


The caster may identify up to three individuals who are given the Gift of Solitude may, up to three times before completing a Short Rest, verbally repel nearby attackers, giving them room to breathe. The target receives the Alone But Not Lonely Power below:

Improved Identification [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Discern to [Item] by Mind: Identification”
Target: Any number of Magic items                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: Mind                                        Effect: Discern


The Mage may learn all of the magical properties of all designated non Divine Relic items. Although it is easy for anyone to determine if an item is magical or special in nature and read the information card to discover obvious properties, this spell will allow the player to look at the folded information card (even if they don’t meet the requirements on the outside) to know its exact properties.


Only the caster will be allowed to read the properties, although if they choose to, they can open each item card to make the properties discoverable to anyone.

Spell Absorption [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Grant Protect vs Damage from Packets”, “Wounding [X] by Force”
Target: Self, Individual                                Duration: Short Rest, Instantaneous
Delivery: None, Spell-Packet                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: Force                                        Effect: Grant Protect, Wounding


This spell gives the Mage a limited ability to absorb magical power and use it themself. They gain a Protect against damaging Packets. When the Protect is used, it will also absorb the magic of the attack. The amount of Power that can be used depends on the damage called with the Power. If the Mage does not have a Spell-Packet in hand, the Mage can Refresh one expended Novice Spell-Slot.


If the Mage has a Spell-Packet in hand, they can call “Spell-Packet Charged!” and give the Spell-Packet the ability to do the same damage as the original Power, although whatever Accent that Power had will be changed to Force. The call for the charged Spell-Packet is “Wounding [Damage] by Force.”
Succumb [Adept]

Twisting fate and warping reality, the Mage lays bare their opponent’s weakness for all to exploit. A single Spell-Packet is charged with this spell.
Wrest Control [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “[Name or Description] Short Dominate to Construct by Force”
Target: One Construct                                Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Verbal                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: Force                                        Effect: Dominate


The Mage can intuit the pathways used in the generation of constructs, and attempt to override them, gaining control. The Mage weaves mystic sigils in the air binding the strands of energy animating the golem, verbalizing their intent, and the construct is tethered to their will… for a time. 










Rogue: Base Class
Martial Class


Description
Rogues are often seen as thieves or burglars, but this is not the real essence of the Rogue. A Rogue is an agile warrior, whose expertise with the throwing dagger is unrivaled. It is true that many Rogues are attracted to the less legal side of life, and it is true that most prefer a good ambush from behind than a face-to-face sword fight, but Rogues are not always this way. There are a few that rely on their speed and cunning to simply out-fight their opponents. The Rogue is great at supporting fire, and what they lack in armor and brute force, they make up for in speed, cunning and deadly skill.


The Rogue of Wellspring brings in heavy supporting fire. There are few things more worrisome than a Rogue with their daggers. They tend to snipe from a distance, only engaging in combat when they know that they can get in killing blows while using their dodging abilities to remain unharmed. Rogues also have great ability with locks, traps and poisons, and make a worthy addition to a team.
Starting Skills
                              * To the Point: Basic Martial Weapons (1), Thrown Weapons (3)
                              * Trust is Rare: Basic Armor (2), Light Armor (3)
                              * Up to No Good - Choose One of the Following:
                              * Short Weapons (3), Scavenge (3)
                              * Fence (3), Lore [Shadow] (2), Profession - Apprentice [Specific Profession] (1)
                              * Basic Lock Skill (5) or Basic Trap Skill (5), Poisoner (1)
                              * Connections (2), Lore: Shadow (2), and Contact (2)
Multiclass Skills
                              * The Tools of the Trade: Basic Martial Weapons (1), Thrown Weapons (3)


Note: If the character selects an option and already has the maximum amount of any of the selected skills, the costs for those skills will be granted as free BP instead.
