Note: Life Points and Maximum Spikes can be found in the Level Progression Table in the Wellspring Core Rules.
The Unbinding
Due to the nature of the interaction with heavier armor and the Arcane Source of the Sourcerer’s magics, they are unable to use Astride the Weave or Packet-Delivery Spells if they are utilizing non-Barrier Armor (they can still use Cantrips).
Arcane Source
The Sourcerer’s Arcane Source takes the place of the Basic Arcane Spell Book. For this reason, skills and powers that require a spellbook are unavailable to Sourcerers and their Advanced Classes. Drawing from power deep within the fabric of the cosmos, the Sourcerer does not rely upon studied knowledge or divine faith to drive the weave of arcane forces at their disposal. Instead, tapping into an Arcane Source of magic, the Sourcerer simply draws upon the arcane strands to alter the world around them, and it obeys their command. While fundamental understanding of what the Arcane actually represents is a matter of constant research and debate, the power wielded by the talents of the Sourcerers is unmistakable.


The Sourcerer’s power is channeled through an object called an Arcane Source, and it is a crafted, physical object that the character must hold in their hand to use. An Arcane Source is created by various crafting skills, and incorporates ever more rare and exotic materials as the Source nears the pinnacle of its power. The Source itself is simply a conduit for the Sourcerer's power, and thus can be anything from a wand, orb, or ritual dagger to the skull of a former lover. Due to the Source’s volatile nature, weapons, shields, or other items required to be held in the hand to activate Powers cannot be used as a Source. The caster may hold no other Wielded items in the same hand as the Source.
Sourcerer Base Incantation


“I call upon the Weave Arcane, its boundless power, my domain,
With Strands compelled through wielded Source, I conjure bursts of Arcane force,
By my hand, my will afire, I now enact my heart’s desire,
With strength unleashed, unfettered soul, All is mine now to control…”
Sourcerer Innate Powers
Internal Battery [Innate]
Requirement: Sourcerer Level 1
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Long Rest


The Sourcerer can meditate and recenter their connection to magic’s undercurrents to rebuild focus and refresh their Arcane Spell-slots. The Sourcerer can spend a Short Rest communing with themselves, contemplating their Source, or practicing their incantations quietly.
 
The Sourcerer can perform this special study and refresh all of their expended Arcane spell-slots. If the Sourcerer has used any other Innate Power from another class to refresh their Arcane spell slots this long rest they may not use Internal Battery.


Additional benefits come if this study is done in an area of powerful arcane magic (such as an Arcane Locus), and performs a personal rite (determined by the Sourcerer). Doing so, the Sourcerer gains three additional Arcane Novice-tier Spell-Slots. These Spell-Slots last until the Sourcerer completes a Long Rest. Additional benefits may be possible (see the Skills, Perks, and Flaws document for more details).

Finally, if the Sourcerer has not worn non-Barrier armor at any point prior to the use of this Power this event they may once per Event refresh Internal Battery immediately. In doing so they lose the ability to wear non-Barrier armor for the remainder of the Event. 
Arcane Source [Innate]
Requirements: Sourcerer Level 1
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Immediate


The Sourcerer has an item (See Arcane Source, above) that is special to them. The character may begin with an object of their choice empowered as a Source. Arcane Sources must be a solid durable object at least 5” in one dimension and 1” in the others. 


After completing a Long Rest, a Sourcerer may empower a new object as their Source (the other one loses its Source properties). This Source must follow all rules defining an appropriate object. If that object is not an Empowered Source, it is attuned to one of the following Accents: Acid, Ice, Flame, or Lightning, chosen at the time of designation as a Source. This Chosen Accent is called their Arcane Accent. The Sorcerer may only have one Source at any one time, and this Source will only work for the Sourcerer who designated it.


However, the Source comes with some risk: should the Sourcerer find themself without an Arcane Source, they will lose access to all Sourcerer Spell-Slots and Known Spells until the Arcane Source is back in hand or a new object is designated (at a Long Rest, see above). Once per Short Rest, the Sourcerer may re-Empower a broken or shattered Arcane Source by performing a personal rite over the Source. If it was Shattered, the Source must first be Rebuilt.


Once the Sourcerer has reached 10th Sourcerer level, when empowering an Arcane Source, the Sourcerer may choose an additional Arcane Accent for the Source. While wielding the enhanced Source, the caster may use either Accent when the [Arcane Accent] is noted. When choosing this Arcane Accent for a given Power, all instances of the [Arcane Accent] must be the same Accent.


Note: The Sourcerer’s Arcane Source takes the place of the Basic Arcane Spellbook. The Sourcerer cannot use Powers or Skills that rely on a Spellbook for their Sourcerer Spells.
Astride the Weave [Innate]
Requirements: Sourcerer Level 2
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Immediate


The Sourcerer’s channeling of magic through the Arcane Source unbinds them from the traditional need to remain still while gathering power, allowing the arcane theurgist to move while incanting their Base Class: Sourcerer Cantrips and Spells. This delicate balance is lost if the character is wearing any non-Barrier Armor and only applies to Base Class Sourcerer Cantrips and Spells.


This ability does not override the specific requirements of the spell being cast, thus, if the specific spell itself calls for the caster to remain in place, or pivot on one foot, or any other requirement, the caster must abide by those restrictions.
Enduring Source [Innate]
Requirements: Sourcerer Level 3
Incantation: RP: Focus Quick 100 miming rebuilding the source.
Call: “Rebuild Source”
Target: Self                                         Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                          Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Rebuild


Once per Short Rest, the Sourcerer may Rebuild a broken or shattered Arcane Source.
Dissipate Curse [Innate]
Requirements: Sourcerer Level 5
Incantation: Focus Quick 30
Call: “Cure [Disarm, Disable, Root, Bind]”
Target: Self                                         Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                         Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                                        Effect: Cure


Once per Short Rest, the Sourcerer may Cure Condition [Disarm, Disable, Root, Bind] on themselves by Focusing for a Quick 30.
Power of Magic [Innate]
Requirements: Sourcerer Level 7
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Long Rest
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Long Rest


The Sourcerer’s channeling of magic through the Arcane Source allows them to make sacrifices other more traditional arcane casters cannot.


Upon completion of a Long Rest, the Sourcerer may Sacrifice a Cantrip that they know, other than Cancel, losing the ability to cast it until their next Long Rest. In exchange, the Sourcerer may select an Accent they have a Source for and add +1 damage to any spell (not Cantrip) cast with that accent, for a Long Rest.  


Note: In all cases, the normal stacking rules apply to these damage bonuses.
Rote Invocation [Innate]
Requirements: Sourcerer Level 10
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Long Rest
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: None                                                            Effect: Grant Signature Spell


The Sourcerer’s time harnessing the arcane has led to their ability to gradually intake mana from their environment, fuelling the spellwork with which they are most familiar. 


Once per Long Rest, you may designate a Sourcerer spell you know as a "Signature Spell" by expending a spell slot that could be used to cast that spell. Until your next Long Rest, you may cast your Signature Spell once per Short Rest as if you had spent the spell slot used to designate the spell. The spell may not have the Duration of Long Rest.
Sourcerer Class Skills
Additional Source [Class] - 4 BP 
Requirements: Sourcerer Level 4
Incantation: RP Short Rest to Create; Focus Quick 100 while altering your binding to a different Arcane Source. 
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Permanent
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Short Rest, Immediate


The Sourcerer has mastered the arts of Source manipulation such that they may have an additional Arcane Source bound.


After finishing a Short Rest linked to an object, the Sourcerer may designate a new item to become their Source in addition to their first. The Source must be aligned with one of the normal Accents that the Sourcerer can choose for Sources.


While a character must hold an Arcane Source in their hand to use its Accent, the character may switch between two different Arcane Sources that they are bound to with a Focus Quick 100.
Accent Substantiation [Class] - 1 BP (8)
Requirements: Sourcerer Level 4
Incantation: None
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None


The Sourcerer can bend the elemental forces of reality itself to mold to their purpose. Many cantrips and spells have optional modes or bonuses that only apply when the accent matches a specific requirement. This skill allows the Sourcerer to gain the benefits of these options with another Accent.


This skill may be purchased multiple times, each time allowing the Sourcerer to choose from the substitution list below.
Arcane Accent
	Substituted Effect
	Agony
	Acid
	Shadow
	Acid
	Force
	Flame
	Radiant
	Flame
	Fear
	Ice
	Void
	Ice
	Divine
	Lightning
	Mind
	Lightning
	

For Example: Trivian the Sourcerer is wielding his Arcane Source with the Agony accent. When casting a spell that has a special effect that benefits from the Acid Accent, the Agony Accent counts as the Acid Accent for purposes of that spell. Note that this does not change the accent of that spell. 
Unsuppressable [Class] - 4 BP 
Requirements: Sourcerer Level 5
Incantation: None
Call: “Concentration”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None


The Sourceror can not be interrupted while incanting by strikes or Effects, or defending from strikes or Effects. The Sourceror can still be interrupted by conditions that would prevent being able to incant, such as being Silenced, being rendered Helpless by damage taken, being Imprisoned, etc. Additionally, the Sourceror may call defenses retroactively after completion of the incantation, by calling “Concentration” followed by any relevant defenses.


Note that while this Power affects Incantations, it does not stop non-Incantation Focus requirements such as for Refresh or the cooldown time on various Spells.
Interlaced Shell [Class] - 3 BP 
Requirements: Sourcerer Level 6
Incantation: None
Call: “Grant 2 Barrier to Self”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Immediate
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Barrier


On the completion of a Short Rest the Sourcerer may grant themselves 2 Barrier by calling “Grant 2 Barrier to Self.” 


Cantrip Scholar [Class] - 4 BP
Requirement: Sourcerer Level 6


The Sourcerer learns an additional Cantrip from the Sourcerer Class Powers or Cantrips.
Faded Slumber [Class] - 1 BP
Requirement: Sourcerer Level 10
Incantation: None 
Call: “Grant Insubstantial”, “Cure Insubstantial”
Target: Self                                         Duration: Special
Delivery: None                                         Refresh: Immediate
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Insubstantial, Cure


The Sourcerer may post an information card on their bunk or bed, stating to others that they are insubstantial while in that bed. This Power can only be activated by Focusing while in the bed for a Slow Count of 60. It then lasts until the Sourcerer says “Cure Insubstantial” and takes down the card, or leaves the bed. 
Sourcerer Cantrips
Sourcerer Cantrip Incantation


“I call upon the Weave Arcane…”
Cancel [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: None
Target: Spell-Packet, Self                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                        Refresh: Immediate


This minor cantrip takes the power out of a Spell-Packet (or multiple Spell-Packets) that has already been charged and returns that power to the caster. The Spell-Packet becomes inert, and the caster refreshes the spell-slot that was used to charge the Spell-Packet. If the spell charges more than one Spell-Packet, all the Spell-Packets must still be charged in order to cancel the spell.
Charge [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Immediate
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Plus Damage


The first packet of the next Cantrip or Spell that the Sourcerer casts deals 1 more damage. 


Enhancement: If the Sourcerer has Warcaster and expends a Spike, all Spell-Packets charged from the next Cantrip or Spell the Sourcerer casts are affected by Charge instead of just the next one. (Reminder: Warcaster does not increase the damage of Cantrips.)
Circle of Four [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “1 [Acid/Flame/Ice/Lightning]”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Focus Quick 100
Accent: Acid/Flame/Ice/Lightning                Effect: Damage, Wounding


The Sourcerer calls forth a ring of elemental power that wreaths their hand in visible crackling energy. The caster charges four Spell-Packets, one with each of the four elemental Accents (Acid/Flame/Ice/Lightning). These can be thrown in any order.


 If the Sourcerer expends a Spike when throwing a charged Spell-Packet from this Cantrip, the Call is changed to “Wounding 2 by [Whichever Element is being Thrown]” for that charged Spell-Packet.
Wild Bolt [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “1 [Arcane Accent]”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Immediate
Accent: [Arcane Accent]                                Effect: Damage


This minor cantrip charges a Spell-Packet of materia with the Element of the Arcane Source’s Accent which will do 1 point of that Accent’s damage to those it hits. 


Enhancement: Expend a Spike: The Cantrip charges additional Spell-Packets equal to the Sourcerer’s Class-Level. 
Energy Shield [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Grant Protect vs [Arcane Accent]”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Until Used
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Focus Quick 100
Accent: [Arcane Accent]                                Effect: Grant Protect


The Sourcerer throws up a shield of energy to protect themself from the elements. This gives the caster one Protect from the Accent of their Arcane Source. If the Sourcerer has more than one Arcane Accent, they may have a Protect for each of those Accents. 


Infuriate [Cantrip]

Call: “Quick Provoke by [Arcane Accent]”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Focus Quick 100
Accent: [Arcane Accent]                                Effect: Provoke


A creature struck by this Spell-Packet suddenly sees the caster as an arch-enemy and hated nemesis.
Mass Flux [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Disarm Shield by [Arcane Accent]”
Target: Individual                                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Spell-Packet                                Refresh: Immediate
Accent: [Arcane Accent]                                Effect: Disarm


When the Sourcerer casts this Cantrip and expends a Spike, it changes the mass of the struck shield, pulling it to the ground for a moment. Unlike other Effects, if no duration is called, the duration of Disarm is Instantaneous, and the item may be picked up immediately upon hitting the ground. 


Synergistic Transfer [Cantrip]
Incantation: Standard
Call: None
Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                                  Refresh: Quick 100
Accent: None                                    Effect: Special


The Sourcerer is able to twist the Weave to their needs when pressed. By Sacrificing two Barrier, the Sourcerer is able to activate an optional power of another Cantrip, as if they expended a Spike.
Whispers of the Unseen [Cantrip]

Accent: [Arcane Accent]                                Effect: Discern


This power taps into the innate understanding of the flows of the magic common to Sourcerers. The Sourcerer may ask each of these questions of a target per cast, but must recast to repeat a question. 


                                 * What is [another] one of your Vulnerabilities?
                                 * What is [another] one of your Resistances?
                                 * What is your Spike Damage?

Spirit Link [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Grant Protect vs [Disable, Root, and Slow]”, “Counter, Spirit Link”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Long Rest
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Protect, Counter


This spell weaves the Sourcerer’s power over them, locking their Arcane Source to them. Until their next Short Rest, any time they are affected by any Disarm, Disable, or Obey Effect that would cause them to drop or give away their Source, they may expend a Spike to Counter it by calling “Counter, Spirit Link”. 


In addition, when the Spell is cast, the Sourcerer gains one Protect against Disable, Root, and Slow.

Aethereal Fade [Novice]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Short Grant Insubstantial by Shadow”, “Cure Insubstantial”
Target: Self                                         Duration: Short
Delivery: None                                         Refresh: Spell
Accent: Shadow                                        Effect: Grant Insubstantial, Cure


When this Spell is cast, the Sourcerer may enter a shadowed area, and become Insubstantial. While the character remains in shadow, they may walk or stand still as they please. They may not cross an unshadowed area or jump across unshadowed areas. If the caster allows themselves to be struck by sunlight, moonlight, torchlight, or other sources of light, the Spell immediately ends. At any time, the caster may call “Cure Insubstantial to Self” to end the effect.

Enhancement: Adept Spell-Slot: Two additional Spell-Packets are charged.

Breach Teleport [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Short Grant Insubstantial to Self”, “Cure Insubstantial to Self”
Target: Self                                        Duration: Short, Special
Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Spell
Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Insubstantial, Cure


This spell must be cast upon a Spell-Packet that has been marked with a symbol that is special to the caster. The incantation charges the Spell-Packet which is then thrown. As soon as it comes to rest, the Sourcerer will become insubstantial and must either move as quickly and directly as possible to the spot where the Spell-Packet came to rest, or end the spell immediately. They may not walk part way and then end the spell. 


Once see the Sourcerer arrives at the appropriate spot, they should call loudly “Cure Insubstantial to Self”. The Sourcerer can do nothing besides move to the Spell-Packet’s location while Insubstantial. Note that if the Spell-Packet hits an individual, the caster should inform the struck person that the Spell-Packet has no effect on them.

Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Spell

This Spell’s effects cannot be altered other than as listed in this Spell’s description.


Enhancement: Expend up to four Spikes: Increase the damage of this Spell by two per Spike expended.
Enhancement: Greater Spell-Slot: The Piercing Effect is added to the damage.


Note: The Source is irrevocably destroyed in the process of casting this spell, and should be turned into the Ash Bin as soon as possible. It may not be Rebuilt.

Spirit Chalice [Adept]
Incantation: Standard
Call: “Grant Spirit Chalice”, “Death to Helpless by [Arcane Accent]”, “Refresh 1 Spike”
Target: Self, Dying Individual                        Duration: Short Rest, Instantaneous
Delivery: None, Touch                                Refresh: Spell, Immediate
Accent: [Arcane Accent]                                Effect: Grant Power, Death, Refresh


Until the end of the Sourcerer’s next Short Rest, they have the ability to use the death of others to power their magic. For the duration, the Sourcerer can touch a creature who is Helpless and cause it to die. The call for this death-touch is “Death to Helpless by [Arcane Accent].” If this is not Prevented, the Sourcerer may Refresh one Spike. This can be done as often as desired during the duration of the spell.


Note: This is a Touch Spell and thus can only be used on the Helpless. It may be necessary to let the player/portrayer of the target know that they are being targeted by a Touch Spell if their eyes are closed or they are otherwise insensible.
