Devotion Accents
Some powers reference a Devotion Accent. When a character takes the Worship skill, they choose a single divine domain in their purchase list (i.e. they can purchase domain Powers in), and gain that as a Devotion Accent. After the first, the character additionally gains a Devotion Accent for any domain the character has Powers purchased in. The character’s devotion accents can then be used whenever a Power calls for a [Devotion Accent]. 


Example: Fru, the Wildfire Druid, starts the game at level 4, and does not purchase any divine domain Powers, but decides that the two domains she will purchase from are Energy: Flame, and Nature. Fru then decides that her first “free” devotion Accent will come from Energy: Flame - granting her the Flame devotion accent. Next game Fru decides to purchase domain Powers in Nature. She now has both Flame and Poison as available Devotion Accents. The next game Fru levels up and decides to change her domain powers to Destruction and Nature. Now Fru has access to the Acid and Poison devotion accents, but has lost access to Flame since it is no longer one of their chosen divine domains. 


Divine Domain
	Devotion Accent
	Chaos
	Lightning
	Creation
	Flame
	Death
	Shadow

Chaos


The Path Less Travelled - 1 BP
Call: “Grant Power Swap, [explanation]”
Target: Individual (Willing)                Duration: Event
Delivery: Touch                        Refresh: Event
Accent: None                                     Effect: Special


Once per event, the character can choose a Willing target and have them swap out a martial Power or Known Spell they have access to for another Power or Known Spell of the same Tier and class. If swapping a martial Power, it must be unexpended at the time of swap. These changes last until the end of the event.


Disruption [Adept] - 2 BP
Incantation: “Calling upon chaos unbounded, I leave my foe lost and confounded”
Call: “Quick Drain by Lightning”
Target: Individual                        Duration: Quick 100
Delivery: Weapon or Materia                Refresh: Spell
Accent: Lightning                        Effect: Drain


The character disrupts a target’s natural processes with a jolt of chaotic energy. The target is drained for a Quick 100. 


The first time this spell is cast each long rest, it will not consume a spell slot. 


Dissolution - 2 BP
Once per short rest, whenever the character uses an attack with an Accent, they may spend one Spike to change the Accent to another Accent of their choice. They may spend an additional Spike to refresh this power immediately. If the attack lands and is not Prevented, they may then use the following Power: 
Untangle Weakness
Call: “Discern by Divine: Are you Vulnerable to [Accent of the previous attack]?”
Target: Individual                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Verbal                Refresh: Special
Accent: Divine                  Effect: Discern


Viewing the impact of their attack, the character may determine if the target is weak to the wielded Accent.
	







Creation


Inspired Hands - 1 BP
The character may reduce all hourglass turns involved in a crafting process by ½, stacking with all other reductions. If a single hourglass turn has already been reduced to a shorter Count from another source, this Power reduces the Count by an additional ½. All other repeated actions are reduced by half (rounded up).


Waste Not - 3 BP
Call: “Mend 2 by Divine”
Target: Individual                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                Refresh: Instantaneous
Accent: Divine                        Effect: Mend


The character may spend a Spike to Mend 2 points of a character’s armor.


Spontaneous Creation - 2 BP
Once per event, the character may complete an action that expends a Resource without actually consuming one of the required Resources. Valid Resources are ones generated via Prospecting, Scavenging, or Foraging (except for Golden Blossom Seeds), or an Essence/Essence Infusion (except for Unravelling Essence).


Example: Chome, a Song in Iron enchanter, is creating an Arcane Bracelet, which requires 3 Ingots, 2 Rare Minerals, 2 Night Prizes, and 1 Essence. Chome may use this ability to reduce the crafting requirements by 1 Rare Mineral. 


Death


Death Pact - 3 BP 


The character becomes incapable of delivering healing of any kind (including self healing), and cannot deliver the Revive effect. All single-target, numeric healing Spells and Cantrips are converted to damage with an Accent of Divine or [Devotion Accent]. The healing restriction of this power does not affect the The Balance of Life power. Healing Spells with Touch delivery are converted to Packet delivery. For the purposes of this power, Healing Touch counts as a cantrip, but additionally counts as casting a spell for the purpose of Mana Sickness. The damage done by these spells is converted from healing to damage before anything else is applied - this means increases to numerical damage may increase the damage done, but increases to healing do not.


The Balance of Life - 4 BP 
Incantation: "I call my faith, divine and right, to knit your wounds with dying light."
Call: “Heal 1 by Divine” or “Heal [X] by Divine”
Target: Individual (Other)                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Touch                                Refresh: Special
Accent: Divine                                        Effect: Heal


The character gains a "Balance Pool" with starting points of 0 and maximum points of 5. Whenever the character delivers a Death Effect or Deathblow to a target and it is not prevented, they gain one point of Balance Pool. One point of Balance Pool can be spent to deliver "Heal 1 by Divine" via Touch using the above incant.


If the character already has a Healing Touch Pool, this pool instead stacks with Healing Touch pool, and can be spent in the same way (Spend and Heal up to X per cast, where X is the character's Cleric level.). Gaining points of Balance Pool via Death/Deathblows cannot exceed the Healing Touch Pool's starting value of 3 * Cleric Level.


If the character also has Death Pact, the Balance Pool must be tracked separately from any existing Healing Touch pool and still has a maximum of 5. It can be used for either Healing or Damage but has the same restriction of 1 Point spent per cast.


Note: Spending Balance Pool does not trigger Peacecaster.


Purposed to Death - 1 BP 


Once per event, the character may perform a Rite over a corpse that lasts at least 30 seconds. They may then speak to that corpse’s willing Spirit on their way to the Spirit River to Purpose them to Death. If the Spirit encounters any creature on their path through the Spirit River and tells them “I have willingly been Purposed to Death,” then the Spirit may be respected in some significant way by whomever, or whatever, they find. 


Note: Purposed to Death’s effects are being kept intentionally vague until the full ramifications of death are discovered by the players. 
Destruction


Dissolve - 4 BP
Call: “Shatter [Target] by [Force or Divine]”
Target: Object                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Weapon or Materia        Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: Force                        Effect: Shatter


The character calls upon their devotion to the forces of destruction to dissolve an item, Shattering it. They may spend 2 Spikes to immediately Refresh this power.


Pyrrhic Strike - 2 BP
Call: “Wounding 10 Final by Force”
Target: Individual                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Melee Weapon        Refresh: Event
Accent: Force                        Effect: Wounding, Final


Once per event, the character may deliver one weapon swing of “Wounding 10 Final by Force”. The user is then immediately dealt 10 points of Final damage by Force.


Heartseeker - 4 BP
When delivering damage with “Wounding” in the Call, the character increases the damage by 1. (Note that this is not Base damage and is subject to stacking rules - see Stacking Effects in the Core Rules.)




Energy: [Acid, Flame, Ice, or Lightning]


Energistic Specialization - 1 BP 
Whenever the character casts a Spell or uses a Power with an existing Accent, they may substitute the Accent of that Call with the domain’s Accent. 


Example: if the domain they purchased this ability in is Energy: Flame, they may cast Arcane Barrage, which normally has the accent of Force, with the accent of Flame. 


Overflowing Energy - 3 BP
Call: “Grant Plus 1 Damage by [Energy Accent]”
Target: Self                        Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: None                Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: [Energy Accent]        Effect: Grant Plus Damage


Once per Long Rest, the character may increase their damage dealt by Spells (not Cantrips) or Powers by one, as long as it uses their Energy Accent. This buff lasts until the character completes a Short Rest. (Note that this is not Base damage and is subject to stacking rules - see Stacking Effects in the Core Rules.)


Energistic Adaptation - 2 BP
Call: “Long Grant Resistance to [Energy Accent]”
Target: Self                        Duration: Long Rest
Delivery: None                Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: None                        Effect: Grant Resistance

Upon activating this power, the character will call “Resist” against their Energy Accent. When they have called “Resist” from this Power 3 times, the Resistance is removed until the character’s next long rest.






Expression


Emotional Attunement - 1 BP
Call: “Subtle Discern by Mind: What is your current strongest emotion?”
Target: Individual                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Verbal                Refresh: Focus Quick 100
Accent: Mind                        Effect: Discern


The character has learned how to tap into the emotions of those around them, and may discern their current emotional status. This ability is often used to enhance performances and tailor their crafts to their audience. 


Divine Performance - 2 BP
Incant: RP - artistic expression (see description)
Call: “[Explanation]”
Target: Up to 6 other individuals        Duration: Long
Delivery: Verbal                        Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: Divine                                Effect: Grant, Imbue


Once per Long Rest, the character may perform an act of expression (singing, dancing, drawing, etc.) with up to 6 other characters for a minimum of 1 minute. Upon completion, the character may deliver up to 2 of the following to each participant:
                                 * “Grant Protect vs Mind”
                                 * “Imbue Touch with Heal 1 to Dying”
                                 * “Imbue Cure [Condition, chosen when Imbued] to Self”
                                 * “Grant 3 Barrier”


Poetry in Motion - 2 BP
Call: “Counter, Poetry in Motion”
Target: Self                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                        Effect: Counter


Once per short rest, the character may Counter a Silence, Sleep, Root, or Disable effect targeting them.




Life


Expanded Healing - 2 BP  
The character gains a Healing Touch pool equal to their character level, divided by two (rounded down). See Cleric for the rules around using Healing Touch pool. This stacks with other sources of Healing Touch pool. 


Sworn to Life - 4 BP 
The character becomes incapable of dealing damage, or causing death directly. 


All healing done by Spells and Cantrips is increased by 1 (this is a Base increase and is not subject to stacking limits). This does not increase the healing done by Healing Touch, Life Tap, or Balance of Life.


Additionally, once per long rest the character may refresh the per Long Rest limitation of a Revive power for each Revive power they possess. This does not refresh the original use of the ability or refresh spell slots.


Lifeline - 3 BP
Call: “Imbue: Revive to Self by Divine”
Target: Individual                Duration: Event
Delivery: Touch                Refresh: Event
Accent: None, Divine                Effect: Imbue
Once per event, the character may target an individual and Imbue them with the ability to Revive themselves while in the Dead state. This Imbue does not automatically end when a character becomes Dead, unlike other Effects and Conditions.


Light


No Stone Unturned - 3 BP
Call: “Obvious Discern by Divine: Was any part of what you just said a deception?”
Target: Individual                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Verbal                Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: Divine                        Effect: Discern


The character can call upon light to illuminate falsehoods and expose deceit. This effect works even when the target is attempting to lie by omission. 


Bring Light to the Shadows - 3 BP
Call: “By My Voice to Insubstantial, Dispel Insubstantial by Divine”
Target: Insubstantial individuals        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Voice                        Refresh: Event
Accent: Divine                                Effect: Dispel


The character can call upon a massive burst of divine light that dispels those who would otherwise be hard to target or find.


Pulse of Light - 1 BP
Call: “By My Voice to Anyone Hiding, Expose”
Target: Anyone Hiding                Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: Voice                        Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: Divine                                Effect: Expose


The character pulses the area with revealing light, exposing anyone who is attempting to hide. Ambushing bandits and fleeing refugees alike have all been caught out by this power. If the character has an Adept rank divine spell-slot, they may spend it to immediately refresh this ability.
Knowledge


Well Read - 1 BP
Prerequisite: Lore (any)
Once per event, the character may act as if they had any necessary Lore skill for a specific requirement on an information card. Additionally, they may passively read the inside of any item information card as if they had cast the Identify spell (note that this does not apply to Divine Relics).


Arcane Secrets - 3 BP
Upon purchasing this domain Power, the character may choose one arcane spell at a rank they are capable of casting, and add it to their Known Spells. This spell does not need to be from an arcane spellcasting class they already have access to. If they do not have any Known Spells, choose a single spell that an equivalent level caster could cast based on total level, up to Adept. They may cast this spell a single time per long rest without expending a spell slot. 


Divine Symposium - 2 BP 
Once per event, the character may adjust the Beckon ritual to call upon a divine creature of Knowledge to speak about a specific topic. (See the Rituals document for the rules to cast the Beckon ritual.) During the Beckon ritual, instead of a message being sent, the character must instead submit a Thesis. A Thesis is a document posing the question they are looking for divine aid in researching, as well as a summary of all the information currently known about the topic, and should include the name of the person submitting it. A well structured and fleshed-out Thesis will result in better, more specific help. After submitting the Thesis, a divine creature of Knowledge will seek out the character who submitted the Thesis. The time it takes them to respond will depend on staff resources, as well as the complexity of the topic. Divine creatures of Knowledge will not wholesale replace the need for research, but will help clarify understanding, will offer some tidbits of interesting information, and will often help in what the next steps may be. Topics that are very specific, and less advanced will yield much better results. Questions such as “what is the nature of the universe” will yield almost no results at all. While the divine creature of knowledge is in a way bound to help the character when this ability is used, they may be deceptive, and may use the questions they asked in ways that may not be beneficial to the character who originally asked for help. Research as to exactly which divine creature of Knowledge they should call upon may be helpful. 


Example: Tales are told far and wide of Anthon, a scholar bound to The Librarian who called upon a Divine Symposium without specifying which creature was Beckoned. The Page Scrawler answered his call and helped him research a specific Relic he believed would help understand the true nature of the Divine - three weeks later Anthon disappeared and was never seen again. 


Manipulation


Mind Wipe - 3 BP 
Incantation: RP - Focus Slow 300
Call: “[Explanation]”
Target: Helpless individual                        Duration: Permanent
Delivery: Touch                                Refresh: Event
Accent: None                                        Effect: Special


Once per Event, the character may spend five uninterrupted minutes with a Helpless target focusing on their mind and memory, and Call “Instruction to Helpless: forget the last one hour of your memory, filling in any gaps with something reasonable to the character.” The target will not have obvious amnesia, but will remember something at their discretion that would replace what was removed.


Subtlety - 3 BP
Once per long rest, the character may add the Subtle modifier to a Call of a Power.


Dominating Presence - 2 BP 
Incant: RP - friendly conversation with a target for Slow 60
Call: “[Name or Description], Subtle Short Charm by Divine” or “[Name or Description], Subtle Short Dominate by Divine”
Target: Individual                Duration: Short Rest
Delivery: Verbal                Refresh: Long Rest, Event
Accent: Divine                        Effect: Charm, Dominate


Once per Long Rest, after having a friendly conversation with a target lasting at least a Slow 60 count, the character may deliver a Verbal “Subtle Short Charm by Divine” to that target. Once per event, if the target is already under the Charmed condition originating from the character, the character may instead deliver a verbal “Subtle Short Dominate by Divine.”
Nature


Nature’s Form - 2 BP 
Once per event, the character gains the ability to cast one use of a Druid Form spell without spending a spell slot. The spell’s tier is either Novice or at any Tier the character has spell-slots in, whichever is higher. Being under the effects of a Form by using this Domain Power does not prevent you from benefiting from the "No Form" Enhancements of Druid spells. If the character has a Form spell in their list of Known Spells, the Form spell gained from this ability counts as if it has the “Flexible Form” Druid class skill.


Moving Grove - 3 BP 
While the character has the Plant type, any other character touching them counts as being in their Grove, even if they do not have a physical Grove themselves. Additionally, anyone in the character’s Grove may cast spells while in a Form. While the character has the Plant type or is in their Grove, they may expend a Spike to increase the healing of a Spell or Power by the character’s base Spike damage. 


Tree Teleport - 3 BP
Call: “Grant Insubstantial to Self”, “Cure Insubstantial”
Target: Self                        Duration: Special
Delivery: None                Refresh: Short Rest, Event
Accent: None                        Effect: Grant Insubstantial, Cure Insubstantial


Once per short rest, while touching a living tree, or an ally with the Plant type, the character may Grant Insubstantial to themselves. They must then choose a different tree within approximately 300 paces, and immediately move at a minimum of a walking pace towards that tree. As soon as they reach the new tree, they must immediately Cure Insubstantial on themselves.


Additionally, this Power may be used once per event to move from a tree to any Grove known to them, whose Druid is friendly towards them.


Order


Orderly Conduct - 2 BP
Call: “Grant Protect vs [Berserk, Disarm, or Shatter]”
Target: Individual                Duration: Short
Delivery: Touch                Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: Divine                        Effect: Grant Protect


The character can resist attempts at disorder and destruction, and is skilled at bestowing that gift to others. 


Create Compact - 1 BP
Once per event, the character can create a written agreement between two willing individuals. If the contract is broken, a penalty is immediately exacted upon the violator, and the contract ends. The penalty can be anything up to immediate death and discorporation and is established in the contract. The contract must be very specific in what it does or does not allow, and any unclear rules will be decided in the direction that favors the potential violator. A contract cannot be established with someone who is not of sound mind, or in complete control of their faculties. Players who have entered into this contract with each other should do their best to self-adjudicate this process and follow the contract in good faith to the best of their ability. Event staff will adjudicate in the case of a dispute, but consistent contract disputes that could have been self-resolved will result in this ability being limited or taken away. 


Locate - 3 BP
Once per event the character can cast a modified version of the Beckon ritual, even if they do not have the Journeyman Ritual Magic skill. (See the Rituals document for the rules to cast the Beckon ritual.) Instead of sending a message or contacting a target, the Beckon ritual will instead provide an item or person’s current location. The more first hand experience or information the character using this ability has on the target, the better it works. This spell has a range of roughly 100 miles, which may be modified up or down by the power and performance of the ritual. The time it takes for Locate results to be delivered is dependent on Staff resources.




Peace


Non-Aggression Pact - 1 BP
Incant: RP - 1 minute of forming truce
Call: “Instruction to all who agreed to the Pact: Until the next long rest, all participants of this Pact may choose to be Immune to any damage or Effect delivered by another participant.”
Target: Individual or group        Duration: Long Rest
Delivery: Special                Refresh: Event
Accent: None                        Effect: Special


Once per event, the character may roleplay for at least one minute with a target or group of targets, and put them under a pact to do no harm. Each participant in the pact must be clearly visible to every other participant. The character must verbally offer the terms: "Do you swear to the Divine to do no harm to those entering this pact?"  All participants must then indicate their intent to agree by holding their hand up, palm facing outward, while verbally agreeing. Once these steps are complete, the character may call “Instruction to all who agreed to the Pact: Until the next long rest, all participants of this Pact may choose to be Immune to any damage or Effect delivered by another participant.”


Sworn to Peace - 3 BP
The character must always seek reconciliation between opposing parties whenever possible. The character gains Inherent Resistance to Berserk, and may spend a Spike to deliver a “Cure Berserk by Divine” via Weapon or Packet on another target individual. If the character is dominated and given a command to harm another individual, they may immediately call “Cure Dominated to Self”.


Aura of Peace - 1 BP
The character may deliver verbally to a target "[Name or Description], Short Obey by Divine: believe that I mean you no harm.” In order to use this Power, the character must genuinely mean the target no harm. This Power refreshes on a Quick 100. If the character clearly does harm to the target, this effect will end.


Protection


Protection Mastery - 4 BP 
Once per long rest, when the character delivers a beneficial Power with a Target of "Individual" to someone else, they may make the same Call again with the delivery changed to “to Self”. This may not be used on any Call containing a Refresh effect.


Selfless - 4 BP
Once per event, the character may change the target of a single Power with the duration of Until Used, Short Rest, or Long Rest from Self to Individual, and change the delivery to Touch. It is the job of the person using this ability to fully explain the effects of the Power to the target. Powers modified this way cannot be combined with Protection Mastery.


Unbreakable - 3 BP
Call: “Counter, Unbreakable”
Target: Self                        Duration: Instantaneous
Delivery: None                Refresh: Event
Accent: None                        Effect: Counter


Once per event, the character may Counter any single Effect targeting them without the Final modifier. 


Shadow


Slippery Tongue - 1 BP 
Call: “Short Obfuscate by Divine”
Target: Self                        Duration: Short
Delivery: None                        Refresh: Long Rest
Accent: Divine                        Effect: Obfuscate


The character can call upon divine shadow to aid in their obfuscation of truth. 


Shadow Step - 4 BP
Call: “Grant Insubstantial to Self”, “Cure Insubstantial”
Target: Self                        Duration: Slow 100
Delivery: None                Refresh: Short Rest
Accent: None                        Effect: Grant Insubstantial, Cure Insubstantial


Once per short rest, the character may step into a shadow large enough to hide their own shadow, and become Insubstantial for a Slow 100. They may then Cure Insubstantial to self either at the end of the Slow 100, or at any time prior if they are in a shadow large enough to hide their own shadow. 

Broodlord - 3 BP
While in a dark corner or in complete darkness, the character may complete a short rest in 3 minutes instead of 5 minutes. Being outside at night, so long as they are not illuminated by any light source, counts as complete darkness. 
War


Severing Strikes - 3 BP 
When the character delivers a Death effect or a Deathblow and it is not Prevented, they gain +1 Spike damage. This bonus lasts until they cause a target to enter the Dying state.


Death to Cowards - 3 BP 
Whenever the character strikes a target from the front with any attack, and the target uses their next attack against a target other than the character, the character deals +1 damage on the next attack against that target. When this Power is triggered, the character must say audibly “Death to Cowards!”


Violent Posturing- 3 BP 
Once per short rest, the character may call “[Name or description], Subtle Discern: what’s the highest damage you’re capable of dealing?” If that target responds with a number greater than the character’s own, and the character enters into combat with that target in the same scene, the character must verbally deliver “[Name or description], Short Provoke by Spite”, and the character must loudly verbally challenge the target to combat. If the character brings that target to 0 Life Points before the end of the combat, the character may Refresh 3 Spikes. If the target brings the character to 0 Life Points before the end of the combat, the character must immediately deliver a Verbal “[Name or description], Refresh all Spikes by Spite” to the target before entering the Dying state.
________________


Wellspring Economy Overview
Wealth
While the boundaries between civilizations are thick with animosity and blood - money always finds a way. Though most civilizations boast their own official sources of coin, the shared nature of life around the Wellspring has led to a standard currency that is accepted almost universally (and often with much grumbling), and can buy all manner of things - both legal and illegal. 


Copper Hexagon, “Copper” - 1 Wealth
Silver Circle, “Silver” - 5 Wealth
Gold Oval, “Gold” - 10 Wealth
Green Arch, “Greens” - 50 Wealth




Auros Starting Wealth
In the recent past, the need for supplies led Auros to use a barter system of resources, but after their arrival at the Wellspring, they will begin to use the universal currency going forward as the pressures of trade and economies weave their way across the border. 


To reflect this, at check-in, starting Aurosian characters may choose to receive their first game’s Wealth either in coin, or in Basic Resources. NOTE: The resources will be diversified and cannot be chosen individually, and will reflect the merchant prices of the resources (see below).


Typical Merchant Prices
Below is a typical selection of items that may be for sale and their average prices in the world.


NOTE: These prices include a markup, and will always be more expensive than the base values of the items gathered or crafted in-game - keep this in mind when trading with other player characters. In addition, NPC merchants will not always be available. 


Some character skills or Powers may affect the merchant markup, as well as other in-game circumstances. 


Basic Resource (Bloom, Ingot, Hide)                                2 for 3 Wealth
	Uncommon Resource (Night Prize, Rare Mineral, Harvest)        3 Wealth
	Mithril Ore                                                         10 Wealth
	Raw Scale                                                        10 Wealth
	Ritual Wand                                                        9 Wealth
	Ritual Powder                                                        18 Wealth
	Golden Blossom Seed                                                30 Wealth
	Essence Infusion                                                35 Wealth
	Other Crafted Items                                                Calculated based on components
	



________________


Crafting (all)
Crafting - Table of Contents
Alchemy
Enchanting
Tinkering


Introduction
Crafting is the process of creating items with in-game effects. In order for a character to perform the crafting process, they will require the appropriate Skill (Alchemy, Enchanting, or Tinkering). There are three tiers of crafting Skills: Apprentice, Journeyman, and Greater. Having a higher tier crafting skill allows a character to use the available recipes of that tier.
Recipes/Formulae/Schematics
All recipes, formulae, and schematics listed in this document are common knowledge. They will be accessible to all characters in-game at their respective Crafting Stations: The Hermetic Laboratory will have recipes for Alchemy, The Enchanting Forge will have formulae for Enchanting, and the Tinker's Workshop will have schematics for Tinkering.
Other, rarer recipes may be found through play.
Recipes in this document will have the following elements:
Crafting Materials
This lists what Crafting Materials or Resources are required in order to make the recipe successfully.
Uses Per Batch
If the process creates more than one use of a recipe, then it will be noted here.
Expiration
This lists how long the item will last after creation. Note: if an Enchantment is being applied to an item, the enchantment’s expiration cannot exceed the base item’s expiration.
Crafting Process
This is the actual description of how the item is created: the recipe itself. These listed steps must be roleplayed at a crafting station, using the required tools. 

If the ritual instructions direct any involved characters to grind up, burn, or otherwise vandalize any in-game resources, characters are encouraged to simply roleplay the action rather than destroying the resource props used; however, providing alternative items to enhance the roleplay (such as powders to represent ground-up resources or lighting pieces of paper safely on fire to represent burning a resource) is highly encouraged. 


Any use of fire should be safely contained within a bowl or other container to prevent accidents, and should only be used in fire-safe locations (any location that is primarily covered in dirt, stone, or cement with no nearby grass, and not indoors); furthermore, fire should never be left unattended. If the location is not fire-safe, or any fire hazard warnings are in effect, just use a representation of fire. 
All referenced phys-reps will be provided by staff, but players are welcome to bring additional props to enhance their roleplay as long as the structure of the process is followed. 
If any part of the crafting process is not followed, the process must either be immediately restarted, or it can be abandoned. If it is restarted, the Crafting Materials are not used up, but the process must start again immediately. If abandoned, all Crafting Materials that would have been used in the recipe are rendered useless and ruined. If the crafter takes or deals damage, takes an Effect, leaves the general area of the crafting station, or completes a Rest, this is considered an interruption, and the crafting process must be restarted or abandoned.
Turn of the Hourglass
Many instructions say to do something for a “turn”; this means one turn of the provided hourglass, which should take approximately one minute.
Item Cards
There are item cards provided at each crafting station where crafters may notate what they create and the properties of that item. It is entirely up to the crafter whether they wish to use the cards, but without an appropriate card, the item will only be usable by the crafter themself and cannot be handed off to others, including other crafters of the same type.
Ashbin
Any spent, consumed or destroyed materials or item cards should be placed in an “ashbin”, a designated container provided by staff nearby the crafting station. 
Description
The description is the in-game view of the item. It may contain history, flavor text, or even physical descriptions of the item. The description says what the crafting character (rather than the player) knows about the item being made.
For items that require attunement, attunement to the item should not occur without a physical representation of the item.
Effect
This is where the exact mechanical and role-playing effects of the item are explained.
Dark Territory
Most crafting recipes, and procedures, have a range of tested and understood parameters. Researchers, crafters, and ritualists, however, are often drawn outside the bounds of known territory. This exploration outside of the normal bounds of knowledge is represented as Dark Territory. Often expansions into Dark Territory end in tears, and if lucky, a little bit of knowledge - but sometimes, just sometimes, Dark Territory can lead to extreme successes that defy logic. 
If a recipe lists “Ritualists,” the requirement to count as a Ritualist is the appropriate rank of the Crafting skill (rather than the Ritual Magic skill).
If a recipe lists “Dark Territory Required: Optional”, that means that the crafting process may be marshaled by the participating player(s) themselves and has no inherent risk of Dark Territory, unless a player chooses to intentionally add it (and a Marshal is available).
All crafting attempts that include Dark Territory require an appropriate Marshal. For rules on how to resolve Dark Territory, see the Rituals document: 
Crafting Resources List
There are six main crafting Resources that players can obtain. Each of these make up the building blocks of most crafting that characters will do.
There are also four advanced resources that are used in the creation of highly advanced items. These resources are rarely found in their finished state and usually must be refined using different crafting schematics or processes.
Bloom (Basic)
Usually a small bundle of organic flora such as herbs or flowers. Obtained through the Forage skill. Usually represented by a small bundle of herbs tied with yellow twine or ribbon.
Hide (Basic)
Hide can be carved with skill off of an animal caught in a trap, or hunted from around the wilderness. Hide is obtained through the Scavenging skill. Usually represented by a small scrap of leather about 2" x 2."
Ingots (Basic)
These are metal nuggets scrounged from old ruins, panned from rivers, or perhaps chipped out of the living rock inside small pit mines. Colloquially, all these sources are simply referred to as ‘Mines’. Mines can have a number of dangers associated with them, but can also be very profitable. Ingots are Gathered through the Prospect skill. Usually represented by a rough metallic nugget.
Night Prizes (Uncommon)
Small fungi, often bioluminescent. When foraged, Night Prizes may occasionally have a card attached that can grant additional effects which may be positive or negative. Obtained through the Forage skill. Usually represented by small mushrooms tied with blue yarn or ribbon.
Harvest (Uncommon)
Harvest is the fangs, teeth, bones, and offal of creatures. These gruesome and often magical anatomical parts are obtained by Scavenging monsters. Gathering Harvest can sometimes have negative effects, such as disease, poisoning, and more. Harvest is obtained through the Scavenging skill. Usually represented by fangs, teeth, or bones.
Rare Minerals (Uncommon)
Collected from deposits around the countryside, Rare Minerals might be sulfur, salts, or other similar materials. Rare Minerals are often associated with more significant danger than other Resources. Rare Minerals are Gathered through the Prospect skill. Usually represented by small vials of salts or crystals.
Golden Blossom (Advanced)
Golden Blossoms are usually obtained by growing them in a Lifepot (created with Tinkering), a special vessel imbued with life energy. It takes an extended period of growth from Golden Blossom Seeds to create a Golden Blossom flower using a Lifepot. See more on Lifepots in the Tinkering section.
Raw Scale (Advanced)
Raw Scales are obtained oftentimes by hunting down and defeating powerful and dangerous beings from the land. Once the Raw Scale is obtained, it must be refined using an Alchemical Process called Cure Raw Scale.
Mithril Bar (Advanced)
Requiring eight pieces of Raw Mithril Ore (which is very rarely obtained through Prospecting), it needs to be refined at a Tinker's Workshop through a process called Smelt Mithril Ore.
Enchanted Hyperium (Advanced)
Enchanted Hyperium is a constructed resource, with a number of processes to refine and craft it. The final result is a silvery-white powder after having been alchemically purified from the raw ore, subjected to high heat by tinkering, and then magically enchanted.
Named Resources
The Basic and Advanced Resources above are not the only items that can be required for crafting. Certain recipes may call for a “named” resource. Often, Named Resources will require investigation, danger, and a quest or task to obtain.
Alchemy


Some tap into the weave of ley lines to produce magical effects. Others rely on skill, training, or physical prowess. The alchemists rely on the science of chemistry, and often more than a little magic, to create a wide variety of potions, elixirs, unguents, and treatments that can provide nigh-miraculous effects. Through careful process, alchemists ply their trade, turning plants and other materials into wondrous concoctions.
Aside from the standard information, Alchemy recipes have the following extra field:
Application
This describes how the substance is to be used. 
Quaff
Quaff means the character must mime drinking the liquid for at least three swallows, or actually drink three swallows (or the entire vial, whichever is less). Potion props should include a container and a representation of contents (such as green resin in a test tube) or actual liquid content. An unconscious or unresisting character can be given a potion by someone else, and it will take about the same time. Trying to force a potion into an actively resisting character is considered to be always unsuccessful. 
The proffering character should mime giving it to the drinker, keeping in mind contact and consent rules. Quaffed substances can always be taken/mixed with food or drink, but giving such a mixture to someone who is unaware must follow the same procedure as using a poison on another (see below). A quaffed substance is always considered ingested. 
Topical
Topical means that the character should mime rubbing it on the target, whether that be a weapon, trap, food, drink, or a person. Please keep in mind that no actual touching is allowed without prior express consent. If the substance is placed in food or drink, it is considered ingested, otherwise it is considered topical. 
Only trained alchemists or characters that have taken the Poisoner skill may apply topical substances (by any method). Putting a topical substance in drink requires that a poison card be attached to the drink and the effects of the substance must be written on the card. Each Topical preparation will also list what happens when it is ingested (usually a more potent effect). Applying a preparation to food takes exactly as much time as it does to place the card on the drink.
Ingest
Ingest means the character must mime eating the food for at least three bites or actually eat a piece that is at least 1” x 1." 
Component
Component means that it creates another physical item that is used as part of another crafting process. 
The Hermetic Laboratory
There will be a designated game space set up as a Hermetic Laboratory. This is where characters will craft all of their alchemy items. The items that will be there and used in different recipes are the following:
                                 * Large flasks/beakers/glassware to perform tests in
                                 * Small vials for final products
                                 * Antique Scale
                                 * Mortar and Pestle 
                                 * Hourglass (1 minute)
                                 * Small Flame of some sort, candle or otherwise
                                 * Retort
                                 * Simple Knife for cutting
                                 * Cutting Board
                                 * Gloves
                                 * Tongs (for holding vials over heat)
                                 * Ashbin 
                                 * Simple Salts (with an OOG bag for refilling nearby)
                                 * Alchemical Suspension (with an OOG container for refilling nearby)
                                 * Water (with an OOG container for refilling nearby)
                                 * Eye dropper
Corrupted Alchemy
Some methods of alchemical creation tap into less than savory aspects of the world, leading down darker paths. These recipes, whether Tainted by poisonous ingredients or incorporating more vile elements, Corrupted Alchemy offer unparalleled power, with a superlative cost.
Effects of Corrupted Alchemy
After using a substance that is created by Corrupted Alchemy, after completing their next Long Rest, it will cause the character to be Inherently Weakened and reduce all sources of Healing to 1 point until cured. Should the next Long Rest be the following event, the character will begin their next event with these effects. The Journeyman Alchemy recipe “Absolution” can cure this effect, though this comes with drawbacks.
Mana Sickness
A character with Mana Sickness will feel slightly nauseous every time they cast a spell. Every time the character should get Mana Sickness while they already have it, the sickness gets worse. A character who uses a substance that would give Mana Sickness who already has it, will start taking a Quick Drain Effect whenever they cast a spell. The duration of this is cumulative, so if they took a second substance that caused Mana Sickness while they already had it, every spell would cause a Drain Effect for a Quick 200. A character recovers from Mana Sickness after a Long Rest.
Apprentice Alchemy Recipes


Adderstrike Venom [Apprentice Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 3 Night Prizes
Uses per Batch: 3 servings
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Topical (Any weapon/drink)
Crafting Process:
Pick apart the caps of the Night Prizes and place them in a vial. Cover just slightly with water and stir gently for a Quick 30. Chop up the stems with a sharp object and slowly add to the mixture. Let it boil for three full hourglass turns and then decant into a vial or jar. Deposit Night Prizes into Ashbin.
Description: A reddish brown liquid that slightly burns to the touch when applied to bare skin. It has a faint acidic smell to it.
Effect: Once applied to a weapon for a Quick 30, the weapon can make one attack with a +2 bonus to its damage “by Poison” with its next attack, before the end of the Event. If a second alchemical preparation of any kind is applied to the same weapon, the Adderstrike Venom will become inert. This may also be added to a drink and it will cause 4 points of Piercing damage by Poison.
Alcohol [Apprentice Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Bloom
Uses per Batch: 3 servings
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Quaff
Crafting Process:
Grind the Bloom into a fine powder, and mix with water inside the retort. Heat the retort with a flame, allow the solution to boil and collect the final product.
Description: Most ancient of all the alchemy is the sacred creation of alcohol. With but a single Bloom and other ingredients for flavoring and texture, three servings of this mystical brew can be created. Each serving is the approximate strength of three shots of most alcohols.
Effect: This brew only has role-playing effects. It will induce a role-playing effect of being Intoxicated. The player may role-play this as they wish. The character can remain drunk until she completes a Long Rest; however, the player may opt to end it at any time for whatever reason. Its effects may be removed by Cure or Dispel Poison.
Alchemical Suspension [Apprentice Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Bloom, 1 Night Prize, or 1 Harvest
Uses per Batch: Unlimited
Expiration: End of Event
Application: Component
Crafting Process:
Take the Resource and meditate over it for a Quick 30 before running water over it and catch the runoff for Suspension.
Description: A clear Alchemical Suspension.
Effect: Produces a finite amount of Alchemical Suspension, enough to fill any standard container. This container of suspension will provide enough fluid for any number of crafts in any given gathering, but generally expires at the end of the event.
Alchemical Salts [Apprentice Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Ingot, 1 Rare Mineral, or 1 Hide
Uses per Batch: Unlimited
Expiration: End of Event
Application: Component
Crafting Process:
Take the Resource and meditate over it for a Quick 30 before putting it in the mortar and grinding it to make salts.
Description: A powdery salt.
Effect: Produces a finite amount of salts, enough to fill any standard container. This container of salts will provide enough salt for any number of crafts in any given gathering, but generally expires at the end of the event.
Bloom Powder [Apprentice Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 10 Bloom (physreps only)
Uses per Batch: 11 Bloom Powder (one item card)
Expiration: Until Used
Crafting Process:
Take Bloom five at a time and grind them in the mortal and pestle for 3 turns of the hourglass. Take the resulting powder and bottle it. After this process is complete, place the Bloom in the Ashbin and fill out the Bloom Powder card.
Description: A small vial with brightly colored powder that looks like colored sand, often the same color as the petals of the flowers used to make it.
Effect: Bloom Powder may be used as a direct substitute for almost anything that requires a Bloom such as Powers, Recipes, or other requirements. Of note, you may not use Bloom Powder to make additional Bloom Powder or other crafting that requires Bloom physreps. The yield of this recipe may not be altered under any circumstances.
Daggerpoint Cognac [Apprentice Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 4 Bloom
Uses per Batch: 1 Shot of Daggerpoint Cognac
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Quaffed
Requirements: Backstab
Crafting Process:
Take two Bloom and dice them into fine pieces. Take the diced Bloom and add it to a beaker. Fill the beaker 3/4 of the way with water. Heat the beaker over the flame for 2 turns of the hourglass. Blow onto the beaker to cool it for about 10 breaths. Transfer the liquid contents into another vial or beaker, while straining out the pieces of Bloom the best you can. Discard the pieces of Bloom that you just strained out. Add 2 pinches of Simple Salts to the remaining liquid. Place the other two Bloom whole into the beaker and heat for another turn of the hourglass. Stir halfway through. Remove the bloom, and decant the liquid into a vial.
Description: This crimson colored cognac smells vaguely of blackcurrant.
Effect: This small dose of strong tasting liquor is enough to send a shiver down the spine of those who drink it, and quite possibly those who fall victim to those who drink it. When drunk, the Rogue Refreshes one Spike, as long as they have the Backstab power. Usable once per Short Rest.
Essence Infusion [Apprentice Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 2 Rare Minerals, 2 Harvest
Uses per Batch: 2 Servings
Expiration: Permanent
Application: Topical (Essence)
Crafting Process:
Grind the Rare Minerals in the mortar and add to a vial of Alchemical Suspension. Cut up the Harvest and add to the vial. Gently stir over low heat for one turn of the hourglass. The Infusion is now complete. 
Description: A clear substance with strange swirls in it. Staring intently at the swirls will bring thoughts to mind that match the Essence held within.
Effect: The Essence Infusion is what is used when Alchemical Recipes, Tinkering Schematics, Rituals, or Enchanting Formulae calls for an Essence. It is the vessel that enchanters store and transfer Essence Crafting Materials that they draw from the Reality Tear.
In order to store an Essence, an enchanter holds the vial in one hand, with another touching the Reality Tear. They will then focus on the Essence that is within their soulhold for a Quick 100. Once that is complete, the Essence Infusion now contains the same energy that was within their soulhold.
Hardening Lacquer [Apprentice Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Ingots, 1 Bloom, 1 Rare Minerals
Uses per Batch: 1 serving
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Topical
Crafting Process:
Place the Rare Minerals in a vial. Heat the Ingot over the flame, holding it in the tongs. After one turn of the hourglass, scrape shavings off the Ingots with the knife into the Rare Minerals vial. Pour the mixture into the mortar and add four drops of Alchemical Suspension. Grind into a paste. Scoop back into a vial and cork.
Description: This dark paste has flecks of sparkle in it and it is usually applied with a disposable rag.
Effect: This special lacquer can be rubbed vigorously on any item to give it a Protect vs Shatter. If the lacquered item is handed around, the information card must go with it. If ingested, the Hardening Lacquer causes Drain by Poison.
Healing Draught [Apprentice Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Bloom
Uses per Batch: 1 Dose
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Quaff
Crafting Process:
Take the Bloom and fit it entirely into a small vial. Add three drops of water and shake the vial vigorously for one hourglass turn. Then, let stand for a second hourglass turn. Finish the product by adding Alchemical Suspension until the vial is full.
Description: A dark gold liquid, it smells slightly of spirits, citrus and peaches. 
Effect: Heals 1 Life Point upon drinking the potion.
Instant Glue [Apprentice Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Night Prize, 1 Hide
Uses per Batch: 1 bag of Instant Glue
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Thrown Packet / Applied
Requirements: Thrown Weapons or Poisoner or Apprentice Alchemy
Crafting Process:
Stretch out the hide and cut it into two strips about 2" by 6" each. Set aside. Take the Night Prize and pull it apart and place it into a beaker. Take two pinches of Simple Salts and spread over the Night Prize. Leave it for one turn of the hourglass. Use the eyedropper to add a drop of Alchemical Suspension, one at a time, until the night prizes are a soft gooey mess (about five drops). 
Stir and massage the mixture for one turn of the hourglass. Lay out each strip of leather, one on top of each other forming an X. Use Night Prize mixture as glue to attach the leather strips to each other, making a little ball shaped pouch. Put the rest of the Night Prize mixture inside the pouch and seal it, using the mixture as glue. Once finished it should be a small leather ball filled with a sticky glue.
Description: This recipe produces a thin bag filled with a viscous fluid that hardens immediately on contact. 
Effect: By applying this to a packet of materia, it can be thrown to cause Quick Root by Poison. If a character handles the packet without the Poisoner skill, Thrown Weapons, or Apprentice Alchemist skills, that character takes the Effect.
Nyte Tea [Apprentice Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Night Prize
Uses per Batch: 4 servings
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Quaff
Crafting Process:
Take the Night Prize and add it to a hot cup of water. Let it steep for 2 turns. Remove it from the water and tear it into smaller pieces. After letting it cool, add it back into the water. Decant into another vial and let sit for 2 more turns.
Description: A light tea, with a slight tingling upon the tongue when drunk. Brewers often have their own blends that alter and add brighter flavors to the drink. Many different blends are enjoyed by different cultures around the land, and everyone seems to have their favorite.
Effect: This brew only has role-playing effects. It will induce a very slight hallucinogenic buzz, much like alcohol-intoxication. The character may experience mild sensory hallucinations of a generically pleasant kind. Too much Tea can result in darker and more revealing sensory experiences. The player may role-play this as they wish. The effect will last until the character completes a Long Rest. However, the player may opt to end it at any time for whatever reason. Its effects can also be removed by Cure or Dispel Poison.
Salve of Shielding [Apprentice Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Bloom
Uses per Batch: 1 Salve of Shielding
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Applied
Crafting Process:
Start with taking the petals off the bloom. Deposit them into a small beaker. Fill with water, about 1/4 of the way. Place over a flame and heat for 3 turns of the hourglass. While it is heating up, chop up the stem and add it in slowly while stirring the liquid. After it is finished heating, take two pinches of Simple Salts and add to the mixture. It should quickly thicken up. Mix it well, for about a turn of an hourglass. Transfer the salve to another container for final use.
Description: This cream colored salve refreshes a Mage’s ability to grant Barrier to others.
Effect: This salve when applied refreshes a Mage's Arcane Barrier if it is currently not available to be cast.
Rare Mineral Powder [Apprentice Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 10 Rare Minerals (physreps only)
Uses per Batch: 11 Rare Mineral Powder (one item card)
Expiration: Until Used
Crafting Process:
Take the Rare Minerals five at a time and grind them in the mortar and pestle for 3 turns of the hourglass. Bottle the result. Place the Rare Mineral reps in the Ashbin and fill out the item card.
Description: A small vial of brilliant golden dust.
Effect: Rare Mineral Powder may be used as a direct substitute for almost anything that requires a Rare Mineral such as Powers, Recipes, or other requirements. Of note, you may not use Rare Mineral Powder to make additional Rare Mineral Powder or other crafting that requires Rare Mineral physreps. The yield of this recipe may not be altered under any circumstances.
Simple Ink [Apprentice Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 4 Bloom, 1 Ingots
Uses per Batch: 2 Novice-tier spells worth of ink
Expiration: Permanent
Application: Component
Crafting Process:
Carefully soak the leaves or flowers of the Bloom in water for three turns of the hourglass. Discard any other pieces of the Bloom you might have. Squeeze out all liquid and press the damp leaves and/or flowers into a very thin sheet. Take the dropper and place 8 drops of Alchemical Suspension onto the sheet, as spread out as possible. Mash into a paste using the Ingot, making sure to use all sides of the Ingot. Discard the used Ingot. Bottle the paste and fill to the top with water. Gently shake the container for a few seconds.
Description: Simple Ink is inherently a reddish color.
Effect: This creates a batch of ink that Enchanters can use to scribe scrolls. It makes up to two spells worth of ink that can be used for Novice scrolls (only). See Enchanting for more information on scribing scrolls.
Vial of Transformation [Apprentice Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 3 Bloom, 1 Night Prize
Uses per Batch: 1 Vial of Transformation
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Quaff
Crafting Process:
Grind up the Night Prize in the mortar and pestle. Once thoroughly muddled, add 3 drops of Alchemical Suspension. Set aside. Lightly cha each Bloom over the flame for one turn of the hourglass. Pick apart the Bloom and place into a pile. Take the Night Prize mixture and slowly drop it into the middle of the pile of charred bloom. 
Slowly fold in the bloom for two turns of the hourglass making sure it is thoroughly mixed. Add the mixture to a vial and fill with water. Shake vigorously to make sure it is combined. Drain out the muddy liquid into a new vial and discard the remains of the Bloom and Night Prizes.
Description: This muddy, dark liquid contains natural power - such that a reasonably attuned magic user might find themselves empowered to transform themselves once again.
Effect: This creates a vial of liquid that when ingested, refreshes a spell-slot of the character’s highest known Tier. This spell slot can only be used to cast a Form spell. The user may only ingest one Vial of Transformation per Long Rest before its effects instead become toxic, causing the user to become nauseated with no benefit.
Journeyman Alchemy Recipes


Absolution [Journeyman Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Bloom, 1 Hide, 1 Harvest, 1 Night Prize
Uses per Batch: 1 serving
Expiration: One Hour
Application: Quaff
Crafting Process:
Using the knife, shave down the Hides as much as possible. Grind the Bloom, Harvest, and contents of a spell-packet/materia (a spell-packet should not actually be disassembled) in the mortar. Mix with water, adding drop by drop until a stiff paste is created. Let sit for three turns of the hourglass and then grind up into powder in the mortar. 
Description: This honey-thick gray liquid tastes about how you might expect it would. It has a faint scent of ash and cinder. 
Effect: Absolution cures the lingering effects from Corrupted Alchemy. After ingesting Absolution, the character is cured of Corrupting Alchemy’s lingering effects (Inherent Weakness, and “reduce all sources of Healing to 1 point until cured.”) This alchemy has no effect on creatures that quaff it who are not affected by Corrupted Alchemy.

This curative for Corrupted Alchemy is a short-lived alchemical brew that cures the effects of Corrupted Alchemy, however, it comes with the side-effect of making future Corrupted Alchemy less potent. For each Absolution that character takes over the course of the Event, Corrupted Alchemy takes twice the normal doses to have any effect. This drawback stacks upon itself. All stacks of Absolution wear off at the end of the event.
Arcane Ink [Journeyman Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 2 Bloom, 1 Harvest, 1 Rare Minerals, 4 Night Prizes
Uses per Batch: 2 Adept-tier spells worth of scrolls
Expiration: Permanent
Application: Component
Crafting Process:
Take the Harvest and dice into small pieces. Rip up the Bloom and add to the Harvest. Add three drops of water to the Harvest pile. Take the Night Prizes and grind in the mortar and pestle. Mix the ground Night Prizes by hand with the Harvest. Add the mixture above with the Rare Minerals and bring to boil in a vial for two turns of the hourglass. Discard all non-liquid ingredients and decant the liquid into vial.
Description: A brownish color, Arcane Ink has a faint golden sheen that sparkles in the sun.
Effect: This creates a batch of ink that Enchanters can use to scribe scrolls. It makes enough ink to pen two scrolls that can scribe Novice or Adept-tier spells.
Bloodseeker Elixir [Journeyman Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 4 Night Prizes
Uses per Batch: 1 serving
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Quaff 
Crafting Process:
This liquid must sit for at least seven hours to ferment. Take the four Night Prizes and add to the bottom of a vial or container. Fill halfway with water. Prick a finger and add 5 drops of blood to the draught. Slowly warm over heat for two turns of the hourglass uncorked. Quickly remove from heat and cork the mixture. Let the mixture rest for at least seven hours to ferment. The longer it ferments, the stronger the intoxication effect.
Description: A strong heady drink, mildly alcoholic with a faint metallic taste.
Effect: After the character drinks this potion, it will increase the Maximum number of Bonus Spikes a character has by +3 until the end of the next Short Rest. It will also create a mild intoxication effect during this period that can end at any time the player wishes.
Greater Healing Draught [Journeyman Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 5 Bloom
Uses per Batch: 1 serving
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Quaff
Crafting Process:
Take all five Bloom and fit entirely into a vial. Add nine drops of water and shake the vial vigorously for one hourglass’s turn. Then, let it stand for a second turn. Finish the product by adding Alchemical Suspension until the vial is full.
Description: A green-tinted liquid that smells strongly of spirits, citrus and peaches. This is often just called a “Health Potion."
Effect: Heal 8 upon drinking the potion.
Elixir of Expeditious Retreat [Journeyman Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 2 Bloom, 2 Harvest
Uses per Batch: 1 serving
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Quaff
Crafting Process:
Take the Harvest and soak it in three finger-widths of water. While it soaks, heat up both Bloom over a candle until the edges of the Bloom start to crisp. Remove the spent Harvest from the water and discard into the Ashbin. Take the Bloom and muddle within the water that the Harvest had been soaking in. Heat the entire mixture over a candle for one turn of the hourglass. Then quickly bottle the preparation and stopper. Let cool in a bottle for use.
Description: Once opened, the clear substance begins to react with the air, heating up. When drunk, the heat consumes the mouth for a few quick seconds but eventually fades as it wears off.
Effect: Immediately after quaffing this elixir, the character gains Immunity to 1-point Weapon attacks (including Uncalled Damage) and Resistance to Damage and Effects that cause Conditions. This Immunity is very specific however. It will only last until the character makes an attack, uses any Power, spends more than 2 seconds without taking a step, or until the character has taken ten steps.
Enervation Poison [Journeyman Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 2 Night Prizes
Uses per Batch: 1 serving
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Topical (Any weapon/drink)
Crafting Process:
Wear gloves for this process, or you could accidentally poison yourself! Heat the Night Prizes slowly over a candle flame until it is hot to the touch (about 30 seconds), but do not burn. Squeeze the warm Night Prizes over a vial and collect any liquid. Add a drop of iron from ore-grade dirt or a drop of your blood to determine the potency; if it bubbles, the process was successful.
Description: A purplish sticky liquid that smells slightly of citrus. The liquid easily sticks to whatever it is poured on and is difficult to wipe off. 
Effect: Once applied to a weapon for a Quick 30, the weapon can make one attack that causes “Weakness by Poison” before the end of the Event. If a second alchemical preparation is applied to the same weapon, the Enervation Poison will become inert. When ingested, Enervation Poison causes Drain by Poison.
Extract Hyperium [Journeyman Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Rare Minerals, 2 Bloom, 2 Night Prize
Uses per Batch: One Block of Unrefined Hyperium
Expiration: Permanent
Application: Component
Crafting Process:
Place the Rare Minerals in the mortar with a sprinkle of Simple Salts and vigorously grind with the pestle for two full turns of the hourglass. Once grinding begins, it is imperative that a continuous motion is kept up until a fine powder is formed. Then, place the ground dust within a Small Vial and heat over the flame. Two drops of Alchemical suspension should be added and allowed to burn off. This process should take one half turn of the hourglass. Set aside and allow to cool. More than one turn of heating at this point ruins the materials, and needs to be begun again.

Grind the Bloom and Night Prizes within the Mortar until a fine paste is formed and add four pinches of Simple Salts. Once a thick paste has been formed, combine the ground materials and the paste and hold over the flame for two full turns of the hourglass swirling occasionally. Allow the paste to char and solidify until white crystals begin to form. After crystallization occurs, remove and allow to cool, leaving unrefined Hyperium as silvery white crystal on a darkened slag brick.
Description: Unrefined Hyperium is a silvery-white crystal that can be extracted from Rare minerals with a lengthy process. Unrefined Hyperium is very delicate, so often, it is kept in a brick of bloom and night-prize slag to keep it supported while additional refining processes are being planned. 
Effect: Begins the process of creating Hyperium, a rare resource. 
Holy Oil [Journeyman Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 2 Bloom
Uses per Batch: 1 serving
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Topical (Any weapon or materia)
Crafting Process:
Note: May only be done in a place of religious power, not at a Hermetic Laboratory.
While speaking a soft chant specific to your Devotion’s tenets, take the Bloom, grind it, and mix it into a vial of water. Then, heat it slowly over a candle flame for five hourglass turns while swirling. Once it begins to simmer, remove the vial, and allow it to cool. Now, either remove the Bloom via decanting or straining the remaining liquid, which will become the final product.
Description: A clear oil, with a faint floral scent. Looking closely at it, you can see particles within it that slightly glimmer.
Effect: Upon pouring the oil over a packet of materia, the materia can then be thrown to harm the Undead. The Call for this packet is “Piercing 2 to Undead by Divine." If applied to a weapon for a Quick 30, it allows the character to add +2 damage to a Spike the character uses as long as they add the “by Divine” Accent and the “to Undead” Qualifier. If ingested (undead hunters might lace bodies with Holy Oil, in which case the body is the container) Holy Oil causes Piercing 4 to Undead by Radiant.
Combustion Paint [Journeyman Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Bloom, 1 Night Prize, 1 Essence Infusion - Energy
Uses per Batch: 3 servings
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Topical (Weapon, drink)
Crafting Process:
This procedure can create unstable mixtures. Be sure to wear gloves, and use glassware that is free of cracks. Begin by filling a small glass bottle with Alchemical Suspension and adding the Essence Infusion. Then, being careful as to not disturb the solution, add the Bloom and Night Prize slowly, leaf by leaf to the solution. Cap and slowly heat this solution for one hourglass’s turn. Then leave the solution to cool for at least 30 seconds. 
Description: Black and sticky, like tar, with glimmers of metallic flakes suspended within the compound.
Effect: Once applied to a weapon for a Quick 30, the weapon can do one blow that is “6 Flame” before the end of the Event. If a second Alchemical preparation is applied to the same weapon, the alchemical mixture will become inert. If ingested, Combustion Paint causes Piercing 8 by Flame.
Liquid Agility [Journeyman Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 2 Bloom, 1 Rare Minerals
Uses per Batch: 1 serving
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Quaff
Crafting Process:
Boil some water in a container over a flame. Once it has a vigorous boil to it, slowly add in the leaves of the Bloom a little bit at a time, over the course of a few minutes. Continue to let it boil. Once all the Bloom has been added, add in the Rare Minerals. Quickly stir the mixture while removing it from heat. As it cools, it will turn into a silver liquid. Add two pinches of salt and 3 drops of Alchemical Suspension while continuing to stir. After a full turn of the hourglass while cooling, the mixture can be poured into a fresh vial to be used later.
Description: Looking like quicksilver, this potion is usually shot quickly as it is believed to be good luck to drink it as fast as possible.
Effect: Grants one Protect vs Thrown Weapons that lasts until the end of the Event or until used. The character should call “Protect” when they use this defense.
Malleable Beauty [Journeyman Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 2 Night Prizes, 3 Bloom
Uses per Batch: 1 serving
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Quaff, Corrupted
Crafting Process:
Start by sprinkling a pinch of salt over the Bloom. Grind all of the Bloom into a fine powder with the mortar and pestle. Dust the mixture over the Night Prizes and heat them over a flame for one turn of the hourglass, keeping it a good distance from the flame so that it doesn’t burn, but instead just lightly toasts. The heat of the flame will activate the Night Prizes and infuse the powder dusted upon it. Gently shake the powder off the Night Prizes. The Night Prizes can be discarded and the powder will then be ready for consumption. The dust should be collected in a vial.
Description: Malleable Beauty is a powder that is poured upon the tongue to dissolve. Most will let it naturally dissolve there as it takes effect. It has a strong sour flavor to it, which is why many eat it with honey.
Effect: This Corrupted Alchemy creates a strong role-play effect. Part of that includes reduced inhibitions, a strong euphoric effect, and will encourage the user to try things that they wouldn’t normally do. In addition, it has the mechanical effect of allowing the character to recover one spent Power of Advanced, Basic, or Utility tier (the Power cannot have the Refresh Effect or a refresh time of greater than a Short Rest), or refresh one expended Novice or Adept spell-slot. The effects last approximately one hour or until the player wishes it to end.
Minor Mana Potion [Journeyman Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 2 Bloom, 2 Night Prizes
Uses per Batch: 1 serving
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Quaff
Crafting Process:
Take the Bloom and wrap it entirely around the Night Prizes. Squeeze the two so that any liquid from the Night Prizes is soaked up by the Bloom. Then, heat the Bloom slowly by candle flame until warm to the touch, but not burning. While the Bloom is still hot, tear it into fine bits and soak in a vial with Alchemical Suspension. Shake for one hourglass turn.
Description: Bright blue, this thick potion tastes sickly sweet when drunk.
Effect: Instantly restores three Novice level spell-slots that have been expended. This potion causes Mana Sickness.
Pillowspore Poison [Journeyman Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 3 Night Prizes, 2 Hide
Uses per Batch: 1 serving
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Topical (Weapon/drink)
Crafting Process:
Take the Hide and shave with the knife, collecting the shavings that come off into a vial. Hold the Night Prizes in the tongs over flame until it burns, then put the ashes into a vial with six drops of Alchemical Suspension. Run the contents of the first vial (with the Skin shaving) through the retort into the second vial. Agitate for a turn of the hourglass, then heat for a turn. Then agitate for a turn. Leave to cool and then cork.
Description: This hazy liquid smells slightly of soap.
Effect: Once applied to a weapon for a Quick 30, the weapon can make one attack that is “Short Sleep by Poison,” before the end of the Event. If a second Alchemical preparation is applied to the same weapon, the Pillowspore Poison will become inert. If ingested, Pillowspore Poison causes “Sleep by Poison.”
Ritual Powder [Journeyman Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 2 Bloom, 2 Hides, 2 Harvest, 2 Night Prize
Uses per Batch: Creates one dose of Ritual Powder 
Expiration: Permanent
Application: Component
Crafting Process:
Using the knife, shave down the Hides as much as possible. Grind the Bloom, Night Prize, and Harvest, in the mortar. Mix with water, adding drop by drop until a stiff paste is created. Let sit for three turns of the hourglass and then grind up into powder in the mortar. 
Description: This powder is used in Ritual magic and is a basic ingredient of those powerful magics. Multiple doses of this powder can be kept in a single container.
Effect: Acts as a basic requirement for many Rituals.
Spellnaught Elixir [Journeyman Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 6 Bloom
Uses per Batch: 1 serving
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Quaff
Crafting Process:
Using the scale, find a packet of materia that is as close to the same weight as your six Bloom. Drip 24 drops of Alchemical Suspension over the packet, and squeeze out the Alchemical Suspension into a vial. Grind all six Bloom into a fine powder with the mortar and pestle, then add to the materia-soaked Alchemical Suspension in a vial. Shake vigorously for at least 50 shakes. The Alchemical Suspension is not consumed in this process.
Description: Smelling of cloves, the potion is a light auburn color.
Effect: The potion, when drunk, Grants a Protect vs Packets.
Steel Oil [Journeyman Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 2 Bloom, 2 Ingots, 1 Essence Infusion - Energy
Uses per Batch: 2 servings
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Topical (armor, food, drink)
Crafting Process:
Harvest the Bloom specifically for any petals it has, and discard all other remains. Soak the petals completely in the Essence Infusion for at least an hour. After returning, decant Alchemical Suspension and keep (as a nice, floral flavored liquor, or for more alchemy in the future). Grind the soaked petals with the Ingots using the mortar and pestle for three turns, then put the resulting slurry into a vial.
Description: A greasy thick oily paste, which after being exposed to air for a few moments, hardens and reinforces cracks.
Effect: Upon roleplaying applying this oil to a piece of armor for at least a Quick 30, while this piece is worn, the character's Armor Points increases by one (both Maximum and Current Armor Points go up by one) until the end of the next Long Rest. Because of the nature of the oil, it stiffens, meaning that the armor piece must be worn when it is applied, and the effect will end if the piece of armor is removed. Steel Oil will work on non-metal armors, but not on summoned or natural armor. No single piece of armor can benefit from more than one Steel Oil at any one time. Steel Oil, if ingested, will cause the Slow by Poison Effect.
Trollblood Potion [Journeyman Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 6 Bloom, 10 Harvest, 1 vial of blood from any creature that was drained within the last hour
Uses per Batch: 1 serving
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Quaff
Crafting Process:
Slowly heat the vial of blood over a flame for ½ turn of an hourglass, or until the blood starts to coagulate. Grind the Harvest in a mortar and pestle; once it is the consistency of rough dirt or rock, start to add in the Bloom while continuing to grind. The total amount of time should be about 30 seconds. Take the mixture and add it to the vial of blood along with two drops of alcohol. Let sit for the time of a Short Rest before using.
Description: A mixture of bone, blood and flower, it is an oddly chunky substance, with a smell of iron and floral overtones. The two do not mix well.
Effect: The character can Heal 1 Life Point by Focusing for a Quick 100. They will Heal to full after any Rest. The character will have vulnerability to Flame and Acid, taking a Quick Drain Effect (as well as any other Effects) from them, and will generally have difficulty thinking quickly or deeply. This effect lasts until the end of a Long Rest.
Vision Valiant [Journeyman Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 6 Night Prizes, 3 Bloom, 1 Essence Infusion - Thought
Uses per Batch: 3 servings
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Quaff, Corrupted
Crafting Process:
Scrape the Night Prizes’ surface for their skins, and add the skins to the mortar. Dispose of what remains of the Night Prizes to the Ashbin. While wearing gloves, slowly bruise the Night Prizes’ skins. At the same time, allow the Bloom to soak in the Essence Infusion. After three hourglass turns of bruising the Night Prizes’ skin, pour the Bloom-soaked water over them, and grind finely with a mortar and pestle for three turns. Pour the entire completed solution into a vial.
Description: Salty and sweet, this milky white liquid goes down like candy.
Effect: This Corrupted concoction lets the character see things usually unseeable. The character will be able to see both tracks (tracking flags: green with the White Halo marker) and the unseen (supernatural flags: red with a White Halo marker) and will be able to read any information cards on those flags. The enhanced vision will last only until the character takes a Long Rest.
Greater Alchemy Recipes


Aerial Doom Powder [Greater Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 4 Night Prizes, 1 Rare Minerals, 5 Hide, 2 Harvest, 1 Alchemical Salts, 1 Ritual Powder, 1 Essence Infusion - Spirit OR Energy
Uses per Batch: 3 servings
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Topical (materia)
Crafting Process:
IMPORTANT: An Alchemist may only create a single batch of Aerial Doom Powder per event unless another power supersedes this rule.
For this recipe, it is important to make sure to cover your mouth and nose with a cloth soaked in water while making it, to try and protect your lungs. Keep the cloth damp throughout the procedure. For the first part of this recipe, boil the Rare Minerals in a bath of the Essence Infusion until the liquid of the Infusion has boiled off and it begins to smell burned. Grind the result into an extremely fine dust for three turns of the hourglass. 
Take the Night Prizes and soak it in water for about 30 seconds. Remove and squeeze out any liquid. Take what is left of the Night Prizes and chop finely.
