Grind up the two Harvest and add to the Night Prizes. Mix thoroughly. Add the ground Rare Minerals to the mixture. Stir it again and then spread it out over a cloth so that it dries. After about 10 minutes, coat a packet of materia in the powder.
Description: A superfine gray powder, much like powdered graphite. A slight whiff of it burns the nostrils.
Effect: By applying this to a packet of materia, it can be thrown to deliver Death by Poison. If a character handles the packet without the Poisoner skill or the Journeyman Alchemist skill, that character takes the Effect. This powder is volatile and if held too close to materia activated with magic or other power, it will either go inert, if it is held while materia is charged, or will cause the other to go inert, if it is applied while there is activated materia in hand. Ingested, Aerial Doom Powder is actually surprisingly ineffective, doing “Piercing 2 by Poison."
Agony Elixir [Greater Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Golden Blossom, 1 Essence Infusion - Life, 1 Daggerpoint Cognac
Uses per Batch: 2 servings
Expiration: End of Event
Application: Quaff
Crafting Process:
The alchemist must provide a vial of heartblood. This is blood taken from as close to the heart as possible, preferably from deep inside the chest cavity. Opening the wound enough will require the loss of at least one Life Point and then two more Life Points worth of blood must be collected before any Healing is received. This is boiled quickly over an open flame and then the Essence Infusion is added and agitated.
Description: This tastes like deep, rich blood. Regardless of how long ago it was brewed, it is warm, and the vibrancy of the liquid is obvious.
Effect: This potion gives the drinker the ability to cast one spell with the Revive Effect, even if they would normally be unable to because of that spell’s once-per-Long-Rest limit. The spell to be cast may be any such spell, but only one additional casting is granted by the Agony Elixir. As soon as this potion is imbibed, the character is wracked with unbelievable pain, which feels like every ounce of blood in their body is trying to stab them with tiny shards of glass from the inside. The character is Inherently Drained by Agony until they complete a Short Rest. There are many who have braved one drink of this type for the common good and have then sworn to die before they take another sip, whatever the cost.
Caustic Glob [Greater Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Night Prizes, 10 Hide, 2 Adderstrike Venom, 1 Hardening Lacquer
Uses per Batch: 10 Servings
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Topical (materia)
Crafting Process:
Take the Night Prizes and soak it in water for about 30 seconds. Remove and squeeze out any liquid. Take what is left of the Night Prizes and chop finely. Grind the Night Prizes in the Mortar and Pestle for a period of at least 60 seconds.  Stir it again and then spread it out over a cloth so that it dries. After about 10 minutes, brush the mixture onto the Hide and bind them together into a glob.
Description: A toxic goop ready to be applied to some materia.
Effect: By applying this to a packet of materia, it can be thrown to cause Wounding 3 by Poison or Wounding 3 by Acid (Chosen by Alchemist at creation and written on card not on application). If a character handles the packet without the Poisoner skill or the Journeyman Alchemist skill, that character takes the Effect. 
Cobrastrike Venom [Greater Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 8 Night Prizes, 1 Harvest, 1 Rare Minerals, 4 Nyte Tea
Uses per Batch: 3 servings
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Topical (Any weapon/drink)
Crafting Process:
Pick apart the caps of the Night Prizes and place them in a vial. Cover just slightly with water and stir gently for a Quick 30. Chop up the stems with a sharp object and slowly add to the mixture. Let it boil for three full hourglass turns and then decant into a vial or jar.  Grind the Harvest and Rare Minerals in the Mortar and Pestle for 1 full hourglass turn then mix into the vial or jar. Deposit Night Prizes, Harvest, and Rare Minerals into Ashbin.
Description: A yellowish brown liquid that slightly burns to the touch when applied to bare skin. It has a strong acidic smell to it.
Effect: Once applied to a weapon for a Quick 30, the weapon can make one attack with a +4 bonus to its damage “by Poison” with its next attack, before the end of the Event. If a second alchemical preparation of any kind is applied to the same weapon, the Cobrastrike Venom will become inert. This may also be added to a drink and it will cause 10 points of Piercing damage by Poison.
Cure Raw Scale [Greater Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 2 Raw Scale, 2 Night Prizes, 1 Rare Minerals, 3 Hide, 2 Harvest, 3 Bloom, 1 Vial of Transformation
Uses per Batch: Refines Two Raw Scale into a single Cured Scale
Expiration: Permanent 
Application: Component
Crafting Process:
Take the Night Prizes and Bloom and pull them apart and place them into a large beaker. Add three drops of Alchemical Suspension as well as three vials worth of water. Heat over a flame for two turns of the hourglass. Slowly pour the Rare Minerals into the beaker, one at a time, making sure not to inhale any of the fumes. Chop the Harvest up into fine pieces and place on top of the Raw Scales. Let the mixture in the beaker cool for a few turns of the hourglass and slowly pour over the Raw Scales. This will release a toxic mist, so be prepared*. Set the Raw Scale out into sunlight for six hours for it to fully cure.
*At this point in the process, you must make a ‘Pollution Call’ of “By My Voice 5 Piercing by Poison and Taint by Poison" unless a Fume Hood is present (see Tinkering).
Description: Using many rare and toxic resources, the Alchemist refines a Raw Scale into a Scale. 
Effect: This process takes a Raw Scale and turns it into a Cured Scale, an Advanced Resource.
Delicious Rage [Greater Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 5 Bloom, 1 Essence Infusion - Weave, 1 Healing Draught
Uses per Batch: 2 servings
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Quaff, Corrupted
Crafting Process:
Draw two fingers worth of Alchemical Suspension into a standard vial. Place the Bloom in the mixture and heat over a flame for one full hourglass turn. Quickly pull from the flame and blow on the mixture to cool it quickly for a Quick 30. Add the Essence Infusion to the vial. Reheat the mixture for another full hourglass turn. Remove from and decant into a vial.
Description: The thickness of blood, but dark purple in color, it has a spicy kick to it.
Effect: This Corrupted Alchemy produces a dark liquid that will give the following benefits until the character completes a Short Rest: 
                                 * The character may move at any speed while carrying a body, and only need one hand to start the carry.
                                 * The character can sacrifice two Life Points in order to refresh one Spike. 
                                 * The first time after drinking the Delicious Rage that the character would be dropped to 0 Life Points by damage, they are instead healed to full Life Points and become Berserk. If they forget to use this ability, it is lost. 
The roleplay effects of this alchemy is that it makes the inhibitor aggressive and irritable while under its effects.
Double Brew [Greater Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Essence Infusion - Life, 1 Essence Infusion - Energy
Uses per Batch: 1 serving
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Topical (Component)
Crafting Process:
Take a vial of Alchemical Suspension, add three pinches of salt. Stir. Bring to a boil while adding five drops each of the two Essence Infusions (the rest should be discarded). Cork.
Description: Suspended gold flakes float around in this faint blue liquid.
Effect: When added as the last step to any alchemical recipe that is in the public list (with the exception of Double Brew), it creates two of those preparations rather than one. That means if you use Double Brew with Alcohol, it will produce 6 servings rather than 3 servings.
Elemental Protection Potion [Greater Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 3 Bloom, 1 Essence Infusion - Energy, 1 Salve of Shielding, 1 Alchemical Suspension
Uses per Batch: 5 servings
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Quaff
Crafting Process:
Draw two fingers worth of Alchemical Suspension into a standard vial. Place the Bloom in the mixture and heat over a flame for one full hourglass turn. Quickly pull from the flame and blow on the mixture to cool it quickly for a Quick 30. Add the Essence Infusion to the vial. Reheat the mixture for another full hourglass turn. Remove from and decant into a vial with Salve of Shielding.
Description: Beautiful mixture of Yellow, Red, Blue, or Green depending on the element protected..
Effect: Grants the drinkers a single Protect from either Flame, Ice, Lightning, or Acid.  The type of Protect granted is chosen by the alchemist at creation.  The batch may mix and match different accent types for the Protect between the four above, but a different card is needed for each type.
Eternal Ink [Greater Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Bloom, 1 Adept Spell worth of Arcane Ink, 3 Rare Minerals, 2 Harvest, 1 Essence Infusion - Thought
Uses per Batch: Two Greater-tier spells worth of scrolls
Expiration: Permanent
Application: Component
Crafting Process:
Take two pinches of salt and sprinkle over the Bloom. Using tongs, heat each Bloom for a few seconds over a candle flame, but not long enough to burn it. Crush the heated Bloom and mix with the Rare Minerals in a vial. Add three drops of Alchemical Suspension. Next, break down the Harvest with the back of your knife. Add to a vial filled halfway with water. Boil for two turns of the hourglass. Remove from heat and slowly add to the Bloom/Rare Mineral mixture. Add water to fill the top of the vial. Let the mixture sit for 3 turns of the hourglass, then decant all the liquid into a vial containing the Essence and stir for one turn of the hourglass. Finally, discard all the non-liquid ingredients.
Description: A purple-bluish ink with a slight silvery sparkle to it.
Effect: Eternal Ink is a specially crafted ink that Enchanters use for powerful inscriptions and can be used to scribe up to two Novice, Adept, or Greater-tier spells. See the Enchanting section for more information.

Last Breath [Greater Alchemy Recipe] [BLOOD MAGIC]
Crafting Materials Needed: 5 Hide, 1 Instant Glue
Uses per Batch: 1 Last Breath
Expiration: Permanent
Application: Component
Crafting Process:
At a crafting station, the Alchemist should take the Hide and glue it together for 3 turns of the hourglass and mark their personal symbol on it.  This symbol can be anything the Alchemist chooses, but they must be consistent in the symbol they use.  (Do not mark the actual physical representation of Hide, instead place your symbol on the final item card.)

This next process may take place anywhere, and does not need to occur at the Alchemy station.

Take the Hide and place it over a recently deceased corpse that has not yet taken spirit form.  When the body dissipates and becomes a spirit, the Hide captures a small amount of the body’s life force and traps it in the Hide.  The Alchemist then should state, “Instruction to Spirit, Tell me your name.”  The Alchemist should write the creature’s name on the item card produced for Last Breath.  This has no obvious impact on the creature that has taken spirit form. 
Description: A piece of hide with the Alchemist’s symbol on it.
Effect: This crafted component is used in other things, totally legitimate I’m sure.  It has no obvious effects by itself.
Liberty [Greater Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Night Prizes, 2 Bloom, 2 Nyte Tea, 2 Alcohol, Essence Infusion - Thought
Uses per Batch: 1 serving
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Quaff, Corrupted
Crafting Process:
Hold the Night Prizes and Bloom in a single bundle in the tongs. Roast over a fire lightly for two turns of an hourglass. Gently remove them from the flame and crush in a mortar to a coarse grit. After sunset (must be done within an hour of sunset), add the mixture to seven drops of Essence Infusion - Thought (the rest should be discarded). 
Description: Upon shaking and then released from the container, the liquid turns to gas and is inhaled with a faint smoky smell.
Effect: By inhaling the fumes from this Corrupted Alchemy, the character is freed from fear of bondage. Until they complete a Long Rest, they will have Resistance to Bind, Charm, Dominate, Slow, and Root. In addition, they will be able to immediately escape from shackles and ropes. The Resistance is immediately lost if the character uses any of the Effects to which they are resistant.
Lifestrike Sunshine [Greater Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Golden Blossom, 2 Holy Oil, Essence Infusion: Life
Uses per Batch: 2 servings
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Topical (Any weapon/drink)
Crafting Process:
Note: May only be done in a place of religious power, not at a Hermetic Laboratory.

IMPORTANT: An Alchemist may only create a single Lifestrike Sunshine per event unless another power supersedes this rule.
Take the Golden Blossom, grind it, and mix it into the Holy Oil. Then, heat it slowly over a candle flame for five hourglass turns while swirling. Once it begins to simmer, remove the vial, and allow it to cool. Now, either remove the Golden Blossom via decanting or straining the remaining liquid, from this liquid uncork the Essence Infusion: Life and add it to the liquid.  This will become the final product.  Deposit the materials in an ashbin.   
Description: A glowing white solution.
Effect: Once applied to a weapon for a Quick 30, the weapon can make one attack which is “Revive to Undead by Radiant” with its next attack, before the end of the Event. If a second alchemical preparation of any kind is applied to the same weapon, the Lifestrike Sunshine will become inert. This may also be added to a drink or quaffed and it will deliver Revive to Undead Final.
Mana Potion [Greater Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 4 Night Prizes, 4 Bloom, 1 Alchemical Suspension
Uses per Batch: 1 serving
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Quaff
Crafting Process:
Take two Bloom and wrap entirely around a Night Prize. Squeeze the two so that any liquid from the Night Prize is soaked up by the Bloom. Discard the Night Prize in the Ashbin. Weigh the two Bloom on the scale with another two Bloom (it should be heavier with the Night Prize liquid). Add salts to the second two Bloom so that they are both equal in weight. Then, heat the two Bloom slowly by candle flame until warm to the touch but not burning. Tear up the last Night Prizes and Bloom and add to the First Bloom. While the Bloom is still hot, tear into fine bits and soak in a vial with Alchemical Suspension. Add the second two Bloom and salts to the vial. Shake for one hourglass turn.
Description: Bright blue, this thick potion tastes sickly sweet when drunk.
Effect: Instantly restores 3 spell slots of Adept tier or lower that have been expended. Drinking a Mana Potion causes Mana Sickness.
Noctilotoxin [Greater Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Night Prizes, 1 Harvest, 3 Adderstrike Venom
Uses per Batch: 2 servings
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Topical (Weapon, food, drink)
Crafting Process:
Take the Harvest and cut it into small pieces/shards using the knife. Separate to one side. Slice the Night Prizes into pieces about the size of your small finger. Heat the pieces over a flame for a Quick 30. Remove from heat and grind in a mortar. Add three drops of Alchemical Suspension and continue to grind. It should start to form a paste at this point. Add in the chopped Harvest slowly while continuing to mix. Let rest for one turn of the hourglass and then carefully store it in a vial until use.
Description: A green sticky substance that has a putrid odor, smelling of decay and rot. The smell is barely noticeable once on a weapon, but in its vial, if you hold it close to your nose it is very noticeable. 
Effect: Once applied to a weapon for a Quick 30, the weapon can make one attack that is “Taint by Poison'' before the end of the Event. If a second Alchemical preparation is applied to the same weapon, the Noctilotoxin will become inert. If ingested, Noctilotoxin causes “Taint by Poison, Silence by Poison.”
Overpotence [Greater Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 2 Bloom, 2 Harvest, 1 Essence Infusion - Spirit, 1 Daggerpoint Cognac, 1 serving of Alcohol.
Uses per Batch: 1 serving
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Quaff
Crafting Process:
This recipe has a few steps, but each one takes a good deal of time to finish. First, take the Harvest and grind in a mortar and pestle for a total of five turns of the hourglass. Take the Bloom and drip 3 drops of Alchemical Suspension over each piece. Let sit for approximately ten minutes. Combine all of the material with one serving of Alcohol. Mix over a low heat for three turns of the hourglass and then strain/decant out all materials other than the liquid. Let rest for 2 hours and then mix with the Essence Infusion of Spirit before corking.
Description: Almost always taken with a chaser, this golden liquid has a strong flavor of ammonia which does not leave the mouth quickly.
Effect: This potion gives the character 3 additional Base Spikes until the next Long Rest; this Alchemy cannot and does not stack with itself.  In addition, it Grants plus One Spike Bonus Damage that lasts until the end of their next Short Rest.
Steelskin Potion [Greater Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 2 Bloom, 2 Ingots, 1 Mithril Ore, 1 Essence Infusion - Life, 1 Essence Infusion - Thought.
Uses per Batch: 1 serving
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Quaff
Crafting Process:
First, put two Bloom each in four separate vials and add one quarter of the Essence Infusion - Life to each vial. Use the mortar to grind the Rare Minerals as finely as possible for at least one turn, then sprinkle a quarter of the dust into each vial of Bloom. Agitate each vial, cork, and let sit. Hold the vial of Essence Infusion - Thought over a flame for a Quick 30. Put one drop from each vial of Bloom into a fifth vial and fill the rest of the way with the Life Infusion. Cork.
Description: A clear liquid. Close inspection will show whirls of metallic strings within the liquid. The taste will be strangely metallic as well.
Effect: Grants three points of Bonus Natural Armor and gives Resistance to Weapon Damage. The character is Inherently Slowed. The effects of this preparation last only until the character completes a Short Rest.
Euphoria [Greater Alchemy Recipe]
Crafting Materials Needed: 6 Bloom, 1 Harvest, 1 serving of Alcohol, 1 serving of Nyte Tea, 1 Healing Draught
Uses per Batch: 3 servings
Expiration: Until Used
Application: Quaff
Crafting Process:
Chop the Harvest into tiny pieces with a knife. Put the pieces in a vial and hold over a flame until all are charred. Put Alchemical Suspension into that vial and agitate, then pour off the Suspension into another vial. Crush the Night Prizes with the mortar, and add to the charred Harvest. Add Alchemical Suspension and agitate again. Run the liquid through a retort and discard. Empty sediment from retort and mix with 6 drops of Alcohol. Heat for one turn. Cork and let cool.
Description: Euphoria is more of a bitter paste that users smear onto their gums rather than a liquid that they drink.
Effect: This paste causes a roleplay effect of extreme euphoria and physical well-being. Most who take Euphoria often feel more confident, attractive, and capable, and find themselves unworried by the possibility of failure, and other things that would normally cause them anxiety, shame, or fear. The character can ignore penalties from Indoor or Outdoor Discomfort and gains Resistance to Berserk and Fear Effects. These effects will last until the character completes a Long Rest, or until the player decides that the effect has worn off.
Enchanting


Enchanting is the process of manipulating and containing the base forces of creation to empower objects. All cultures and societies have their own conception of what each aspect is, and theories as to its true nature. While no society completely agrees on these fundamentals, core themes do seem to repeat between each. These fundamentals coalesce into Essences: physical representations of the building blocks of the universe. Enchanters use these building blocks to great effect, often producing marvels of mortal creation. 
Essences
The most important materials for the enchanter are Essences - physical manifestations of the core building blocks of the universe. These Essences can either be immediately used (in Enchanting or other crafting skills) or may be stored for longer periods in Essence Infusions (see Alchemy).
There are five types of known essences:


                                 * Weave - raw magic, volatile and awe inspiring.
                                 * Energy - electricity, heat, and dissolution.
                                 * Thought - mental energy, will, and attention.
                                 * Life - representation of life everlasting.
                                 * Spirit - the consistency of sentience after life.




	  

	

Drawing
The act of pulling the building blocks of Essence through the Reality Tear is called Drawing. Although no one can Draw without using an Enchanting Formulae to do so, all the Formulae that allow a character to pull Essences from a Reality Tear work in the following way: 
The character should reach into the Reality Tear, doing at least one manual stir to help push through the settling reality. Each Essence has a different feel, an energy signature that can be read by those who are sensitive to it. By feeling the faces of each block, the character may try to locate the Essence they are seeking. The Essence should be pulled out of the Reality Tear, and only then can it be visually inspected. The color of the markings on the Essence Block will give an easy way to determine which Essence has been drawn.
A reference key for feeling the faces of each block can be found here:  
Soulholding
Once they know which Essence they have Drawn, the enchanter should replace the Block in the Reality Tear. The power of the Essence is now held deep within them, called soulholding, to be used quickly or stored for later. If the character leaves the Enchanting Forge, all soulheld Essences immediately lose their structure and fade to nothing. Death in and of itself does not release the Essences, but if the character discorporates and leaves the Enchanting Forge, the resources are lost.
The Essence may be used in a Formulae, or preserved in an Essence Infusion (Alchemy), allowing it to survive outside the Enchanting Forge and to be used by others.
Unravelling Essence
Deep within Reality Tears exist Unraveling Essences. Unraveling Essences cannot be soulheld, and there are no known methods of storing them in Infusions. They will, however, kill you - horribly. If you draw an Unravelling Essence, marked with black paint, then you take a “Death Final” effect. 
Formula Types
The most common types of Formulae are Enchant, Empower, Scribe, and Draw. An Enchanting Formulae imbues an item with an Essence. An Empower type Formulae strengthens the power of an existing magical effect or object using a variety of resources. A Draw type Formulae is how the Enchanter retrieves Essences from the Reality Tear. A Scribe type Formulae uses powerful magical inks to scribe scrolls and tomes.
Measuring Time & Chants
There are three chants used to both keep time and focus energy while enchanting. These are used in conjunction with a ring of wood, stone, or similar material, with ten notches, sigils, or other carvings. Traditionally in order to keep time, the chant would be recited and each time it was finished, the Enchanter would move their hand down the ring, continuing the chant again. 
The Chant of Sacrifice
“Ashes, ashes, smoke at night, grant us sacrificial light.”
The Chant of Empowerment
"Force of being now ignite; rally to your inner light."
The Chant of Assignment
"In this time and in this hour, inward I imbue my power.”
Primal Script
Known to invoke great power, the Primal Script is a rarely used, but very powerful set of characters that imbues the objects they are inscribed upon with magical power. The process of inscription, along with the ritualistic actions performed are well known, but the actual reasons as to why the script is necessary, or from whence it derives its power are unclear. The commonly known sigils are presented here for reference:


Attunement
Many Enchanted items need attunement or cause attunement. Attunement is to the Item, not the Enchantment. If these Enchantments are put on an item that is already Attuned, the Attunement will remain as it is, to the same person it was Attuned to, and will not require additional Attunement or take up additional Attunement slots.
The Enchanting Forge
In each game, there will be a designated game space setup as an Enchanting Forge. This is where characters will craft their enchantments. The Enchanting Forge has five key components:
The Reality Tear is where the enchanter Draws power from the Enchanting Forge.
The Circle of Sacrifice is where the enchanter normally sacrifices resources to power their enchantments.
The Circle of Assignment is where the enchanter takes soulheld energy and places it into objects.
The Circle of Empowerment is where the enchanter empowers objects with powerful energies.
The Rune Circle is where enchanters use the Primal Script to scribe powerful enchantments and scrolls.
The items that will be there and used in different formulae are the following:

                                 * The Reality Tear 
                                 * 5 Essences (one of each type), 1 Unravelling Essence
                                 * 3 Circles around the Tear itself
                                 * A piece of each of these materials to match the Essences.
                                 * Glass
                                 * Materia
                                 * Quartz
                                 * Silver
                                 * Wood
                                 * Crystals
                                 * Resonance Fork
                                 * Bell
                                 * Foci to represent the elements
                                 * Candles - Fire
                                 * Sand - Earth
                                 * Basin of Water - Water
                                 * Breath - Air
                                 * Quills
                                 * Real Quills
                                 * Calligraphy Pens
                                 * Heat Source
                                 * Paper (different types)
                                 * Inks (different types)
Summoned Armor Repair


All enchanters have the ability to realign summoned armor. This has to be done at the Circle of Empowerment within the Enchanting Forge using Realign Summoned Armor. 
Realign Summoned Armor [Apprentice Enchanting Formula]
Crafting Materials Needed: None
Uses per Batch: N/A
Expiration: Never
Crafting Process:
The Enchanter should use a series of focuses to realign the target’s Summoned Armor for one turn of the hourglass. Such focuses may depend on the Enchanter or the target and may range from wands, sigils drawn on or around the targets, or other enchanting equipment found within the Enchanting Forge.
Description: The Enchanter realigns the weave of the target’s Summoned Armor to revitalize its strength.
Effect: After realigning the Summoned Armor of up to six individuals for one turn of the hourglass, the Enchanter may return a suit of Summoned Armor to its maximum armor points (this is a Mend Effect). Enchanters can always Mend any suit of Summoned Armor to maximum points by spending one turn realigning it.  If working on Summoned Armor created by an item they can also Rebuild Summoned Armor that has been previously Dispelled. 
Apprentice Enchanting Formulae
Bless Holy Symbol [Apprentice Enchanting Formula]
Crafting Materials Needed: 3 Harvest, 2 Night Prizes, 1 Base Talisman, 1 Essence - Thought
Uses per Batch: 1 Blessed Holy Symbol
Expiration: Permanent
Type: Empower
Requires Attunement
Crafting Process:
Circle of Sacrifice: Enter the Circle of Sacrifice with the Harvest and Night Prizes. Kneel or sit on the ground.  One at a time, take a Harvest and set it on the ground.  Using a piece of silver, ring a bell over the Harvest and then recite the Chant of Sacrifice. Repeat with each Harvest and then each Night Prize, each time ringing the bell once and reciting the Chant. Discard the expended resources into the Ashbin.
Circle of Empowerment: Take the Base Talisman in both hands and hold aloft, reciting the Chant of Empowerment six times, each time increasing in volume.  Pour the Essence - Thought over the Talisman.  Make sure that the entire Talisman gets covered.  Touch the Talisman once with the piece of silver and recite the Chant of Empowerment once last time loudly.  Discard the Essence - Thought into the Ashbin.
Description: This consecrates a Talisman with Divine power, causing the wielder to be able to heal those who may be injured more easily.
Effect: A Cleric or Druid can use the Blessed Holy Symbol to grant themselves 1 additional Healing Touch / Life Tap per Cleric / Druid class-level. The Base Talisman used is not consumed in the process, but is instead augmented by this Enchantment, and carries with it all existing effects.
Copy Formula, Schematic, or Recipe [Apprentice Enchanting Formula]
Crafting Materials Needed: 3 Hide, 1 Simple Ink
Uses per Batch: 1 Formulae, Recipe, Schematic
Expiration: Permanent
Type: Scribe
Crafting Process:
Circle of Sacrifice: Sacrifice the Hide in the Circle of Sacrifice and infuse it into the parchment. Hold the Hide in hand while sitting or kneeling and speaking the Chant of Sacrifice. The expended Hide should be dropped into the Ashbin while its essence is soulheld.
Rune Circle: Take a piece of parchment which is at least 5 1/2” x 8” and make the Chant of Sacrifice to release the soulheld Hide-essence into the paper. Then, using the Ink, copy the Formulae, Recipe, or Schematic, and sign your name to the bottom.
Description: The enchanter takes a piece of parchment and uses Simple Ink to replicate an existing Schematic, Recipe, or Formulae that they can reference during the process, consuming the Simple Ink in the process.
Effect: This creates an additional copy of the referenced Formulae, Schematic, or Recipe.
Note: Some Formulae, Schematic, or Recipe may have additional materials required to copy. And some may not be able to be copied at all. If this is the case, those alterations will be notated on the Formulae, Schematic, or Recipe. Any Formulae, Schematic, or Recipe in the rulebook does not need to be copied, as they are considered general knowledge available to all.
Enchant Patch Kit [Apprentice Enchanting Formula]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Field Patch Kit, 2 Harvest
Uses per Batch: 1 Enchanted Patch Kit
Expiration: End of Event
Type: Enchant
Crafting Process:
Circle of Sacrifice: Place the Patch Kit to be enchanted in the center of the Circle of Sacrifice, and arrange the Harvest in a line on top of the Patch Kit. Take the Quartz and place it below the Patch Kit.
Then, gather the power of the Harvest and Quartz within your spirit, and lift the assembled resources to your heart, and recite the Chant of Sacrifice twice. Bring the empowered Patch Kit to the Rune Circle. Discard the expended resources into the Ashbin.
Rune Circle: Bring the Patch Kit to the Rune circle, focusing on magical words of power. Inscribe the word “MYSTIC” onto the Patch Kit in Primal Script, reciting the chant of Assignment three times as you do so. Finally, place the Enchanted Patch Kit on the ground in the middle of the rune circle and draw a circle with the silver to purify the last of the enchantment energies.
Description: This is a small kit of materials preassembled to quickly patch up a suit of summoner armor. The kit represents a kit including items such as rune engraved leather strapping, small enchanted metal plates, mystic herbs, and the like. Can quickly be used to patch up a suit of summoned armor. 
Effect: This kit allows someone to patch up a suit of summoned armor with a Focus Quick 100. In order to patch the summoned armor, the player must roleplay as though they are actually fixing the armor. An Enchanter using such a kit will Mend 4 points of summoned armor. If a character without any Enchanting skill uses an Enchanted Patch Kit, it will Mend only 2 points of summoned armor. Either way, this uses up the kit.
Enchant Sharpening Stone [Apprentice Enchanting Formula]
Crafting Materials Needed: 20 Sharpening Stones, 20 Bloom, 5 Harvest, 1 Rare Minerals, 1 Essence - Any
Uses per Batch: Creates 20 Enchanted Sharpening Stones
Expiration: End of Event
Type: Enchant
Ritualists: 1
Total Participants: 1 (2)
Dark Territory Suit: Implements
Dark Territory Required: Optional
Crafting Process: 
Circle of Sacrifice: Bring the Bloom, Harvest and Rare Minerals into the Circle of Sacrifice. Place the Sharpening Stones in a pile in the middle of the circle.  Place the Bloom around the Sharpening stones.  Sit or kneel on the floor.  Take four Bloom in the left hand, one Harvest in the right hand.  Repeat the Chant of Sacrifice. Do this five times using up all the Bloom and Harvest.  Take the Rare Minerals and pour over a piece of material corresponding to the Essence that you are using for the enchantment. Deposit the Bloom, Harvest, Rare Minerals and Essence into the Ashbin.
The Circle of Assignment: Take the piece of material that corresponds to the type of enchantment that you are performing.  Hold a sharpening stone in the left hand, the piece of material in the right.  Standing aloft, repeat the Chant of Assignment, once each sharpening stone layering the power over the Sharpening Stones.
Description: A stone made of a fragile material. When a weapon is run across the edge it is quickly worn away but not until the object is sharpened. This has the additional effect of being enchanted, based on the Essence used.
Effect: A character can use a Sharpening Stone to alter their Spike damage on one Spike they use before the next Short Rest, as well as the other effects listed below, based on the Essence used in the Enchantment. 
Note: A stone can be used on any weapon, not just edged - a fact that has confounded weapon crafters for as long as time itself. Any usage of a Sharpening Stone takes a Quick 100. Once it has been used, it is used up and needs to be discarded. The Sharpening Stone should be represented with some sort of smooth stone.

                                 * Weave - Next spike attack with the weapon deals +1 Spike Damage, and the call gains the one of the Accents: Force, Radiant, or Shadow, chosen by the Enchanter when the stones are enchanted.
                                 * Energy - Next spike attack with the weapon deals +1 Spike Damage, and the call gains the one of the Accents: Lightning, Flame, Ice, or Acid, chosen by the Enchanter when the stones are enchanted.
                                 * Thought - Next spike attack with the weapon deals +1 Spike Damage, and the call gains the one of the Accents: Mind, Fear, or Agony, chosen by the Enchanter when the stones are enchanted.
                                 * Life - Next spike attack with the weapon deals +1 Spike Damage, and the call gains the one of the Accents: Life, Poison, or Disease, chosen by the Enchanter when the stones are enchanted.
                                 * Spirit - Next spike attack with the weapon deals +2 Spike Damage, and the call’s accent does not change.
Enchant Source [Apprentice Enchanting Formula]
Crafting Materials Needed: 2 Bloom, 1 Ingots, 2 Rare Mineral, 2 Hide, 1 Essence - Any 
Uses per Batch: 1 Arcane Source
Expiration: Permanent
Type: Enchant
Ritualists: 1
Total Participants: 1
Dark Territory Suit: Implements
Dark Territory Required: Optional
Requires Attunement
Crafting Process: 
Circle of Sacrifice: Place the vessel to be enchanted in the center of the Circle of Sacrifice, and arrange the Bloom, Ingot, and Hide in a circle around the vessel. Then crush and place the Rare Mineral on top of the vessel, allowing the powder to spill over the side. Finally, gather the power of the Essence within your spirit, and lift the vessel above your head, and recite the Chant of Sacrifice three times. Bring the harmonized vessel to the Circle of Empowerment. Discard the expended resources into the Ashbin.
Circle of Empowerment: Holding aloft the harmonized vessel face west, then take a piece of quartz, and crush it with your free hand over the top of the vessel, and recite the Chant of Empowerment.
Next, still holding aloft the harmonized vessel, face east, take a piece of silver, and burnish the vessel with it, and recite the Chant of Empowerment.
Then, still holding aloft the harmonized vessel, face north, take a piece of materia, and burnish the vessel with it, and recite the Chant of Empowerment.
Finally, still holding aloft the harmonized vessel, face south, take a piece of glass, and burnish the vessel with it, and recite the Chant of Empowerment. Then take the synchronized vessel to the Circle of Assignment.
Circle of Assignment: Sit down or kneel, and place the vessel in a bowl of sand in front of you. Recite the Chant of Assignment five times while touching the quartz to the vessel. Once that is complete, the next creature with Sourcerer class-levels to hold the vessel outside the Enchanting Forge will be attuned to it.
Description: The item that is the target of this enchantment is imbued with elemental power, creating an Arcane Accent able to alter the Sourcerer Spells cast with it depending on the type of Essence used to perform the enchantment.
Effect: Each item may only be enchanted once. The next creature to hold the Arcane Source outside the Enchanting Forge will attune to it and may use it. 


                                 * Spirit - While attuned to this Source, the wielder uses their chosen Arcane Accent as per the Sourcerer feature, but deals one additional damage with spells (not Cantrips) that deal the chosen Arcane Accent, or spells (not Cantrips) that have bonus effects for matching the Arcane Accent.
                                 * Energy - While attuned to this Source, the wielder treats their Arcane Accent as Lightning, Flame, Ice, or Acid, chosen when the spell is cast.
                                 * Weave - While attuned to this Source, the wielder treats their Arcane Accent as Force, Shadow, or Radiant, chosen by the enchanter when the Arcane Source is created.
                                 * Life - While attuned to this Source, the wielder treats their Arcane Accent as Life, Poison, or Disease, chosen by the enchanter when the Arcane Source is created.
                                 * Thought - While attuned to this Source, the wielder treats their Arcane Accent as Mind, Fear, or Agony, chosen by the enchanter when the Arcane Source is created.


Enchant Weapon [Apprentice Enchanting Formula]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Rare Mineral, 2 Harvest, 10 Ingots, 1 Essence - Any
Uses per Batch: 1 Melee or Projectile Weapon         
Expiration: Permanent        
Type: Enchant
Ritualists: 1(2)                 
Total Participants: 1(3)         
Dark Territory Suit: Arms 
Dark Territory Required: Yes                
Requires Attunement
Crafting Process:
Circle of Sacrifice: Place the Ingots around the circle. Standing in the middle, recite the Chant of Sacrifice ten times, one for each Ingot.
Circle of Empowerment: Temper the weapon by holding it over a candle while reciting the Chant of Empowerment.
Next, strengthen the weapon by pouring sand over it while reciting the Chant of Empowerment.
Next, purify the weapon by pouring water over it while reciting the Chant of Empowerment.
Finally, prepare the weapon for change by whispering the Chant of Empowerment over it. Take the weapon to the Circle of Assignment without letting it touch candle-flame, water, breath, or sand.
Circle of Assignment: Lay hands upon the weapon and recite the Chant of Assignment six times.  Chant each time with increased mental pressure, layering the power of the Essence into the item, releasing the desired Essence from your soulhold. 
The weapon is now ready. All resources used should be placed in the nearest ashbin. The next creature to hold the weapon outside the Enchanting Forge will attune to it and may use it.
Note: If the implement is unattuned, the enchantment ends.
Description: The weapon is augmented to hum with an aspect of creation. The exact effect depends on the type of Essence used. Each Essence has multiple effects, and one effect must be chosen per item. 
Effect: Each item may only be enchanted once. 
                                 * Spirit - Once ever, the wielder may call “Death” with a weapon attack. Using this power will immediately expend the power of the enchanted item, disenchanting it. 
                                 * Spirit - Once per Long Rest, the wielder may plant the weapon in a corpse and use it to ask a single question. The wielder can Call: “Discern to Corpse: What was your purpose in being here?"
                                 * Energy - Once per Long Rest, the wielder can perform a Spike attack with a +3 bonus to Spike Damage and must add Flame, Ice, Acid, or Lightning as an Accent to the attack. This accent is chosen at the time of enchantment.
                                 * Energy - Once per Short Rest, the Wielder may Counter one effect that targets or lands on the enchanted weapon by calling “Counter, Enchanted Weapon.”
                                 * Weave - Once per Short Rest, the wielder may regain a spell-slot used on a spell that physically misses its target. The effect must be called within 10 seconds of missing the spell, and the call is “Refresh Spell-slot.”
                                 * Weave - Upon activation the wielder gains +1 Spike Damage for their next three attacks with this weapon that are augmented by Spike Damage. This refreshes on a Long Rest.
                                 * Life - Once per Long Rest, the wielder can make a weapon attack that causes a “Slow by Poison” Effect.
                                 * Life - Once per Long Rest, the wielder may Heal 4 Life Points to whomever the weapon strikes. The weapon must touch the target, which cannot be the wielder, and the Call is “Heal 4 by Life."
                                 * Thought - The weapon confers Resistance to the Mind Accent. The wielder may resist a number of Mind Accent attacks equal to their Life Point Maximum per Event.
                                 * Thought - Once per Event, the wielder may sacrifice 1 Maximum Life Point to gain +1 Maximum Spikes while wielding the weapon until their next Long Rest.
Scribe Novice Scroll [Apprentice Enchanting Formula]
Crafting Materials Needed: 3 Hide, 1 Simple Ink
Uses per Batch: 1 Scroll
Expiration: Until Used
Type: Scribe
Crafting Process:
Circle of Sacrifice: Sacrifice the Hide in the Circle of Sacrifice and infuse it into the parchment. Hold the Hide in hand while sitting or kneeling and speaking the Chant of Sacrifice. The expended Hide should be dropped into the Ashbin while its essence is soulheld.
Rune Circle: Take a piece of parchment which is at least 5 1/2” x 8” and make the Chant of Sacrifice to release the soulheld Hide-essence into the paper. Then, using the Ink, copy an Incantation from a Novice spell from a viewed spellbook onto the parchment. Just below that, write a copy of the Incantation in Primal Script.
Description: The enchanter takes a piece of parchment and uses Simple Ink to scribe a magical scroll based off of a Novice spell in any Spellbook they can reference during the process, consuming the Simple Ink in the process.
Effect: While the scroll is held in one hand (it need not be the only thing in the hand) and the Incantation on it is read aloud three times, the effects of the spell will occur as if produced by a spell-caster of the minimum level required to cast the spell. The effect of the spell cannot be altered from the base casting by any means. Characters with the Enchanting Skill need only read the incantation aloud twice. The reader must have at hand any material components required by the spell, such as Packets, weapons (if weapon-delivered), or the like. While reading, if the character is disturbed in any way that normal spell-casting would be disturbed (see Core Rules), the spell fails to cast, and the scroll is considered expended. Once the spell is cast, the scroll crumbles to dust, unless it would otherwise be refunded.
Note: Effects that grant the Life Effect, and those that have the Target of Self are not scribable.
Delve Reality (Minor) [Apprentice Enchanting Formulae]
Crafting Materials Needed: 5 Bloom, 5 Night Prizes
Uses per Batch: 1 Essence
Expiration: Lasts until one Essence has been pulled
Type: Draw
Crafting Process:
Circle of Sacrifice: Sitting or kneeling in the Circle of Sacrifice, hold the Bloom in the left hand and the Night Prize in the right. To sacrifice the resources, repeat the Chant of Sacrifice three times while holding the resources and then deposit them into the Ashbin. Go to the Reality Tear.
Reality Tear: Ring the bell three times, each time waiting for silence before ringing it again. At that point, draw Essences from the Reality Tear (see Drawing procedure at the beginning of the Enchanting section).
Description: The apprentice enchanter focuses their internal energy inward enough to be able to survive the process of reaching into a Reality Tear, retrieving a small piece of energy from the ether. The energy must be used quickly or otherwise preserved in an Essence Infusion.
Effect: The character can Draw from the Reality Tear (see Drawing procedure at the beginning of the section). If they draw an Unravelling Essence they immediately take a Death Final Effect.
Harvest Essence [Apprentice Enchanting Formula]
Crafting Materials Needed: 10 Harvest (physreps only)
Uses per Batch: 11 Harvest Essence (one item card)
Expiration: Until Used
Type: Empower
Crafting Process:
Circle of Sacrifice: Enter the Circle of Sacrifice with the Harvest. Kneel or sit on the ground. One at a time, take a Harvest and set it on the ground. Using a piece of silver, ring a bell over the 10 Harvest and then recite the Chant of Material Sacrifice. At the end of the entire process, place the Harvest reps in the Ash Bin and fill out the item card.
Reality Tear: Ring the bell four times, each time waiting for silence before ringing it again. At that point, draw an Essence from the Reality Tear (see Drawing procedure at the beginning of the chapter). If you successfully draw the Spirit or Life Essence, place the Essence back and the process is complete. On any other draw including Unravelling, place the Essence back and begin the steps of the Reality Tear again.
Effect: Harvest Essence may be used as a direct substitute for almost anything that requires a Harvest such as Powers, Recipes, or other requirements. Of note, you may not use Harvest Essence to make additional Harvest Essence or other crafting that requires Harvest physreps. The yield of this recipe may not be altered under any circumstances.
Night Prize Essence [Apprentice Enchanting Formula]
Crafting Materials Needed: 10 Night Prize (physreps only)
Uses per Batch: 11 Night Prize Essence (one item card)
Expiration: Until Used
Type: Empower
Crafting Process:
Circle of Sacrifice: Enter the Circle of Sacrifice with the Night Prizes. Kneel or sit on the ground. One at a time, take a Night Prize and set it on the ground. Using a piece of silver, ring a bell over the 10 Night Prizes and then recite the Chant of Material Sacrifice. At the end of the entire process, place the Night Prize reps in the Ash Bin and fill out the item card.
Reality Tear: Ring the bell four times, each time waiting for silence before ringing it again. At that point, draw an Essence from the Reality Tear (see Drawing procedure at the beginning of the chapter). If you successfully draw the Energy or Thought Essence, place the Essence back and the process is complete. On any other draw including Unravelling, place the Essence back and begin the steps of the Reality Tear again.
Effect: Night Prize Essence may be used as a direct substitute for almost anything that requires a Night Prize such as Powers, Recipes, or other requirements. Of note, you may not use Night Prize Essence to make additional Night Prize Essence or other crafting that requires Night Prize physreps. The yield of this recipe may not be altered under any circumstances.
Journeyman Enchanting Formulae


Enchant Arcane Bracelet (Lesser) [Journeyman Enchanting Formula]
Crafting Materials Needed: 3 Ingots, 2 Rare Minerals, 2 Night Prizes, 1 Essence - (1 of Energy, Spirit, or Weave)
Uses per Batch: 1 Arcane Bracelet
Expiration: Permanent
Type: Enchant
Ritualists: 1 (2)
Total Participants: 1 (2)
Dark Territory Suit: Gems
Dark Territory Required: Yes
Requires Attunement
Crafting Process:
Circle of Sacrifice: While sitting down in the center of the circle, light three candles around you. Take the three Ingots and pass them through one of the candle flames while reciting the Chant of Sacrifice. Take the 2 Rare Minerals and pass them through a different one of the candle flames while reciting the Chant of Sacrifice. Lastly, take the 2 Night Prizes and pass them through the last candle’s flame while reciting the Chant of Sacrifice.
Circle of Assignment: Enter the circle and sit or kneel. Place the Essence on the ground before you, then create a triangle around it with the quartz, glass, and materia. Repeat the Chant of Assignment thrice, focusing your attention on the Essence; after each time you recite the chant, ring the bell. Pour the Essence over the material corresponding to the chosen Essence (quartz for Energy, glass for Spirit, materia for Weave), repeating the Chant of Assignment once more.

Circle of Empowerment: Take the target and material corresponding to the chosen Essence into the circle. Place the target within the center of the circle, then stand aloft before the target, holding the material in both hands. Chant the Chant of Empowerment twice loudly, and focus the energy of the soulheld Essence into the target. 
After chanting with conviction and vigor, the now magical bracelet will be imbued with protective energies. The bracelet is now ready. All resources used should be placed in the nearest ashbin. The next creature to hold the target outside the Enchanting Forge will attune to it and may use it.
Description: Through this process, the enchanter imbues a non-magical bracelet with the ability to create summoned armor. The first person who wears the bracelet outside of the Enchanting Forge will attune themselves to it.
Effect: When worn, this bracelet grants two points of summoned armor. The armor starts off exhausted (at 0 Armor Points) when the bracelet is first put on and the armor must be Mended before it is useful. The bracelet’s Summoned Armor may be mended to full by an Enchanter by spending a Slow 60 roleplaying re-forging the magical armor at the Enchanting Forge’s circle of Empowerment.
Enchant Gathering Implement [Journeyman Enchanting Formula]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Essence - Any, 2 Harvest, 2 Night Prize, 2 Rare Mineral
Uses per Batch: 1 Gathering Implement
Expiration: Permanent
Type: Enchant
Ritualists: 1
Total Participants: 1
Dark Territory Suit: Arms
Dark Territory Required: Yes
Requires Attunement
Crafting Process:
Note: This process can only be performed on a gathering implement (sickle/pick/knife).
Circle of Sacrifice: Sit or kneel within the circle with a single candle in front of you. Close your eyes, and recite the Chant of Sacrifice. Keep the chanting quiet enough that the flame of the candle in front of you is not blown out, repeating the chant three times. Once you have completed the chant, burn the Harvest, Night Prizes and Rare Minerals with the candle. 
Circle of Empowerment: Take the target into the Circle. In addition, bring a piece of the material that corresponds to the Essence being imbued upon the weapon (such as Quartz for Energy). Heat the material over the flame of a candle while reciting the Chant of Empowerment three times.
Circle of Assignment: Enter the circle, standing up and facing north. Hold the target in one hand, and hold the material that corresponds to the type of soulheld energy being used in the other. Facing north, call forth the power of magic by reciting the Chant of Assignment. Focus and channel that energy from yourself into the target. Starting to the east, turn to the other three cardinal directions, reciting the Chant of Assignment each time.
The target is now ready. All resources used should be placed in the nearest ashbin. The next creature to hold the target outside the Enchanting Forge will attune to it and may use it.
Note: If the implement is unattuned, the enchantment ends.
Description: The Gathering Implement is imbued with raw magical energy, allowing small effects depending on the type of Essence used to perform the enchantment.
Effect: The enchanter takes a piece of Essence and directly enchants a gathering implement with a single chosen effect from the following list. This will attune the implement to the wielder. Each item may only be enchanted once.
                                 * Spirit - Once per long rest, you may call “Immune” to a single effect delivered by revealing an Unravelling Essence. 
                                 * Spirit -  After completing a harvest with this implement, the wielder may siphon trace amounts of energy from the resource and store it within their implement for later use. For every 5 Resources that the character gathers with this implement, they will store one charge into the implement. All charges of this effect are lost when the character takes a Long Rest. The wielder may use one charge to call “Grant Insubstantial to Self.” If the wielder walks more than 100 paces from where they became insubstantial from this implement, or a Slow 600 (10 minutes) have passed, they must immediately call “Cure Insubstantial to Self.” 
                                 * Energy - The character gains Immunity to accented repercussions from Gathering from any of the following Accents: Flame, Ice, Lightning, or Acid. The appropriate call for this is “Immune” when used. 
                                 * Energy - After completing a harvest with this implement, the wielder may siphon trace amounts of energy from the resource and store it within their implement for later use. For every 5 Resources that the character gathers with this implement, they will store one charge into the implement. Additionally, each time the character takes a negative effect from Harvesting, they may store one charge into their implement. All charges of this effect are lost when the character takes a Long Rest. They may then spend one charge and touch any spell-packet, even one held by another individual, to Imbue it with “Wounding 1 by Agony.” The call to charge a held spell-packet is “I coalesce siphoned energy” The call to charge a spell-packet held by another is “I coalesce siphoned energy. Imbue: Wounding 1 by Agony.”
                                 * Weave - Once per Short Rest, the character can return a Resource card to the Mine deck, Scavenged NPC, or other source and draw/request a new one. To help explain things to the NPC, the PCs should say “Instruction: Take back this card and give me a different one if you have it.” If no other card is available, the character will get the same card returned to them, and this power refreshes.
                                 * Weave - Once per Short Rest, while holding the implement and no other weapons or shields, the character can Imprison themself by calling “Imprison Self by Force." They must remain Imprisoned for a Quick Count of 100, then may call “Cure Imprisoned to Self” at any time.
                                 * Life - After completing a harvest with this implement, the wielder may siphon trace amounts of energy from the resource and store it within their implement for later use. For every 5 Resources that the character gathers with this implement, they will store one charge into the implement. All charges of this effect are lost when the character takes a Long Rest. The wielder may use one charge to prepare one use of “Heal 1 to Self;” this may be used at any time, at-will, until the character completes a Short Rest.
                                 * Life - While wielding this implement, the character may harvest one more Bloom per rank of Forage than they otherwise would be able to. This effect cannot be refreshed by re-attuning to a different implement with the same enchantment. 
                                 * Thought - The wielder of the implement acts as if they had the Insight skill.
                                 * Thought - Once per Short Rest, the character may activate this enchantment to instantly harvest an appropriate resource. In addition, if a negative consequence arises from that harvest, it is Countered by this power (the character calls “Counter, Enchanted Implement”).
Enchant Glowstone [Journeyman Enchanting Formula]
Crafting Materials Needed: Raw Radianite (special material), 1 Essence - One of [Spirit, Weave, or Energy]
Uses per Batch: Up to 3 pieces of Glowstone
Expiration: Permanent
Type: Enchant
Crafting Process: 
Circle of Assignment: Sitting or kneeling in the circle, repeat the Chant of Assignment thrice, once for each Essence soulheld. While doing so, hold aloft a piece of glass, materia, or quartz representing the Essence that are soulheld. 
Circle of Empowerment: Take the Radianite and place it within the center of the circle, then stand aloft before the Radianite. Strike a tuning fork against a piece of crystal, focusing the mind to find the exact moment of silence that happens after the tuning fork rings and slowly fade away; it often takes several rings to isolate the exact moment of ringing into silence. Once that moment has been found, clasp both hands together, chanting the Chant of Empowerment twice loudly, and focusing the energy of the soulheld Essence into the Radianite. After chanting with conviction and vigor, the Radianite will be transformed into Glowstone. Up to three pieces can be empowered at once.
Description: The discovery of Radianite was first thought to be good for nothing more than party tricks and late night Euphoria fueled dance parties. When researchers first realized its potential industrial applications, industry rapidly consumed every ounce of it they could get their hands on. Eventually, however, this led to a collapse in the supply - and as a result, the raw materials needed to craft Glowstones are rare to find.
Effect: Raw Radianite is an item that needs to be found in-game and players cannot start with it. By using Radianite and this Formula, a player can rep a UV flashlight to shine UV/Blacklight from it at will. The natural properties of Night Prizes are said to glow under the light.
Enchant Hyperium [Journeyman Enchanting Formula]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Essence - Any, 3 Ingots, 3 Harvest, 1 Refined Hyperium
Uses per Batch: 1 small bag of Enchanted Hyperium
Expiration: Permanent
Type: Enchant
Ritualists: 1
Total Participants: 1
Dark Territory Suit: Implements
Dark Territory Required: Optional
Crafting Process: 
Circle of Sacrifice: Enter the Circle of Sacrifice and place the bar of Refined Hyperium in the center, with the six resources equally in a ring around it, alternating between Ingot and Harvest. Recite the Chant of Sacrifice thrice, each time walking counterclockwise around the circle, unwinding the magical impurities in the Ingots and Harvest, noting that they wither into dust. Deposit the expanded Ingots and Harvest ash into the Ashbin. 
Circle of Empowerment: Take the Purified Hyperium into the Circle of Empowerment, and strike it twice with the Resonance Fork to verify the harmonics of the crystals within. Quietly recite the Chant of Empowerment twice while infusing the energy of the Essence into the bar of Purified Hyperium, waiting until the resonance abates. Then strike the bar twice again, and recite the Chant again ensuring the bar has enough resonance to endure heating. 
Circle of Assignment: Take the Resonant Hyperium to the source of heat and let it bask in the flame for one recitation of the Chant of Assignment. Take care to avoid dropping the Hyperium ingot, as it now contains an exceptional amount of magical power, and is likely to shatter, rendering it worthless. Carefully quench the Resonant Hyperium in the basin of water, and let cool for one recitation of the Chant of Assignment. Once cooled, the ingot may be crumbled to dust in the hand and the crystals of Enchanted Hyperium pulled out. Discard the slag and ash, and collect the crystals.
Description: One small bag worth of loose iridescent and translucent crystals known as Enchanted Hyperium.
Effect: Enchanted Hyperium is used in the crafting of other, often magical items.
Enchant Shield [Journeyman Enchanting Formula]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Essence - Any, 5 Hide, 5 Ingots, 5 Rare Minerals
Uses per Batch: 1 Shield
Expiration: Permanent
Type: Enchant
Ritualists: 1 (2)
Total Participants: 1 (2)
Dark Territory Suit: Arms
Dark Territory Required: Yes
Requires Attunement
Crafting Process:
Circle of Sacrifice: Enter the circle and wrap one piece of Hide around an Ingot and Rare Mineral. Do this four more times. Take a piece of crystal and while peering through it, looking at each wrapped bundle, recite the Chant of Sacrifice once for each bundle.
Circle of Empowerment: Take the target into the Circle. In addition, bring a piece of the material that corresponds to the Essence being imbued upon the weapon (such as quartz for Energy), and the Essence itself. Take the tuning fork and strike the target with it until the magical harmonies align. Once you feel that the pitch is right, say the Chant of Empowerment aloud three times while pouring the Essence over the material. The first time, chant loudly to wake up the soulheld energy within you. The second time, chant softly, focusing on the material that corresponds with the soulheld energy being used. The third time, chant with confidence, focusing on transferring the energy from you to the material.
Circle of Assignment: Take a basin of water, the target, and the material into the circle. Place one hand into the basin and hold onto the material that corresponds to the enchantment with the other hand. Recite the Chant of Assignment. Move your hand from the basin to the front of the target. Repeat this process nine times (for a total of ten times) - placing your hand back in the basin before beginning the chant each time - focusing on transferring your energy to the target, letting the basin of water ground your magical energies each time. 
The shield is now ready. All resources used should be placed in the nearest ashbin. The next creature to hold the target outside the Enchanting Forge will attune to it and may use it.
Note: If the shield is unattuned, the enchantment ends.
Description: The shield is imbued with raw magical energy, allowing a single chosen effect from the following list depending on the type of Essence used to perform the enchantment.
Effect: The enchanter takes a soulheld piece of Essence and directly enchants a shield. This will attune the shield to the wielder. Each item may only be enchanted once.
                                 * Spirit - Once per Long Rest, the wielder may place this shield on the ground and call “Grant Insubstantial to Self.” Once Insubstantial, the character may walk away from the shield freely for up to one hundred steps. Once those steps are up, the character is unable to move, or they complete a Short Rest, the character must call “Cure Insubstantial to Self.”
                                 * Spirit -  The Shield gains two charges of Counter to Shatter Effects that target the shield, and can spend a charge by calling “Counter, Enchanted Shield.”. All charges refresh at the end of a Long Rest.
                                 * Energy - At the end of each Short Rest, the shield’s attuned wielder may gain a Protect vs. Flame, Ice, Acid, or Lightning that strikes the shield. They do not need to choose the element ahead of time. The character can only have one Protect from this enchantment at any one time.
                                 * Energy - Once per Long Rest, when the shield is struck by an Effect with the Flame, Ice, Acid, or Lightning Accent, they may immediately call “Grant 4 Barrier by [Accent of received Effect] to self”.
                                 * Weave - Once per Long Rest, when the shield is struck by an Effect, the attuned wielder can call “Absorbed!” and Counter the effect. They may then, immediately, call the same effect against their attacker with a Verbal delivery. If the wielder uses any other power or makes any attack prior to returning the effect, this effect is lost. Reminder: Effects with the Final modifier cannot be Countered.
                                 * Weave - Once per Short Rest, the wielder may expend a Novice spell slot to Counter an effect that strikes this shield by calling “Counter, Enchanted Shield.”
                                 * Life - One time of the character’s choice, when the wielder dissipates into Spirit form after dying, they may instead be the target of Revive by Life. Once this effect is used, the enchanted shield is irrevocably destroyed, and cannot be Rebuilt.
                                 * Life - Once per Long Rest, when the shield is struck with a Death Effect, the wielder may instead take a Taint Final effect by calling “Altered, Enchanted Shield”.
                                 * Thought - Once per Long Rest, when a melee weapon strikes the shield, the attuned wielder can call a verbal “Shatter by Mind” Effect on the weapon that struck their shield.
                                 * Thought - Once per Long Rest, when a melee weapon strikes the shield, the attuned wielder can call “Quick Obey By Fear: Run away from me” via Verbal delivery to the person who struck the shield.
Enchant Thrown Weapon [Journeyman Enchanting Formula]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Essence - Any, 3 Ingots, 2 Hide, 1 Harvest
Uses per Batch: 3 Throwing Weapons
Expiration: Permanent
Type: Enchant
Ritualists: 1 (2)
Total Participants: 1 (2)
Dark Territory Suit: Arms
Dark Territory Required: Yes
Requires Attunement (a set of three requires one Attunement Slot)
Crafting Process:
Note: This process can only be performed on a set of three thrown weapons.
Circle of Sacrifice: Enter the circle with the targets, 3 Ingots, 2 Hide, and 1 Harvest. Touch each one to the targets while reciting the Chant of Sacrifice, once for each Ingot. Wrap the Harvest with both of the Hides. Char the outside of the Hide with the candle and rub the charred hide onto each target. 
Circle of Empowerment: Take the targets into the Circle. In addition, bring a piece of the material that corresponds to the Essence being imbued upon the weapon (quartz for Energy, for example). Start the enchantment by ringing a bell and reciting the Chant of Empowerment three times, each time focusing on the material and the soulheld energy.
Circle of Assignment: Enter the circle and sit or kneel down, placing the targets in a bowl of sand in front of you. Hold aloft the targets in one hand and the material in the other. Recite the Chant of Assignment five times while touching the material to the targets, each time concentrating on transferring the power from the material to the targets.
The throwing weapons are now ready. All resources used should be placed in the nearest ashbin. 
The next creature to hold the targets outside the Enchanting Forge will attune to them and may use them.
Note: If the throwing weapon is unattuned, the enchantment ends.
Description: The weapon is imbued with raw magical energy, allowing a single chosen effect from the following list depending on the type of Essence used to perform the enchantment.
Effect: The enchanter takes a soulheld piece of Essence and directly enchants a throwing weapon. This will attune the throwing weapon to the wielder. An attuned throwing weapon, if lost, will generally re-appear with the character (or close-by, such as at their Hearth or doorstep) at the beginning of the next Long Rest. The item will always have re-appeared in the character’s possession at the beginning of an Event. Each item may only be enchanted once. All three weapons must be wielded or thrown by the attuned user in order to gain the enchanted effects.
                                 * Spirit - The weapon has significant power when targeted on a specific creature by using its name. The wielder may add a Qualifier of “to [Name]” to any attack with the weapon. If the Qualifier is added the following can also be done: The attack may gain +2 Spike Damage, or expend a spike to call “Quick Weakness by Mind." Once a name has been used as a Qualifier, it cannot be changed without completing a Short Rest.
                                 * Spirit - Once per Long Rest, the attuned thrower can expend a Spike to deliver a Death Effect. Note: If selected, this Enchantment will only create one Throwing Weapon per Batch instead of the normal three.
                                 * Energy - Once per Short Rest per thrown weapon, the character can expend a Spike to cause the thrown weapon to do a “Wounding [Spike Damage] by [Flame, Ice, Acid, or Lightning]” attack. If the attack misses or is Prevented, it is still expended.
                                 * Energy - Once per Long Rest per thrown weapon, if the throwing weapon is in hand, the character may call “Counter, Enchanted Weapon” to a Packet delivered Effect or damage with the Flame, Ice, Acid, or Lightning Accent. This is from deftly throwing the thrown weapon at the materia mid-air as it is charged with the opposing element (do not actually throw your thrown weapon at the packet).
                                 * Weave - The character can expend a Spike to cause the thrown weapon to deliver a “Short Slow by Force” Effect.
                                 * Weave - When the character uses a Spike to throw this weapon, they get a +1 bonus to their Spike Damage for that throw.
                                 * Life - When throwing this weapon, three times per Short Rest, if the attack is a valid hit and not Prevented, the attuned may call “Heal 1 to Self by Life.”
                                 * Life - Once per Long Rest, the thrown weapon may be thrown for “Revive by Life.” Only one thrown weapon enchanted this way can be attuned to each character. Note: If selected, this Enchantment will only create one Throwing Weapon per Batch instead of the normal three.
                                 * Thought - The attuned wielder can expend a Spike to make a “Quick Drain by Shadow” attack. Each time it is used this way, the wielder takes “Piercing 1." 
                                 * Thought - When throwing this weapon, if the attack is not Prevented, the weapon gains the ability to deliver a “Short Root by Mind” Effect that can be used on a future attack. The weapon will only ever have one charge of this Effect at any one time. 
Delve Reality (Intermediate) [Journeyman Enchanting Formula]
Crafting Materials Needed: 5 Bloom, 5 Night Prize
Uses per Batch: 1 Essence
Expiration: Lasts until the enchanter attempts to draw an Essence three times or leaves the Tear.
Type: Draw
Crafting Process:
Circle of Sacrifice: Sitting or kneeling in the Circle of Sacrifice, hold the Bloom in the left hand and the Night Prize in the right. To sacrifice the Resources, repeat the Chant of Sacrifice nine times while holding the Resources and then deposit them into the Ashbin. Go to the Reality Tear.
Reality Tear: Circle the Tear three times, each time ringing the bell. At that point, draw Essence from the Reality Tear (see Drawing procedure at the beginning of the section). For each draw attempt, circle the Tear three more times, ringing the bell again.
Description: The apprentice enchanter focuses their internal energy inward enough to be able to survive the process of reaching into a Reality Tear, retrieving a small piece of energy from the ether. The energy must be used quickly or otherwise preserved in an Essence Infusion. This Formula is intended for experienced Enchanters to have a greater chance of getting the specific Essence they want.
Effect: The character can Draw from the Reality Tear (see Drawing procedure at the beginning of the section) up to three times. Each time, they can choose to keep the Essence they have chosen and soulhold it, or they may put it back. If they put it back, they can choose again, allowing up to three chances at a specific Essence the enchanter may want. Once they have chosen to soulhold an Essence, the Reality Tear closes and they cannot Draw another. If they draw an Unravelling Essence they immediately take a Death Final Effect.
Note: Soulheld resources are lost when leaving the Reality Tear, even if in Spirit form.
Greater Enchanting Formulae


Empower Arcane Bracelet [Greater Enchanting Formula]
Crafting Materials Needed: 5 Hide, 3 Night Prizes, 6 Ingots, 1 Mithril Bar, 1 Essence - Energy, 1 Essence - Weave, One Arcane Bracelet (Lesser, Medium, or Greater)
Uses per Batch: Enhances one Arcane Bracelet (Lesser, Medium, or Greater)
Expiration: Permanent
Type: Empower
Ritualists: 1 (2)
Total Participants: 1 (3)
Dark Territory Suit: Implements
Dark Territory Required: Yes
Crafting Process:
Circle of Sacrifice: Place the target between two candles, then light the candles. Burn the Hide to a blackened state over one candle and grind the remains of the Hide into the candle with your hands while reciting the Chant of Sacrifice. Burn the Night Prizes to a blackened state over the second candle and grind the remains of the Night Prizes into the candle with your hands while reciting the Chant of Sacrifice. Place the Ingots in front of you with the candles and the target. While focusing your energy into the candles, recite the Chant of Sacrifice loudly once more.
Circle of Empowerment: Take the target into the Circle. Holding aloft the materia in one hand and the piece of quartz in the other, repeat the Chant of Empowerment while focusing energy from the quartz into the materia. Holding aloft the materia in one hand and the piece of silver in the other, repeat the Chant of Empowerment while focusing energy from the silver into the materia. Holding aloft the materia in one hand and the piece of glass in the other, repeat the Chant of Empowerment while focusing energy from the glass into the materia. Infuse the energy of the Essences into the materia, reciting the Chant of Empowerment loudly once more.
Circle of Assignment: Enter the circle and light four candles around you. Facing north, hold aloft the target as well as the charged piece of materia from the previous steps, then recite the Chant of Assignment. Starting with the east, turn to each cardinal direction in turn and recite the Chant of Assignment three more times. Extinguish the candles with the materia and repeat the chant one final time.
Description: Through this process, the enchanter channels powerful magic into the bracelet, greatly enhancing its protective powers at the cost of the energy imbued, causing the bracelet to glow.
Effect: The enchanter enhances an already enchanted Arcane Bracelet so that it gives one additional summoned armor to its existing summoned armor.  It also provides the wearer 1 Counter vs Dispel Summoned Armor once per Short Rest by calling “Counter, Enchanted Bracelet”.
Enchant Arcane Bracelet (Medium) [Greater Enchanting Formula]
Crafting Materials Needed: Essence - Energy, 3 Ingots, 3 Hide, 4 Rare Minerals, 2 bags of Enchanted Hyperium
Uses per Batch: 1 Arcane Bracelet
Expiration: Permanent
Type: Enchant
Ritualists: 1 (2)
Total Participants: 1 (3)
Dark Territory Suit: Implements
Dark Territory Required: Yes
Requires Attunement
Crafting Process:
Circle of Sacrifice: Sit down in the center. Light six candles around you, placed evenly. Take each of the Ingots and Hide, and pass them over the flame of a different candle, reciting the Chant of Sacrifice once for each. While holding the target in one hand and the Enchanted Hyperium in the other, recite the Chant of Sacrifice. Watch as the Enchanted Hyperium is absorbed into the bracelet, leaving only inert dust.
Circle of Assignment: Bring the target into the circle and place it on the ground. Sitting or kneeling in the circle, hold a piece of quartz in one hand and wood in the other. Repeat the Chant of Assignment five times, ringing the bell after each one.
Circle of Empowerment: Take the target and place it within the center of the circle, then stand before the target. Hold the Essence in one hand, then recite the Chant of Empowerment five times loudly, focusing the energy of the soulheld Essence into the bracelet.
The now magical bracelet will be imbued with protective energies. All resources used should be placed in the nearest ashbin. 
Description: Through this process, the enchanter imbues a non-magical bracelet with the ability to create summoned armor. The first person who wears the bracelet outside of the Enchanting Forge will attune itself to it.
Effect: When worn, this bracelet grants four points of summoned armor. The armor itself starts off as exhausted when the bracelet is first put on and the armor must be mended before it is useful. The bracelet’s Summoned Armor may be mended to full or recreated if Dispelled by an Enchanter by spending a Slow 60 roleplaying re-forging the magical armor at the Enchanting Forge’s circle of Empowerment.
Enchant Crafting Station [Greater Enchanting Formula]
Crafting Materials Needed: Essence - Life, 20 Ingots, 10 Hide, 2 Mithril Bars, 2 Cured Scales, 1 Essence - Any
Uses per Batch: 1 Crafting Station
Expiration: Permanent
Type: Enchant
Ritualists: 1 (2)
Total Participants: 1 (3)
Dark Territory Suit: Implements
Dark Territory Required: Yes
Requires Attunement (Multiple individuals may be attuned to the same Crafting Station)
Note: While Attunement is required to use the enchantment, the basic function of the crafting station is not “gated” behind Attunement.
Crafting Process:
