You will need a tool associated with the crafting station (such as a hammer from the Tinker's Workshop, crystals from the Enchanting Forge, or a bottle from the Hermetic Laboratory; from this point on, the tool will be referred to as the target.)
Circle of Sacrifice: Enter the circle and sit down in the center. Place the Ingots on the ground before you in a grid of 4 Ingots by 5 Ingots. Drape the Hide over the Ingots until the grid is entirely covered. Place one Mithril Bar each on the top and bottom of the Ingot grid, reciting the Chant of Sacrifice once for each bar. Place one Scale each to the left and right of the Ingot grid, reciting the Chant of Sacrifice once for each bar. Place the target on top of the Hide-covered Ingot grid, then wave your hands over the entire assembly, loudly reciting the Chant of Sacrifice once more.
Circle of Empowerment: Bring the target into the circle, then place it in the center of the circle. Place the Materia, Wood, and Silver in a triangle around the target, reciting the Chant of Empowerment each time. Infuse the target with the energy of the Essences by pouring them over it, reciting the Chant of Empowerment once for each Essence. Strike the target with the resonance fork, then hold it to your ear until the sound fades completely.
Circle of Assignment: Bring the target into the circle and place it in the center. Walk in a circle around the target three times, reciting the Chant of Assignment once per revolution.
The tool will be imbued with magic. All resources used should be placed in the nearest ashbin. 
The enchantment will be activated when the tool representing the crafting station is placed on the crafting station it came from and the following is spoken aloud: “I release the power held within.” The individual who first places the enchanted tool upon its associated crafting table may automatically attune to it; others may attune to the crafting station normally using the Attune ritual.
Description: The area is imbued with energy, allowing a single chosen effect from the following list depending on the type of Essence used to perform the enchantment.
Effect: The enchanter empowers a crafting station with one of the benefits outlined below. Each crafting station may only be enchanted once. The crafting station should be obviously marked with a blue card outlining the effects of this enchantment, as well as those who are attuned to that effect.
                                 * Spirit - Once per Event, when you craft an item at this station that produces a Death effect that does not otherwise have an accent, you may add the Flame, Ice, Acid or Electricity accent to it.
                                 * Spirit - Once per Long Rest, you may, while working on a project at the crafting station sacrifice a Forgesource to do one of the following:
                                 * Produce an additional serving of an Apprentice Alchemy that produces more than 1 serving
                                 * Add a requirement to Attune an item from the following list:
                                 * Must be [Specific Lineage] to Attune
                                 * Must serve [Devotion] to Attune
                                 * Must be [Specific Base or Advanced Class] to Attune
                                 * Must follow [Divine Being] as a Patron to Attune
                                 * Must be a member of [Specific Civilization] to Attune
                                 * Add 1 Ritual Point to a Ritual done at the Crafting Station
                                 * May Redraw from the Reality Tear without consequence
                                 * May discount their crafting process by 1 Bloom, Ingot, Hide, Night Prize, Harvest, or Rare Mineral
                                 * Energy - Anyone actively crafting at the enchanted crafting station may Counter weapon or packet attacks delivered against them by calling “Counter, Enchanted Station”, but must spend a Resource or Component for each time they are attacked. If the crafter is left with insufficient resources within reach to complete their craft, the crafting process fails and all materials involved are placed in the ashbin.
                                 * Energy - This enchantment may only be used if you are Attuned to the crafting station. Once per Event, when performing a craft that requires an Essence at this crafting station, you may (without interrupting your crafting process) walk to the nearest Reality Tear (if this is not one already), incant "I focus my (crafting station) to tap Reality!" and perform an immediate draw from the Tear at no cost, Soulholding any resource you draw other than an Unravelling Essence. You must then immediately return to the crafting station you left, where you can immediately use the drawn resource without placing it into an Essence Infusion, if it is appropriate to your craft. If the resource was not appropriate to your craft, or you take any action other than immediately returning and resuming crafting, the Soulheld resource is lost.
                                 * Weave - If someone falls to zero Life Points as a direct consequence of their work at this crafting station, they will Heal 1 just before they would otherwise transition to Dead once per Long Rest. If someone takes a Death effect as a direct consequence of their work at this crafting station, once per Event they may Alter it to 10 Final Damage, as though they had used the Will to Live perk. 
                                 * Weave - Once per Long Rest, when you are performing a craft at this crafting station and a Marshal adds a card from the Dark Territory deck (whether or not it is face up) to the hand for your current process, you may call "Discern to Marshal by Mind: What is likely to happen if that card were my final draw?", indicating the desired card.
                                 * Life - Once per crafting process that involves a sacrifice of Life Points, a chosen sacrifice counts as though it was worth twice as much as was actually sacrificed. Additionally, if a crafting process results in death, a crafter may instead drop to zero health once per long rest.
                                 * Life - You gain Resistance to all damage and effects that you would not otherwise take as Final while working at the crafting station. As long as you successfully Resist or otherwise call a defense, and you remain conscious, this does not interrupt your crafting process.
                                 * Thought - Once per Short Rest, you may call "I invoke the enchantment imbued here to work with speed" to reduce all hourglass turns involved in a crafting process by 1/2, stacking with all other reductions. If this would reduce any particular wait time below 1 hourglass turn, you may ignore that hourglass turn for this process.
                                 * Thought - Once per crafting process at this station for which you are considered a primary ritualist, if a Marshal adds a card from the Dark Territory deck to the current hand and you aren't sure why, you may call "Discern to Marshal by Mind: What action caused this card to be added?" Alternatively, at any point during a process involving Dark Territory at this station, you may call "Discern to Marshal by Mind: What action caused the most recent increase to the Dark Territory modifier for this process?"


Enchant Cypher Quill [Greater Enchanting Formula]
Crafting Materials Needed: 2 Hide, 1 Harvest, 1 Cured Scale, 1 Essence - Thought
Uses per Batch: 1 Cypher Quill
Expiration: Permanent
Type: Enchant
Ritualists: 1
Total Participants: 1
Dark Territory Suit: Implements
Dark Territory Required: Optional
Crafting Process: 
Circle of Sacrifice: Sit in front of a single lit candle. While repeating the Chant of Sacrifice, burn first the Hide, then the Rare Minerals, and lastly the Harvest. Each time a Resource is burned, pass the quill being empowered over the smoke of the object being burned. Once all Resources have been burnt and deposited into an Ashbin, go to the Circle of Empowerment with the candle that was used to burn all of the resources. 
Circle of Empowerment: Holding aloft the quill, repeat the Chant of Empowerment twenty times, each time increasing in volume. During the very last repetition, cut into your flesh and sacrifice a drop of blood onto the top of the quill. 
Description: The enchanter can empower a quill into a temporary magical artifact that allows someone to write missives in ink mixed with the blood of the recipients. Only the people with the blood mixed into ink will be able to read the missives.
Effect: Can build a special minor permanent magic item that will encode missives so that it can only be readable by very particular people. Someone with the Quill must use ink mixed in with blood in order to activate the magic item. Up to six people’s blood (including the person sending the missive if they wish to read it after they write it) may be included in the ink. The item will allow the player to fold the letter (or enclose it in an envelope) and mark it with the Black Key marker. The person using the kit can write the names of the group of individuals on each missive, making it openable to only the named people whose blood was used to scribe it.
Enchant Land [Greater Enchanting Formula]
Crafting Materials Needed: 10 Hide, 10 Ingots, 1 Bag of Enchanted Hyperium, and [1 Essence - Any OR 1 Last Breath]  
Uses per Batch: 1 plot of land no greater than 10 by 10 yards approved by Plot in advance
Expiration: Permanent        
Type: Enchant
Ritualists: 1(5)                 
Total Participants: 1(10)         
Dark Territory Suit: Gems
Dark Territory Required: Yes        
Crafting Process:
You will need a representation of the land to be enchanted (from this point on, this representation will be called the target).
Circle of Sacrifice: Approach the circle with the Hide, Ingots, and bag of Enchanted Hyperium. Place the Ingots equally around the circle, reciting the Chant of Sacrifice each time. Place the Hide equally around the circle between the Ingots, reciting the Chant of Sacrifice once for each Hide. Sprinkle the bag of Enchanted Hyperium over the Ingots and Hide, reciting the Chant of Sacrifice once loudly.
Circle of Empowerment: Step into the circle, placing the material corresponding to the Essence (or the blood for Last Breath) in the center of the circle. Strike one crystal with the tuning fork, listening to ensure that the resonance of both objects matches. Once both objects are silent, place the crystal south-east of the material (or blood). Strike a second crystal with the tuning fork, listening to ensure that the resonance of both objects matches. Once both objects are silent, place the crystal south-west of the material (or blood). Strike a third crystal with the tuning fork, listening to ensure that the resonance of both objects matches. Once both objects are silent, place the crystal north of the material (or blood). Touch the Essence (or Last Breath) to the material (or blood) and recite the Chant of Empowerment three times.
(Optional: BLOOD MAGIC): After reciting the Chant of Empowerment, if using Last Breath in place of an Essence, chant the following three times: “[Name on the Last Breath], I claim your life’s essence, your heart’s blood, and with it I steal its power.  Mine forever, yours is lost, from the weak to the strong.” 
Circle of Assignment: Take the Essence (or Last Breath) to the Circle of Assignment. Place the target in the center of the circle. Walk in a circle around the target three times, reciting the Chant of Assignment each time. Pour the Essence (or Last Breath) over the target, then hold the target aloft in one hand and recite the Chant of Assignment one more time.
The representation of the land is now ready. All resources used should be placed in the nearest ashbin. 
The enchantment will be activated when the representation of the land is placed in its intended location and the following is spoken aloud: “I release the power held within.” 
Description: The area is imbued with energy, allowing a single chosen effect from the following list depending on the type of Essence used to perform the enchantment.
Effect: An area (which should be marked with a Blue card that the player benefiting from the Enchantment is responsible for putting up every event) gains one of the following benefits. An area may only be Enchanted once at a time.
                                 * Spirit - Any Spirit on the way to the Spirit River may first walk to this area and wait for a Slow 300 before continuing. Additionally, any spirit in the area may speak a single sentence once per Short Rest while in the area.
                                 * Spirit - The area is more likely to have Night Prize appear in it, often having large quantities of Night Prizes.
                                 * Energy - The area is likely to include a mining node. Additionally, any character using the Prospecting skill on the node may replace a hazard or yield card from the deck once per Long Rest.
                                 * Energy - The area is likely to include a mining node. Additionally, any character using the Prospecting skill on the node may once per event, instead of their normal yield, draw 5 cards from the yield deck.  After doing so, the Prospector takes a Death Final.
                                 * Weave - Once per Long Rest per individual, after taking a Short Rest in which they regain spells in the area, an individual may regain an additional Novice Spell Slot for free.
                                 * Weave - Any individual taking a Short Rest in the area gains 1 Barrier.  
                                 * Life - Once per Event per individual, taking a Short Rest in the area causes them to Heal to full LP.
                                 * Life - The area is more likely to have Bloom appear in it, often having large quantities of Bloom.
                                 * Thought - Once per Event, when a Ritual takes place in the area the Ritual Points are increased by 1.
                                 * Thought - Any individual crafting in the area may reduce all hourglass turns by 1, and all repetitions by half (round up) to a minimum of 1
                                 * Last Breath - All Dark Territory draws in the area are +2.


Enchant Materia [Greater Enchanting Formula]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Essence - Any, 4 Night Prizes, 1 Mithril Bar
Uses per Batch: 1 Spell-Packet
Expiration: Permanent
Type: Enchant
Ritualists: 1
Total Participants: 1
Dark Territory Suit: Implements
Dark Territory Required: Yes
Requires Attunement
Crafting Process:


Note: This process can only be performed on a packet of Materia. 
Circle of Sacrifice: Place the target on top of the Mithril Bar, then flick drops of water over them both while reciting the Chant of Sacrifice two times. Light the Night Prizes on fire with a candle, then blow the smoke of the Night Prizes onto the target. 
Circle of Empowerment: Take the target into the circle and stand in the center, placing the target in front of you as well as the material that corresponds to the Essence. Hold aloft a candle in each hand, palms wide open. Repeat the Chant of Empowerment loudly twice. Set the candles on the ground in front of you. Kneel down and take the material in your hand. This time focus your soulheld energy into the material while chanting the Chant of Empowerment once more, quietly.
Circle of Assignment: Take the material, the still-lit candles and the target into the circle; If the candles go out, repeat this step. Place a candle on either side of you and the material in front of you, then sit or kneel in the center of the circle. Heat the material lightly over each candle for a few seconds. Pass the target over the hot air of the candle and imbue the energy of the Essence into the target while repeating the Chant of Assignment three times.  When finished, blow out the candle. 
The Materia is now enchanted and ready. All resources used should be placed in the nearest ashbin. 
The next creature to hold the Materia outside of the Enchanting Forge (who bears a connection to the sigil marked on the Materia) will attune to it and may use it. 
Note: If the Materia is unattuned, the enchantment ends.
Description: The Materia is imbued with raw magical energy, allowing a single chosen effect from the following list depending on the type of Essence used to perform the enchantment.
Effect: The enchanter takes a piece of Essence and directly enchants a specific packet of Materia. Each item may only be enchanted once. The Materia must be marked in some way to identify it as the enchanted piece of Materia with a specific sigil that is unique to the caster. An attuned Materia, if lost, will generally re-appear with the character (or close-by, such as at their Hearth or doorstep) at the beginning of the next Long Rest. The item will always have re-appeared in the character’s possession at the beginning of an Event. Each item may only be enchanted once.
                                 * Spirit - Once per Long Rest, a damaging spell that is cast using the Packet can be given the Qualifier “to Living” (in place of any Qualifier it already has) and the amount of damage dealt will double. After using this power, the caster takes a Quick Drain by Agony.
                                 * Spirit - Once per Long Rest, when this packet is used to deliver a spell to a target, the caster may immediately call “Grant Insubstantial to Self.” The caster must then call “Cure Insubstantial to Self” within a Slow 30 count later.
                                 * Energy - Whenever the Packet is charged, the caster can change any Accent in the spell to an Accent associated with an Element, chosen at the time of the Enchantment from Flame, Ice, Acid, or Lightning. Alternatively, damaging or healing Powers that already have one of the listed Accents, deal one additional point of damage or healing.
                                 * Energy - Once Per Long Rest, when this packet delivers damage and is not Prevented, the caster gains Barrier equal to the damage dealt by the spell. 
                                 * Weave - Whenever this Packet is charged with a damaging Power, it will do 2 additional base damage for the use of this packet. Note: the damage is added when this packet is thrown, not the spell, should it charge any additional packets.
                                 * Weave - Once per Long Rest, when this packet is used to deliver a spell to a target, the caster may immediately cast the same spell a second time without expending a spell-slot. The call for this effect is “Multicast [Name of Spell]”. If this multicast effect is used on a Greater or higher level spell-slot, the packet is exhausted for the rest of the event. 
                                 * Life - Once per Short Rest, when the Packet is charged with a damaging Power, it can be caused to deliver Healing instead of its normal damage. Alternatively, if the Packet is charged with a Healing Power instead, it will Heal the caster (for however much the Power Heals) when the Packet is used, as well as having its normal effects on the target.
                                 * Life - Once per Short Rest, when this Packet delivers a Power on a target without being Prevented, the Caster is Granted a Protect vs Packets. This Protect will last until used, or until the Enchanted Packet is thrown again.
                                 * Thought - Once per Long Rest, When the Packet hits a target without being Prevented, the caster may regain the Spell-Slot used to charge the spell-packet.
                                 * Thought - Once per Short Rest, the character can cast a spell that charges the Packet at incredible speed, substituting “Quickcast [Name of Spell]” for the entire Incantation of the spell.
Enchant Talisman (Rod / Orb / Wand / Source) [Greater Enchanting Formula] 
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Essence - Thought, 2 Ingots, 2 bags of Enchanted Hyperium, and [1 Golden Blossom or 1 Last Breath] (if this substitution occurs the entire Enchanting process becomes BLOOD MAGIC)
Uses per Batch: 1 Talisman 
Expiration: Permanent
Type: Enchant
Ritualists: 1
Total Participants: 1
Dark Territory Suit: Implements
Dark Territory Required: Yes
Requires Attunement 
Crafting Process:
Circle of Sacrifice: Enter the circle and place three crystals evenly around the circle. With one bag of Enchanted Hyperium in each hand, repeat the Chant of Sacrifice five times. Place the target in the center of the circle, then hold the Ingots over the target and recite the Chant of Sacrifice twice. Touch the Golden Blossom or Last Breath to each of the crystals, reciting the Chant of Sacrifice each time.
(Optional: BLOOD MAGIC): After reciting the Chant of Sacrifice, if using a Last Breath, chant the following three times: “[Name on the Last Breath], I claim your life’s essence, your heart’s blood, and with it I steal its power.  Mine forever, yours is lost, from the weak to the strong.”
Circle of Assignment: Enter the circle, placing the Silver, Quartz, and Materia in a small triangle on the northern side of the circle. Concentrate on the Essence, and recite the Chant of Assignment thrice, touching the Silver, Quartz, and Materia after each time.
Circle of Empowerment: Bring the Silver, Quartz, and Materia into the circle, placing them in a triangle around the center. Place the target in the center of the Silver, Quartz and Materia. Then, focus on releasing the Essence energies into the target while repeating the Chant of Empowerment ten times, each repetition increasing in volume. Once complete, the empowerment will be complete. 
All resources used should be placed in the nearest ashbin. This enchantment does not take an additional attunement slot beyond the original attunement from a Talisman.
The first creature that holds the Talisman outside the Enchanting Forge will attune to it.
Description: The enchanter uses raw Essences along with other materials to empower a Base Talisman, allowing the caster to use another Cantrip.
Effect: After a Talisman has been empowered, the caster may gain access to an arcane or divine Cantrip that they do not have in their Spellbook until they complete a Short Rest. They must meditate upon the talisman and the Spellbook of another caster that has the Cantrip in order to learn it, reciting the incantation of the Cantrip a minimum of 10 times. They will then have access to that Cantrip until the end of a Short Rest or until the Talisman is Shattered or lost, whichever is sooner. A caster may only learn one additional Cantrip this way at a time even if they have additional Talismans.
Enchant Weapon (Greater) [Greater Enchanting Formula] 
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Mote of Power (Unique, may not be substituted), 2 Mithril Bars, 2 Cured Scales, 15 Ingots, 2 Golden Blossom, 1 Bag of Enchanted Hyperium, 3 Essence - Any of the same Resource. (The Enchanter may also choose to substitute up to 3 of these ingredients on a 1:1 basis with Last Breath; if this substitution occurs the entire Enchanting process becomes BLOOD MAGIC)
Uses per Batch: 1 Melee/Projectile Weapon, or a single batch (3) of Thrown Weapons         
Expiration: Permanent        
Type: Enchant
Ritualists: 1(3)                 
Total Participants: 1(5)         
Dark Territory Suit: Arms 
Dark Territory Required: Yes                
Requires Signature Attunement
Crafting Process:
Circle of Sacrifice: Enter the circle and stand in the middle, then place the Ingots equally around the circle, reciting the Chant of Sacrifice once per Ingot placed: “Ashes, ashes, smoke at night, grant us sacrificial light.”   Create a secondary inner ring, placing the Mithril Bars, Scales, and Golden Blossoms equally around the circle, reciting the Chant of Sacrifice once per item placed. Sprinkle the Bag of Enchanted Hyperium in a third, innermost circle around you, reciting the Chant of Sacrifice once.
(Optional: BLOOD MAGIC): After reciting the Chant of Sacrifice, for each Last Breath used, chant the following six times: “[Name on the Last Breath], I claim your life’s essence, your heart’s blood, and with it I steal its power.  Mine forever, yours is lost, from the weak to the strong.”
Hold the Mote of Power aloft in both hands, then crush it while reciting the Chant of Sacrifice once more.
Circle of Empowerment: Step into the circle, making sure to have access to the candle, sand, water and the target. Temper the target by holding it over a candle while making the Chant of Empowerment. Strengthen the target by pouring sand over it while making the Chant of Empowerment. Purify the target by pouring water over it while making the Chant of Empowerment. Finally, prepare it for change by whispering the Chant of Empowerment over the weapon. Take the target to the Circle of Assignment without letting it touch candle-flame, water, breath, or sand.
Circle of Assignment: Enter the circle with the target. Lay hands upon the target, and recite the Chant of Assignment six times. Chant each time with increased mental pressure, layering the power of the Essence into the item, releasing the desired Essence from your soulhold. 
The weapon is now ready. All resources used should be placed in the nearest ashbin. 
The next creature to hold the weapon outside the Enchanting Forge will attune to it and may use it.
Note: If the weapon is unattuned, the enchantment ends.
Description: The weapon is imbued with radiating energy, allowing a single chosen effect from the following list depending on the type of Essence used to perform the enchantment.
Effect: Each item may only be enchanted once, and a weapon enchanted with this process is a Signature Item (no character can ever be attuned to more than one Signature Item at a time); however, a Signature Weapon crafted through Tinkering may be further enchanted with this process once.  If a Thrown Weapon is enchanted, a set of three weapon reps may be enchanted together with the same effect and will count as a single attuned item.
                                 * Spirit - Once per Long Rest, the wielder may call “Death” with a weapon attack. While wielding this weapon the attuned character gains Resistance to Death. Unfortunately, the character even when not wielding this weapon also gains Resistance to Revive.
                                 * Spirit - The wielder may alter the call of any attack or damaging Power delivered by this weapon: they may add the “to Insubstantial” qualifier, and the Base Damage of that attack increases by 1.  Once per Short Rest, the wielder may Counter any attack, Power or Effect that has the “to Insubstantial” qualifier by calling “Counter, Enchanted Weapon”.
                                 * Energy - Once per Short Rest, the wielder can perform a Spike attack with a +15 bonus to Spike Damage, and must add Acid, Flame, Ice, or Lightning as an Accent to the attack. This accent is chosen at the time of enchantment.  Additionally, the wielder of this weapon gains Resistance to the accent chosen at the time of casting this enchantment (Acid, Flame, Ice, or Lightning).
                                 * Energy - The wielder gains +1 Base Damage with this weapon and any Wounding or basic damage Powers that use this weapon as a method of delivery.
                                 * Weave - The wielder of this weapon gains +1 Barrier whenever they deliver a Deathblow or “Death to Helpless” to an enemy creature. Additionally, once per Long Rest, the wielder of this weapon may reduce their Maximum Life Points to 1 until their next Short Rest.  Their Maximum Life Points may not be increased for any reason while this ability is active.  The wielder also gains Base Maximum Spikes equal to the difference between their old Maximum Life Points and their new Maximum Life Points.  The wielder may add the Force, Radiant, or Shadow accent to any spike attack they make, chosen at the time of casting this enchantment.
                                 * Weave - The weapon can be used to Counter any weapon attack or packet that hits the weapon by spending a spike. The wielder should call “Counter, Enchanted Weapon” when the Counter is used. Additionally, the wielder of this weapon may have two additional maximum Protects on them as long as the weapon remains Attuned. The normal rules for Protect stacking otherwise still apply.
                                 * Life - At will, by spending a Spike, the wielder may call “Heal X by Life” where X is their Spike Damage plus their Base Damage.  Additionally, the wielder’s Life Tap and Healing Touch pools may be delivered by weapon rather than touch (the incantation must still be used). Finally, anytime the wielder of this weapon delivers a Revive effect to another individual they may add 10 to their Life Tap, Healing Touch, or other such “healing pool” if they have one.
                                 * Life - At will, by spending a Spike, the wielder can make a weapon attack for any of the following: 
                                 * Quick Slow by Disease
                                 * Quick Root by Disease
                                 * Wounding 2 by Disease
                                 * Additionally, once per Long Rest, the wielder of this weapon may Counter any Power or Effect with the Life, Disease, or Poison accents by calling “Counter, Enchanted Weapon”.
                                 * Thought - The weapon confers Selective Resistance to the Mind Accent (you may choose whether or not to Resist). Additionally, any spell with a delivery of Packet may instead be delivered by this Weapon if the wielder spends a spike.  Finally, once per event, when recovering spells, if this weapon is part of the meditation process the wielder may recover their caster levels in additional bonus Novice level spells as though they had not recovered them for tracking purposes. All other normal rules for refreshing spells apply.
                                 * Thought - The weapon becomes immune to Shatter and Disarm.  Additionally, the wielder may choose to ignore any command including those from Charm, Dominate, or Obey that would force them to give up or be separated from their weapon.  When such an Effect would otherwise target them they should call “Immune, Enchanted Weapon”.  (Note: Instructions are not Effects and should not be ignored.)
Scribe Adept Scroll [Greater Enchanting Formula]
Crafting Materials Needed: 5 Hide, 1 Arcane Ink
Uses per Batch: 1 Scroll
Expiration: Until Used
Type: Scribe
Crafting Process:
Circle of Sacrifice: Take the four Hide and hold them aloft while repeating the Chant of Sacrifice two times, imbuing their energy into the parchment. The expended Hide should be dropped into the Ashbin while its essence is soulheld. 
Rune Circle: Take a piece of parchment which is at least 5 1/2” x 8” and speak the Chant of Sacrifice to release the soulheld Hide-essence into the paper. Then, using the Ink, copy an Incantation from an Adept spell from a visible spellbook onto the parchment. Just below that, write a copy of the Incantation in Primal Script.
Description: The Enchanter takes a piece of parchment and uses Arcane Ink to scribe a magical scroll based off of an Adept spell in any Spellbook they can reference during the process. 
Effect: While the scroll is held in one hand (it need not be the only thing in the hand) and the Incantation on it read aloud three times, the effects of the spell will occur as if produced by a spell-caster of the minimum level required to cast the spell. The effect of the spell cannot be altered from the base casting by any means. Enchanters need only read the incantation aloud twice. The reader must have at hand any material components required by the spell, such as Packets, weapons (if weapon-delivered), or the like. While reading, the character can be disturbed in any way that normal spell-casting could be disturbed (see Core Rules). Once the spell is cast, the scroll crumbles to dust, unless it would otherwise be refunded..
Note: Effects that grant the Life Effect, and those that have the Target of Self are not scribable.
Delve Reality (Major) [Greater Enchanting Formulae]
Crafting Materials Needed: 5 Bloom, 5 Night Prize, 3 Hide, 5 Harvest, 3 Ingots, 4 Rare Minerals
Uses per Batch: Up to 3 Essence
Expiration: Lasts until the enchanter draws 3 pieces of Essence or leaves the Reality Tear.
Type: Draw
Crafting Process: 
Circle of Sacrifice: Sitting or kneeling in the Circle of Sacrifice, hold aloft the resonance fork and ring it once. After it has been silenced, repeat the Chant of Sacrifice three times, sacrificing one Resource. Repeat this with the resonance fork for each other Resource. After that is done, deposit the sacrificed Resources into the Ashbin. Go to the Reality Tear.
Reality Tear: Ring the bell three times, each time waiting for silence before ringing it again. At that point, draw Essence from the Reality Tear (see Drawing procedure at the beginning of the section).
Description: The apprentice enchanter focuses their internal energy inward enough to be able to survive the process of reaching into a Reality Tear, retrieving a small piece of Essence from the ether. The energy must be used quickly or otherwise preserved in an Essence Infusion. This Formulae is intended for very experienced Enchanters to be able to pull multiple Essences at a reduced cost.
Effect: The character can Draw from the Reality Tear (see Drawing procedure at the beginning of the section) up to three times. Once they have drawn three Essences, the Reality Tear closes and they cannot Draw another. If the Enchanter draws a Unravelling Essence, they will take an immediate Death Final Effect.
Note: Soulheld resources are lost when leaving the Enchanting Forge, even if in Spirit form.
Tinkering




Metalwork has always been a core part of the technological advancement of many societies. Tinkering collectively represents the widespread art of metalwork, regardless of type. While apprentice tinkers start with being able to create basic items and repair armor, Greater Tinkers can create masterful items, oftentimes imbued with special properties making them highly sought after by adventurers.
The Tinker's Workshop
There will be a designated game space set up as a Tinker's Workshop. This is where characters will craft all of their Tinkered items. The items that will be there and used in different schematics are the following:
                                 * Anvil - Used to shape hot metal
                                 * Awl - Used to punch holes
                                 * Bellows - For stoking the Forge
                                 * Bucket of Water - To quench metal
                                 * Buffing Cloth - To shine or clean materials
                                 * Crucible - For holding molten metal
                                 * Forge - For heating up metal objects
                                 * Hammer - For beating objects into shape
                                 * Hourglass (any description of a “turn” signifies one turn of the hourglass, or 1 minute)
                                 * Leather Gloves - To prevent burns
                                 * Knife - For cutting
                                 * Oil - To lubricate items
                                 * Small Tools - For intricate work such as screwdrivers, picks, wrenches
                                 * Tongs - For holding hot or heating metal
                                 * Whetstone - For sharpening edges
Armor Repair




All tinkers have the ability to instruct others in how to fix their armor. This has to be done at the Tinker's Workshop using Supervise Armor Mending. 
Supervise Physical Armor Mending [Apprentice Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: None
Uses per Batch: N/A
Expiration: Never
Crafting Process:
The Tinker should look over each person’s armor and show them the type of tools needed to fix their type of armor. For plate mail it should be the hammer, pounding it back into shape. Chainmail armor should be fixed using pliers and wire, etc. 
Description: The Tinker should give basic instructions. Armor should not have to be removed to be fixed.
Effect: After instructing up to six others on how to Mend their physical armor, the others can each spend one turn of the hourglass bringing one suit of physical armor up to its maximum Armor Points (this is a Mend Effect). Tinkers can always Mend any suit of physical armor to maximum points by spending one turn patching it up.
Apprentice Tinkering Schematics


Badge of Brilliance [Apprentice Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 5 Ingots, 10 Hide, 8 Harvest, 2 Rare Minerals
Uses per Batch: Creates one Badge of Brilliance
Expiration: Permanent
Requires Attunement
Crafting Process:
Slice each Harvest in half lengthwise, then take the Rare Minerals and sprinkle them over each half of each Harvest. Place the Harvest in the crucible along with the Ingots. Place the filled crucible in the forge and pump the bellows twenty times - the Ingots shall have melted with the Harvest pieces. 
Pour out the liquified material onto the anvil and hammer into a badge shaped piece, about ten strikes of the hammer. Quench the workpiece in water. Then, heat the workpiece in the forge for another ten pumps of the bellows, remove, hammer again ten times. 
Cut the Hide into strips and wrap around the badge as tight as possible. Place in the forge again for five pumps of the bellows, burning the hide off leaving its residue on the badge underneath. Finally, quench the finished workpiece to stabilize. 
Description: A shiny badge, suited for a Bodyguard of means.
Effect: While the Bodyguard visibility wears the Badge of Brilliance, they instead gain three Barrier from the Socialite ability “Commission of Protection,” instead of the normal Effect.
Base Talisman [Apprentice Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 2 Night Prizes, 6 Ingots, 7 Rare Minerals, 2 Hides, 3 Harvest
Uses per Batch: Creates one Basic Talisman
Expiration: Permanent
Requires Attunement
Crafting Process:
Crush the Rare Minerals on the Anvil with the hammer. Collect the powder and save. Now place the six Ingots in the forge, pumping the bellows thirty times. Remove two Ingots and flatten it with a hammer on the anvil. Then add the powder from the Rare Minerals onto the flattened Ingots. Fold the Ingot and hammer it flat again. Put it to one side. Take out the other two Ingots and flatten it on the anvil with a hammer. Put the Hides down on the hot Ingot and then place the Harvest, as layers. Fold and hammer flat again. Take out the last Ingots and hammer flat. Add the Night Prizes, fold Ingot, and hammer flat.
All six Ingots go back in the forge for 30 pumps of the bellows. Pull back out with tongs, layer and hammer all six flat. Fold and hammer again. And a third time. Now put the result back in the forge for 30 pumps of the bellows. Pull out and shape. Work designs in with tools, including the caster-sigil. Leave to cool for two hours.
Description: This item differs for each caster and usually has some significance to them, tying into how and where they draw their power from. Many people craft intricate wooden objects with inlaid jewels at the end. Others use perfectly round metal orbs, polished to perfection, while others use specially enchanted musical instruments. Each one is attuned to the caster it is created for and can only be used by that one person. There is superstition about channeling magic within the land while carrying another's Talisman as it will call their spirit back to haunt you.
Effect: This allows a Tinker to create a special item called a Talisman which can take many forms, but represent the Weave in a way that is significant to their society - often in the form of wands, orbs, and rods. This Talisman allows the wielder to regain one Novice Spell Slot per Long Rest with a Focus Quick 100. This may be improved by a Tinker (at higher levels). The Talisman must be clearly marked with the caster-sigil of the character for whom it is created, and will be attuned only to that character. A Talisman can be held in a hand that would normally be empty for spell-casting.
Basic Mechanical Lock [Apprentice Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 4 Ingots
Uses per Batch: Creates one basic lock
Expiration: Permanent
Crafting Process:
Take the Ingots, and heat it in the crucible, in the forge, for three turns of the hourglass. Pour out the molten metal onto the anvil and use the hammer and other tools to shape the metal into two pieces: one piece rolled into a thin bar and then bent into a U-shape (for the U shape part of the lock), the other hammered thinly. Heat the thin piece within the forge using the tongs and pump the bellows ten times. 
Remove and shape into a box, leaving one side open. Use the small intricate tools to put together the inside of the lock, finishing it off by closing the box using pliers. If keys need to be made, heat up some scrap metal from what you just did for one turn of the hourglass total, pull out, and use the hammer and small tools to cut out the keys. Quench all of it in the bucket of water.
Description: This is a simple lock, but can keep valuables protected.
Effect: Creates a non-magical diary style type lock. See the Skills document (Basic Locks) for more information on how these are used. Up to five keys can be created to go along with this lock.
Field Patch Kit [Apprentice Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 3 Crafting Materials (Ingots or Hide) per Kit.
Uses per Batch: Creates one Patch Kit
Expiration: Until Used
Crafting Process:
This Schematic can make as many kits simultaneously as desired, using up 3 Crafting Materials per kit. Using any Ingots that you might have, heat them in the forge for one turn of the hourglass. Hammer them out flat into plates with 5 blows each and using the hammer and awl, punch holes into the corner plates so that they can quickly be riveted back onto a suit of armor in the field. If any Hide is used, use the small tools to cut 3 square leather patches per Hide, then punch holes into the four corners of each piece. Layer the patches. Rivet the four corners.
Description: This is a small kit of materials preassembled to quickly patch up a suit of armor. The kit represents a kit including items such as leather strapping, small metal plates, boiled leather scraps, bolts, nuts, and rivets. Can quickly be used to patch up a suit of armor until it can be fully mended at a Tinker's Workshop. 
Effect: This kit allows someone to patch up a suit of armor with a Quick 100. In order to patch the armor, the player must roleplay as though they are actually fixing the armor. A Tinker using such a kit will Mend 4 points of armor. If a character without any Tinker skill uses a Field Patch Kit, it will Mend only 2 points of armor. This uses up the kit. In-game, it should be represented by a 2-4” x 2-4” square piece of leather or leatherish-material, or metal or metal looking-material. Players are encouraged to use their own physreps for this item. 
Craft Item [Apprentice Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 3 Crafting Materials of any combination of Ingots and Hide.
Uses per Batch: Creates one generic piece of equipment
Expiration: Permanent
Crafting Process:
The tinker may come up with whatever roleplay makes sense for the item they are creating. It is entirely up to them to decide how it is built and how long it takes.
Description: This is a more advanced item than the Generic Forged Item makes at the Apprentice level. This schematic creates an item such as a sword, a bow, a shield, or a piece of armor. This schematic is usually used when an item has been lost, or an extra is needed for a ritual or other purpose.
Effect: This allows a tinker to make a weapon, shield, piece of armor, or group of up to 6 thrown weapons or projectiles.  
Generic Forged Item [Apprentice Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Ingot or 1 Hide
Uses per Batch: Creates one generic item
Expiration: Permanent
Crafting Process:
The tinker may come up with whatever roleplay makes sense for the item they are creating. It is entirely up to them to decide how it is built, which Resource it requires, and how long it takes.
Description: This is a basic generic forged item such as a small bronze statue with no real value, a leather-bound book, a set of horseshoes, or a pair of spectacles. This allows (also) for the creation of basic Gathering Implements.
Effect: This allows a tinker to make a generic forged item around the size of 1 cubic foot that does not have a mechanical benefit. This also allows for the creation of gathering implements including Mining Picks, Foraging Sickles, and Skinning Knives in order to further enhance them.
Hide Plates [Apprentice Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 10 Hide (physreps only)
Uses per Batch: 11 Hide Plates (one item card)
Expiration: Until Used
Crafting Process:
Take the Hide two at a time and stretch them using the tools available for 1 turn of the hourglass per couple. Take the resulting plates of hide and lay them aside. After this process is complete, return all Hide reps to the Ashbin and fill out the item card.
Description: Stitched “hide plates”.
Effect: Hide Plates may be used as a direct substitute for almost anything that requires a Hide such as Powers, Recipes, or other requirements. Of note, you may not use Hide Plates to make additional Hide Plates or other crafting that requires Hide physreps. The yield of this recipe may not be altered under any circumstances.
Ingot Plates [Apprentice Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 10 Ingots (physreps only)
Uses per Batch: 11 Ingot Plates (one item card)
Expiration: Until Used
Crafting Process:
Take the Ingots two at a time and pound them on the anvil for 10 strikes. Take the resulting sheets of metal and lay them aside. After this process is complete, return all Ingot reps to the Ashbin and fill out the item card.
Description: Small flat sheets of metal.
Effect: Ingot Plates may be used as a direct substitute for almost anything that requires an Ingot such as Powers, Recipes, or other requirements. Of note, you may not use Ingot Plates to make additional Ingot Plates or other crafting that requires Ingot physreps. The yield of this recipe may not be altered under any circumstances.
Mercurial Iron Kit [Apprentice Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 10 Ingots, 15 Hide, 3 Harvest, 3 Rare Minerals
Uses per Batch: Creates one Mercurial Iron Kit
Expiration: Permanent
Requires Attunement
Crafting Process:
Put on leather gloves and use the large tongs to place the Ingot in the forge for two turns of the hourglass, so that the metal gets red hot. Pull out the Ingot and strike five times soundly with a hammer on one side of it, flattening the metal. Then flip it over, and strike it five times on that side. Quench in water. Use a whetstone to grind one edge for a turn of the hourglass. Use new edged metal to grind/shape the smooth stone for one turn of the hourglass.
Description: Several vials and droppers of liquid metal.
Effect: An Artisan character attuned to a Mercurial Iron Kit adds 1 additional Mend Point per Artisan class-level to their Living Iron pool.
Minor Bow Reinforcement [Apprentice Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 2 Ingots, 1 Hide
Uses per Batch: Reinforces one bow
Expiration: End of Event
Crafting Process:
First, wet the Hide and use tools and tongs to stretch the Hide over the anvil. Keep stretching and working the Hide for one turn of the hourglass, then cut into strips the width of the weapon. While cutting, heat the two Ingots in the forge. Take out before molten and use the hammer to flatten the Ingots into long strips the width of the weapon. Lay the Hide strips on the metal strips and punch holes through both layers with the awl and hammer. Then lay on the bow and hammer/rivet into place.
Description: By using simple materials and the tools at a Tinker's Workshop, a Tinker can reinforce a common bow or crossbow past its normal breaking point allowing it to take more damage before breaking.
Effect: The Tinker can reinforce a bow or crossbow so that it can withstand the pounding punishment of blades. As long as the weapon is deemed safe by a Safety Marshal, it can be used to block with and will not be shattered as normal when struck by a weapon. Once the weapon has taken one blow, the effects of this augmentation will end at the completion of the next Short Rest. So, an archer could block with a bow for one attack or for an entire combat, but after resting, would need to have the bow reinforced again. The projectile weapon’s user should be instructed to Call “Reinforced” when the bow is struck. If the same player hits the bow multiple times, the Call doesn’t need to be made for each blow, just often enough that the other players understand that the bow is reinforced.
Minor Shield Reinforcement [Apprentice Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 2 Ingots or 2 Hide
Uses per Batch: Strengthens one shield
Expiration: End of Event
Crafting Process:
Take two Ingots (or two hide) and place one in the crucible. Put both in the forge for one turn of the hourglass. Remove the Ingot not in the crucible with tongs and hammer for one turn of the hourglass while the other melts, trying to make a thin sheet. Put it briefly back in the forge for three bellows-pumps before taking it out and cutting into plates with the knife. Quench each piece in water and lay on the shield. Use the awl to punch holes at the corner of each plate and then take the molten Ingot that is in the crucible out of the forge and pour a little bit into each awl-hole. Leave to cool for at least a half hour.
Description: By using the materials at a Tinker's Workshop, the Tinker can reinforce and strengthen a shield.
Effect: The Tinker can give a shield one Protect vs any Shatter Effect that targets the shield. The wielder of the shield should call “Protect” when the Counter is used. This Protect should be communicated to anyone who is given the shield, and the benefits are wasted if the shield is abandoned. 
Rebuild Item [Apprentice Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Ingot
Uses per Batch: Rebuilds one item
Expiration: Permanent 
Crafting Process:
Using the Ingot, perform any and all work on the item over the period of three turns of the hourglass. This may include hammering out dents in the metal, welding together broken pieces of weaponry, or sewing additional patches onto broken leather armor.
Description: Magical effects, acid, dropping from tall cliffs, and other such extremes often damages equipment to the point where it is unusable. Tinkers are experts at patching up old equipment so that it can be used again.
Effect: If an item has been Shattered, this Rebuilds the item fully.
Ritual Wand [Apprentice Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials: 2 Ingots, 2 Hide, and 1 Rare Mineral
Uses per Batch: Creates one wand
Expiration: Permanent
Crafting Process:
The Ingots must be heated in the crucible until soft (one turn of the glass) and then removed with tongs. The metal should be shaped into a wide oval. Rare minerals and the innards (Materia) of one spell-packet (a spell-packet should not actually be disassembled) should be poured in a line into the center of the oval and the oval folded over. The shape should go back in the crucible for softening and then should be hammered and folded at least ten times, returning to the fire with the tongs if it gets too hard. Finally, the lump should be heated for one turn of the glass and then shaped, generally in a twisting, pulling motion, to create a wand shape. The Hide should be wet and wrapped around the wand. Other decorations may be added as applicable.
Description: This will create a ritual wand, an item with a small amount of expendable magic that is a basic requirement of many Rituals.
Effect: Acts as a basic requirement for many Rituals.
Sharpening Stone [Apprentice Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Ingot
Uses per Batch: Creates one sharpening stone
Expiration: End of Event
Crafting Process:
Put on leather gloves and use the large tongs to place the Ingot in the forge for two turns of the hourglass, so that the metal gets red hot. Pull out the Ingot and strike five times soundly with a hammer on one side of it, flattening the metal. Then flip it over, and strike it five times on that side. Quench in water. Use a whetstone to grind one edge for a turn of the hourglass. Use new edged metal to grind/shape the smooth stone for one turn of the hourglass.
Description: A stone made of a fragile material. When a weapon is run across the edge it is quickly worn away but not until the object is sharpened. 
Effect: A character can use a Sharpening Stone to increase their Spike damage by +1 on one Spike they use before the next Short Rest. A stone can be used on any weapon, not just edged - a fact that has confounded weapon crafters for as long as time itself. If used to sharpen projectiles or thrown weapons, up to three may be given a +1 to damage. Any usage of a Sharpening Stone takes a Quick 100. Once it has been used, it is used up and needs to be discarded. The Sharpening Stone should be represented with some sort of smooth stone. Players are encouraged to use their own physreps for this item. 
Journeyman Tinkering Schematics


Base Latch Trap [Journeyman Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Ingots, 1 Rare Mineral, 1 Forgesource
Uses per Batch: One Trap
Expiration: Permanent
Crafting Process: 
Heat the Ingot just enough to cut with a knife. Cut six pieces. Use the tools, knife, and hammer to form into a six-sided box just long enough to hold the Forgesource. Add a splash of water into the Rare Minerals. Pump the bellows 10 times to stoke the fire, then put Rare Minerals in the crucible to heat in the forge for 10 seconds. Take out and use tools to coat the inside pieces of the box with Rare Minerals. Put the Forgesource in and heat the pieces of metal enough to seal edges. Quench.
Description: By harnessing and storing small bits of energy in metal containers, the Tinker can create a trap to protect a lock from being picked.
Effect: This trap protects a lock (on a box or door for example) from being picked. See the Skills document (Basic Traps) for more information on this item. You must have the Advanced Traps skill to set traps. The default damage of all traps is 3 damage. This can be enhanced by adding an Area or Alchemical Trap Augment. 
Base Triggered Trap [Journeyman Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Ingot and either 1 Bloom or 1 Hide, 1 Forgesource
Uses per Batch: Creates one trap
Expiration: Permanent
Crafting Process:
If using Bloom, take the Bloom and strip it, weaving the fibers into a braid. If using Hide, cut the Hide into strips and braid together. Heat the Ingot in the crucible and use the bellows 30 times. The Ingot should be fully molten. Pour out onto the anvil and shape into a box. Quench. With the small tools, set the braid inside the box before sealing to build the mechanism. 
Description: A small explosive or mechanical trap that makes a noise and does a slight amount of damage when set off. It can be a large clamshell trap on the ground, a small tripline hooked to an alchemical explosive, or something similar.
Effect: These traps make a snap sound when they go off. They are represented by mousetraps, party poppers, and snaps. See the Skills document (Basic Traps) for more information on this item. A character must have the Advanced Traps skill to set traps. It can be enhanced by turning it into an Area or Alchemical Trap Augment. 
Black Arrow [Journeyman Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 2 Rare Mineral, 1 Ingot, 1 Hide, 1 Harvest
Uses per Batch: 1 Arrow
Expiration: Permanent
Crafting Process:
Wet the Hide and stretch it over the anvil. Leave to dry for at least three turns of the hourglass. Put the Ingot in the crucible and melt in the forge for 15 pumps of the bellows. Pour it out in a line on the anvil. Sprinkle the Rare Minerals into one end. This end will be the tip. Shape into an arrow with the hammer. Wrap the stretched Hide around it. Sharpen with the whetstone for 1 turn of the hourglass to a very sharp point. 
Description: The Tinker builds one special arrow, called a Black Arrow.
Effect: The user of the Black Arrow can fire one shot that, with the expenditure of a Spike, the arrow does base damage and can be Called with Piercing Damage and the Final modifier. The arrow itself must be marked with a white streamer, and, if it misses, it is not expended, although that particular arrow must be retrieved first. Once it hits, however, the Black Arrow is expended and cannot be used again even if retrieved.
Catspaw Boots [Journeyman Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 5 Hide, 1 Harvest, 1 Rare Minerals, 1 Essence Infusion - Energy
Uses per Batch: 1 Pair of Catspaw Boots
Expiration: Permanent
Crafting Process:
As you can either use the hide to make a new pair of boots, or you can use it to augment a current pair of boots, you will need to either cut out a boot pattern out of the five Hide and stitch it together (or rivet) using the small tools, or cut out strips of Hide and attach it to a current pair of boots. Make sure that the construction or augmentation is sturdy! 
Once that is complete, take the Harvest and chop it up into fine pieces. Place the chopped harvest into the crucible and heat in the forge for four turns of the hourglass. While that heats, mix the Essence - Energy and the Rare Mineral into a gluelike substance. Stir it thoroughly for at least two turns of the hourglass, let stand to thicken.
Remove the heated Harvest pieces from the forge, and use the tongs to press them against the soles of the boots. Use the glue that you created before to coat the bottoms of the boots, holding the Harvest in place. Allow to cure for at least ten turns of the hourglass before wearing.
Description: This recipe produces or augments a pair of boots to allow the wearer to survive falls that would normally be lethal.
Effect: The wearer of the Catspaw Boots, may, once per Event, negate the consequences of falling damage or Effects by calling “Counter, Catspaw Boots.”
Enhanced Cookpot [Journeyman Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 2 Ingots, 2 Rare Minerals, 2 Harvest, 1 Essence - Life
Uses per Batch: 1 Cookpot
Expiration: Permanent
Crafting Process:
Heating the Ingots in the forge, the crafter should pump the bellows 10 times until the metal is red hot. Pulling them out with tongs, they should be slowly hammered into a bowl or pot shape adding the Rare Minerals while being hammered. At least three times the metal should be reheated in the forge for 5 pumps of the bellow in order to keep the metal hot enough to work. The Harvest should be diced up into small strips and worked into the hot metal in the final shaping of the pot. Lastly it should be quenched in water to cool.
Description: The Tinker creates a cookpot that allows alchemy to be stretched into multiple doses via being administered to a meal.
Effect: This allows anyone using the cookpot to put any alchemy into a meal, rather than having to ingest it as a liquid, applying a single dose of an Elixir to multiple pieces of food.  Each alchemy can be applied in this way to 6 pieces of food, and the food will lose the alchemy’s effect after around five to ten minutes - reverting back to normal food.  Each piece of food needs to be around 1”x1”x1” (a medium sized cookie would work). The following alchemy may be used in this way:
                                 * Alcohol
                                 * Daggerpoint Cognac
                                 * Healing Draught
                                 * Nyte Tea
                                 * Bloodseeker Elixir
                                 * Liquid Agility
                                 * Spellnaught Elixir
                                 * Trollblood Potion
Forgesource [Journeyman Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 2 Ingots
Uses per Batch: Creates one Forgesource
Expiration: Permanent 
Crafting Process:
        Note: No more than one Forgesource can be put in the forge by any one character between Long Rests.
The Tinker melts the Ingots in the Crucible, then pours it out drip by drip into the quenching bucket, making small nuggets of metal. Ten of these are collected and pounded flat with the hammer. With the small tools, inscribe each with a different number of straight lines from one to ten. Fill the grooves with materia, generally from one spell-packet. Place all the lined discs together with the lines always perpendicular to the lines on the disc behind. Now leave in the forge for eight hours. 
Description: This is a small cylinder which absorbs the heat and energy from the forge. It will be slightly warm to the touch at all times and will feel electric, making hairs stand up.
Effect: A forgesource is a reservoir of magical energy that can be used as a component for a number of other Tinkering Schematics.
Full Patch Kit [Journeyman Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: Any combination of 5 Ingot or Hide
Uses per Batch: Creates one kit
Expiration: Until Used
Crafting Process:
This Schematic can make as many kits simultaneously as desired, using 5 Crafting Materials per kit. Using any Ingots that you might have, heat them in the forge for one turn of the hourglass. Hammer them out flat into plates with 5 blows each and using the hammer and awl, punch holes into the corner plates and one hole in the middle of two other sides, so that they can quickly be riveted back onto a suit of armor in the field. If any Hide is used, use the small tools to cut three rectangular leather patches per Hide, then punch holes into the four corners of each piece and a hole into the middle of each long side of the rectangle. Layer the patches. Rivet the four corners.
Description: This is a large kit of materials preassembled to quickly patch up a suit of armor. It includes items such as leather strapping, large metal plates, boiled leather scraps, bolts, nuts, and rivets. Can quickly be used to patch up a suit of armor until it can be fully Mended at a Tinker's Workshop.
Effect: This kit allows someone to patch up a suit of armor with a Quick 100. In order to patch the armor, the player must roleplay as though they are actually fixing the armor. Normally, a Full Patch Kit will Mend 4 points of Armor, but a character with the Tinker skill can Mend a suit of armor to its maximum value; either use will expend the Full Patch Kit, which must then be discarded. In-game, it should be represented by a 2-4” x 4-7” piece of leather or leatherish material, or metal or metal-looking material. Players are encouraged to use their own physreps for this item. 
High Quality Foraging Sickle [Journeyman Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 1 Hide, 1 Ingots, 1 Rare Mineral
Uses per Batch: Creates one High Quality Foraging Sickle
Expiration: Permanent
Requires Attunement
Crafting Process:
Place the Ingot into the crucible and then into the forge. Pump the bellows until the Ingot is red hot, about ten pumps of the bellows. Remove and pour out onto the Anvil. Using the Hammer, flatten the molten metal by striking it five times. Sprinkle the Rare Minerals on top of the molten metal and place back into the crucible. Place back into the forge and pump the bellows again ten times. Pour out onto the Anvil again and use the hammer to shape the metal into a sickle. Quench it in the bucket of water. Slice the hide into strips and wrap it around the handle. Sharpen the sickle on the whetstone for at least three turns of the hourglass to make sure it is very sharp.
Description: This allows for the creation of a better Gathering Implement, focused on Foraging.
Effect: While wielding the High Quality Foraging Sickle, the gatherer may collect one additional Bloom per Event.
High Quality Mining Pick [Journeyman Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 2 Hide, 2 Ingots
Uses per Batch: Creates one High Quality Mining Pick
Expiration: Permanent
Requires Attunement
Crafting Process:
Place the two Ingots into the crucible and then into the forge. Pump the bellows for thirty pumps to melt the Ingots. Pour out onto the Anvil and using the Hammer, shape half the metal into the handle, a round shape about 1' long. Shape the rest of the metal into the top pick. Place both objects back into the Forge to allow it to heat up slowly for one turn of the hourglass. Remove and use the Hammer and tools to weld the two pieces together. Quickly quench both pieces. Cut the Hide into thin strips. Use one of the pieces of Hide to help reinforce the weld that you did early, lashing the pick head and the handle together. Use the second Hide as a wrap to act as a handle of the pick. Spend one turn of the hourglass sharpening both ends of the pick.
Description: This allows for the creation of a better Gathering Implement, focused on Mining.
Effect: While wielding the High Quality Mining Pick, the gatherer may Counter a Broken Pick or Shatter Mining Pick event from the Mining Deck, once per Event. The appropriate call is “Counter, High Quality Mining Pick.”
High Quality Skinning Knife [Journeyman Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 2 Hide, 5 Ingot, 1 Harvest
Uses per Batch: Creates one High Quality Skinning Knife
Expiration: Permanent
Requires Attunement, Requires Scavenging II
Crafting Process:
For this skinning knife, it is important to start by taking the Harvest and trimming it into a knife-like blade. Use another sharp knife to whittle it down, spending about five turns of the hourglass shaping it. At the same time you are doing this, place the Ingot into the Crucible and pump the bellows twenty times. Once the Harvest has been trimmed into a knife-like shape, take the molten metal from the Forge and pour it onto the blade, coating it fully with metal. Make sure that the metal encompasses the entire time, shaping it using the hammer if needed. This should take at least three turns of the hourglass to fully coat it. Once that is done, quench the knife and use the hide to act as a grip for the blade. Last, sharpen it on the whetstone for at least one turn of the hourglass.
Description: This allows for the creation of a better Gathering Implement, focused on Skinning.
Effect: While attuned to the High Quality Skinning Knife, once per long rest when turning in a Scavenge token at Player Logistics, the gatherer may designate “High Quality Skinning Knife” on their logistics form. The Material Logistics Helper will then draw 3 cards and use the highest yield result when processing the Scavenging.
Refine Hyperium [Journeyman Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: Unrefined Hyperium, 2 Hide, 4 Ingots
Uses per Batch: One Refined Hyperium
Expiration: Permanent
Crafting Process:
Soak both Hides in the bucket of water, and then carefully wrap the Unrefined Hyperium with the damp hides binding the brick tightly together. In the forge, melt the Unrefined Hyperium until fully liquid: the Hides will be consumed in this process, however during the heating the Unrefined Hyperium will have a tendency to spark, which will be reduced by the dampened hides.
This process is daunting, and will require constant pressure from bellows to keep the heat high. It is advised that an assistant help with the bellow: should the heat be reduced at any point in the refining process, the Unrefined Hyperium will be destroyed. After two turns of an hourglass of bellowed heat, place the ingots into the slag mixture. This will usually result in a massive sputtering of molten material and will bring the Tinkerer in harm’s way. 

At this point, the Tinkerer should take 5 by Flame. This will only interrupt the process if it reduces the tinker to 0 Life Points.
Continue to apply bellow pressure for two additional turns of the hourglass, reaching in with the tongs to mix the molten metal at the end of each turn. Remove the crucible from the forge and let it cool. Tamp out with hammer after solid. 
Description: Refined Hyperium is an iridescent, shimmering alloy created by introducing Unrefined Hyperium in small amounts to base metals. It is difficult to mass produce and therefore often very expensive. Famously, Hyperium is able to hold the magical stimulus of enchanting better than most other metals.
Effect: Refined Hyperium is used in the creation of Enchanted Hyperium, a rare resource. 
Reinforce Bow [Journeyman Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 4 Ingots, 2 Hide
Uses per Batch: Reinforces one bow
Expiration: End of Event
Crafting Process:
First, wet the Hide and use the tools and tongs to stretch both pieces of Hide over the anvil. Keep stretching and working the Hides for one turn of the hourglass, then cut into strips the width of the bow. While cutting, heat two of the Ingots in the forge. Take out before they become molten and use the hammer to flatten the Ingots into long strips the width of the bow. Flatten the other two Ingots with the hammer while cold, 25 blows each. Sandwich the Hide between the hot metal strips and the cold ones and punch holes through all layers with the awl and hammer. Then lay on the bow and hammer/rivet into place.
Description: The Tinker uses strong materials to protect a bow from almost all blows for a long period of time.
Effect: The Tinker can reinforce a bow so that it can withstand the pounding punishment of blades. As long as the bow is deemed safe by a Safety Marshal, it can be used to block with and will not be shattered as normal. Once the bow has taken one blow, the effects of this schematic will end at the completion of the next Long Rest. So, an archer could block with a bow for one attack and then for the rest of that day, but after a Long Rest, would need to have the bow reinforced again. If a bow is already reinforced with the Minor Bow Reinforcement, a Journeyman can enhance it to this level of reinforcement with only 2 Ingots and 1 Hide. The bow’s user should be instructed to Call “No Effect, Reinforced Bow” when the bow is struck. If the same player hits it multiple times, the call does not need to be made for each blow, just often enough for other players to understand that the bow is reinforced.
Shield Strengthening [Journeyman Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 7 Ingots
Uses per Batch: Strengthens one shield
Expiration: Until used
Crafting Process:
Place one Ingot in the crucible. Put the rest of the Ingots in the forge for two turns of the hourglass. Remove the four that are not in the crucible with tongs and leave the one to sit for one turn of the hourglass. Quench the removed Ingots immediately. Hammer both into separate sheets then layer the sheets and hammer them 25 times while the third melts, trying to make a single thin sheet. Put the sheet briefly back in the forge for three bellows-pumps before taking it out and cutting into plates with the knife. Quench each piece in water and lay on the shield. Use the awl to punch holes at the corner of each plate and then take the molten Ingot in the crucible out of the forge and pour a little bit into each awl-hole. Leave to cool for at least a half hour.
Description: Creates a number of multi-layer laminated armor sheets for a shield.
Effect: The Tinker can grant the shield a Protect vs one Shatter effect that targets it. The Shield also gains one Protect vs Wounding or Piercing Effects, stopping them from getting through the shield if they hit it. The wielder of the shield should call “Protect” when either Counter is used. These Protects should be communicated to anyone who is given the shield, and the benefits are wasted if the shield is abandoned. 
Shield of Deflecting [Journeyman Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 10 Ingots, 5 Hide, 8 Rare Minerals, 2 Harvest, Essence Infusion - Weave
Uses per Batch: Builds or augments one Shield of Deflecting
Expiration: Permanent
Requires Attunement, Shield Expertise
Crafting Process:
Prepare the Harvest by setting them in the Forge to char, for at least two turns of the hourglass, but not more than four turns (otherwise they will be destroyed). Take out the charred Harvest. Pour out four of the Rare Minerals and roll the charred Harvest over it, coating it fully, using tongs or gloves to protect the skin.
Place back into the Forge directly on the coals for one turn of the hourglass. Remove and use a blunt object to break up the Harvest into small pieces. Place within a Crucible and add the ten Ingots. Heat within the Forge but make sure to get it very hot, pumping the bellows at least twenty times. Pour out the molten metal onto the anvil and use the hammer to shape it into a shield shape (or coat another shield with the molten metal). Make sure you do a detailed job at shaping it, hammering at least forty times.
Once it is in the shape of a shield, place back into the forge directly for one turn of the hourglass. While it is heating up in the forge, slowly pour the Essence over the back of the shield. Remove from the heat and sprinkle the last four Rare Minerals onto the face of the shield. Quench. Use the five Hide to make straps and attach them with rivets and the small tools.
Description: Creates a Shield of Deflecting, allowing a character to negate a melee attack that strikes the shield itself.
Effect: The character can Counter one weapon attack that hits their shield. They should call “Counter, Shield Expertise” when they use the Counter. This will refresh after the character completes a Short Rest. This effect is cumulative with the skill Shield Expertise.
Greater Tinkering Schematics


Air Blade [Greater Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 3 Hide, 9 Ingots, 8 Rare Minerals, 2 Harvest
Uses per Batch: One Blade
Expiration: Permanent
Crafting Process:
Melt Ingots in the crucible by pumping the bellows 25 times. Pour them out onto the anvil and begin to fold, trying to trap bubbles with each fold. Do this for one turn of the hourglass. Then pour out Rare Minerals all around the edges of the metal. Use the bellows to blow the Rare Mineral dust at and over the cooling metal. With the tongs, hold the metal in the forge briefly before taking it out and shaping with the hammer and small tools into a shortsword. Work the Harvest into the hilt and guard. Wrap the handle with Hide. Then use a whetstone to sharpen the blade.
Description: This masterwork blade is light and flexible, without being brittle or dull.
Effect: This Shortsword counts as a Hand Weapon for the purposes of Powers that require a Hand Weapon. The character still needs to have proficiency with a Shortsword.
Alchemical Trap Augment [Greater Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 2 Ingots, 1 Harvest, 3 Bloom, 1 dose of alchemical substance listed below
Uses per Batch: Improves one Trap
Expiration: Until used
Crafting Process:
Break up the Bloom into small pieces. Use the knife to shave the Harvest into thin shavings and mix in with the Bloom. Take the 2 Ingots and place them in the crucible. Heat the Ingots for 4 turns of the hourglass or until they are almost molten. Pour out onto the surface of the Anvil and hammer into the metal half of the bloom and harvest mixture. Fold the metal twice over while hammering. Place back in the forge for 2 turns of the hourglass to heat. Remove, and use the tongs, leather gloves, and small tools to shape into a sealed box with a hole at the top. Stuff the box with the second half of the Bloom and Harvest mixture. Deftly pour the dose of substance into one of the openings, being careful not to smell or touch the substance. Use the small tools to seal the box and attach to the trap. 
Description: Tinkers are able to deftly create containers to replace the effect on traps with a variety of alchemical substances.
Effect: Changes the Effect of the trap to the Effect that the added substance has when it is ingested. Add the Alchemy card to the card attached to the trap and describe the Effect on it. Below is the list of substances that can be added to a trap this way. See the Skills document (Basic Traps) for more information. Traps that have been augmented with the Area Trap Augment may not be enhanced with Alchemical Trap Augments  (Alchemical must come before Area).
                                 * Adderstrike Venom - Piercing 4 by Poison
                                 * Enervation Poison - Drain by Poison
                                 * Holy Oil - 4 Piercing to Undead by Radiant
                                 * Noctilotoxin - Taint and Silence both by Poison
                                 * Pillowspore Poison - Sleep by Poison
Area Trap Augment [Greater Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 3 Ingot, 1 Hide, 2 Harvest, 3 more doses of an alchemical substance if it already contains an Alchemical Trap Augment.
Uses per Batch: Improves one Trap
Expiration: Until used
Crafting Process:
The Tinker should take the Ingots and heat it in the forge while pumping the bellows 30 times. After the Ingot is red hot, it should be removed with the tongs and shaped into a flat rectangle using the hammer, tongs and small tools. The two Harvest should be shaved with the knife into small pieces and hammered into the metal of the Ingot for 10 strikes of the hammer. The metal should be heated once again for 3 turns of the hourglass. Once red hot, use the hammer and anvil to shape the metal into a cylinder. Quench the metal and then after cutting the Hide into strips, use the Hide to attach the cylinder to the trap. If it has been enhanced with an alchemical substance, two more vials of the substance may be carefully inserted into the metal cylinder.
Description: By using mechanical effects, the tinker can rig a trap to affect an area rather than just the individual that triggers it.
Effect: This augment changes the delivery of the trap from affecting the individual that triggered it, to everyone within a room. The delivery will begin with the words "In This Room..." Everybody in the affected area will take the effect. If used outside the trap will only affect the person triggering the trap. 
Armorer’s Kit [Greater Tinkering Schematic]
Crafting Materials Needed: 6 Ingots, 5 Hide, 2 Harvest, 1 Rare Minerals
Uses per Batch: 1 Kit
Expiration: Permanent
Crafting Process:
The Ingots must be heated up in the crucible in sets of three. Each one must be given 5 pumps of the bellows and then left in the forge for one turn of the hourglass. The molten metal is then taken out and poured onto the anvil in small lines, which are then worked with the small tools to make the armorer’s tools. One of the Harvest and the Rare Minerals must be worked into two of the shaped tools; each of these must be fashioned for one turn of the hourglass. Finally, four of the Hide are sliced with the knife into very thin strips which are used as handle wrappings on some tools, as well as sewing leather cord which will go in the kit.
